Items.Title: 停止移开焦点 | Stop Removing Focus
Items.Description:
您知道网页上交互项目周围的边框吗？ 链接、表单组件（复选框、按钮、文本字段）、标签、分页。 在您使用和测试的浏览器中不同的边框。设计师讨厌的“丑陋”轮廓？

我已经看到很多焦点指标被从网站上剥离或删除，而没有预先考虑到它最近对可访问性的影响。 我们需要停止这样做，因为它是有害的，我会告诉你原因。

为什么不好

是否是:focus{ outline: none; } 时期。

停止转移焦点的原因

照片由Joshua Hoehne在Unsplash上拍摄

  1. 当使用键盘作为导航方式时，有视觉问题的用户将看不到他们在网页上的位置。
  2. 有视力的用户（比如我自己）也将使用键盘来浏览交互式项目。
  3. 许多盲人和视障人士使用键盘交互来按顺序使用他们的屏幕阅读器。
  4. 当一个元素获得焦点时，这意味着该元素已准备好进行交互。 您正在删除与该元素进行视觉交互的能力。
  5. 视觉指标可以帮助患有执行功能障碍和其他视觉或处理相关问题（光敏感、偏头痛等）的人，因为它一次将注意力吸引到一件事上，这有助于减少认知负荷。

你正在创造一个障碍，而不是消除一个。

在许多 CSS 重置文件中包含了outline:0 ，例如Eric Meyer 的 CSS Reset 。 它将outline:0应用于所有页面元素。 不要忘记重新添加焦点样式并使其以高对比度（至少 10:1）可见。

浏览器默认值

如果用户由于年龄、视力退化、青光眼、散光、其他黄斑/视觉退化而无法看到轮廓，则在大多数情况下默认轮廓不够好。

再次，让它们可见！

在我之后重复...

不要删除焦点指示器！

标题图片由Stefan Cosma在Unsplash上拍摄



原文:

You know that border around interactive items on a Web page? Links, form components (checkboxes, buttons, text fields), tags, pagination. That border that differs in the browsers you use and test in. The "ugly" outline that designers hate?

I've been seeing a lot of focus indicators being stripped or removed from sites with no forethought on the impact it will have on accessibility lately. We need to stop doing this because it is harmful and I'll tell you why.

Why It's Bad

Whether it's :focus{ outline: none; } or the one that really makes an accessibility person's blood pressure rise, * { outline: 0; } the continued omittance of focus styles and indicators is bad for accessibility. Period.

Reasons to Stop Removing Focus

Photo by Joshua Hoehne on Unsplash

  1. Users with visual issues will not see where they are on a Web page when using the keyboard as a means of navigating.
  2. Sighted users (like myself) will also use the keyboard to tab through interactive items also.
  3. Many blind and visually impaired people use keyboard interactions in order with their screen readers.
  4. When an element receives focus it means that element is ready for interaction. You're removing the ability to interact visually with that element.
  5. Visual indicators help people with executive dysfunction and other visual or processing related issues (light sensitivity, migraines, etc.) because it draws attention to one thing at a time which helps helps reduce cognitive load.

You're creating a barrier rather than removing one.

CSS Resets

The inclusion of outline:0 in many CSS reset files, such as Eric Meyer’s CSS Reset. It applies outline:0 to all page elements. Eric clearly indicates that focus styles should be re-implemented;

/* remember to define focus styles! */ :focus { outline: 0;

}


Don't forget to add the focus style back in and make it visible with a high contrast (at least 10:1).

Browser Defaults

Default outlines in most cases are not good enough if the user cannot see the outline due to age, degenerating eyesight, glaucoma, astigmatism, other macular/visual degeneration.

Again, make them visible!

Repeat After Me...

Don't remove focus indicators!

Header image photo by Stefan Cosma on Unsplash
Items.Author: colabottles

Items.Title: 本周 React #103：Contentlayer、Remotion 3、Stale Closure、跨平台、Jest 28、Hydration、Netlify Edge Functions... | This Week In React #103: Contentlayer, Remotion 3, Stale Closure, Cross-platform, Jest 28, Hydration, Netlify Edge Functions...
Items.Description:
大家好！

本周大发布：Contentlayer、Remotion 3、Jest 28、Netlify Edge Functions...

大量的 React 文章，包括 2 篇与跨平台相关的文章，这是我非常感兴趣的一个主题。

如果您喜欢本时事通讯，请优先订阅：

  • 反应

    您定义一个架构，它将验证/编译您的内容并有效地生成一个带有 TypesScript 类型的.contentlayer文件夹，并且准备好将内容直接导入您的应用程序。 这似乎显着提高了构建性能（即使使用冷缓存也可以达到 x2）。 测试版有一个官方的Next.js 集成，减少了胶水代码的数量。 我建议观看介绍视频（5 分钟）以了解它在 Next.js 上下文中的工作原理。 经过 10 个月的开发，v3.0 刚刚发布（ 预告片），主要的新功能是对AWS Lambda上的无服务器渲染的新支持，它允许您更快地扩展、降低成本并制作视频！

    有助于全面了解 React 中的陈旧闭包问题的交互式文章。 如果你使用 memoization 并且不遵守 ESLint exhaustive-deps -deps 规则，你就有可能在你的代码库中引入这种类型的问题，而且调试并不总是那么容易！

    关于通过门户在多个窗口中渲染 React 应用程序的有趣反馈。 有优点（控制所有窗口的单一状态）但也有一些挑战需要解决。

    附加功能：

    反应原生

    还唤起了如何将 Web 应用程序移植到 React-Native。

    他建议使用原始组件，并出于 DX 原因采用 Web 优先的方法：在浏览器中开发并使用 Cypress 进行测试，然后验证移动应用程序是否正常工作。

    附加功能：

    其他

    这个版本中有很多很酷的东西！ 我的亮点：

    • 分片支持以并行化测试执行
    • 完全支持包"exports"
    • 读取 JavaScript 文件、创建依赖关系图、捆绑运行时、最终捆绑组装……这是一种技术阅读，极大地揭开了简单捆绑器的内部工作原理。

      在尝试失败（边缘处理程序）后，Netlify 发布了一个新的无服务器@Edge 产品，其边缘功能处于测试阶段，基于Deno Deploy基础设施，因此与 Web 标准 API 更兼容。 这尤其允许使用服务器端代码更优化地运行 React 元框架：Remix、Next.js（目前只有中间件）、Hydrogen、服务器组件、Astro...

      附加功能：



原文:

Hi everyone!

Great releases this week: Contentlayer, Remotion 3, Jest 28, Netlify Edge Functions...

A good diversity of React articles, including 2 related to cross-platform, a subject that interests me very much.

🙏 Support the newsletter:

If you like this newsletter, subscribe in priority there:

  • 🇬🇧 ThisWeekInReact.com
  • 🇫🇷 ReactHebdo.fr

React

Contentlayer: Content Made Easy for Developers (beta)

Contentlayer is a new system that offers to manage the glue between your content (Markdown, CMS, Notion...) and your code. You define a schema, and it will validate/compile your content and efficiently generate a .contentlayer folder with TypesScript types, and the content ready to be imported directly into your app. This seems to improve build performance significantly (up to x2 even even with cold caches). The beta has an official Next.js integration reduces the amount of glue code. I recommend watching the intro intro video (5min) to understand how it works in a Next.js context. A new project by Johannes Schickling, also creator of Prisma, also working on the glue between code and DB.

Remotion 3.0

Remotion allows you to create videos programmatically, with React (web) code and data/props. After 10 months of development, v3.0 has just been released (trailer) and the main new feature is the new support for serverless rendering on AWS Lambda, which allows you to scale, reduce costs, and produce videos much faster!

Hooks, Dependencies and Stale Closures

Interactive article to help fully understand the stale closure problem in React. If you use memoization and do not respect the ESLint exhaustive-deps rule, you risk introducing this type of problem in your codebase, and it is not always easy to debug!

The challenges of rendering an OpenLayers map in a popup through React

An interesting feedback on rendering a React app in several windows via portals. There are advantages (a single state to control all the windows) but also some challenges to solve.

Extras:

React-Native

Migrating React And Native Apps To React Native

Callstack engineers explain how to adopt React-Native for an existing app, with 2 different approaches: greenfield (full rewrite) and brownfield (incremental migration). Also evokes how to port a web application to React-Native.

Writing cross-platform components for web and React Native

Artem gives some ideas for cross-platform web/mobile development. He suggests using primitive components, and taking a web-first approach for DX reasons: developing in the browser and testing with Cypress, then verifying that the mobile app works.

Extras:

Other

Jest 28: Shedding weight and improving compatibility

Lots of cool things in this release! My highlights:

  • Sharding support to parallelize test execution
  • Full support for package "exports"
  • GitHub Actions Reporter looks very convenient
  • ESM support still blocked
  • jest-runner-tsd: for testing TypeScript types
  • jest-light-runner: 2x faster on Babel codebase

Building a JavaScript Bundler

Christoph Nakazawa explains how to create a JavaScript bundler, based on some existing Jest packages. Reading JavaScript files, creating the dependency graph, the bundle runtime, final bundle assembly... A technical reading that greatly demystifies the internal workings of a simple bundler.

Netlify Edge Functions (beta)

After a failed attempt (Edge Handlers), Netlify releases a new serverless @ Edge offer with its Edge Functions in beta, based on the Deno Deploy infrastructure, and thus more compatible with web standard APIs. This allows in particular to run React meta-frameworks using server-side code more optimally: Remix, Next.js (only middleware for now), Hydrogen, Server Components, Astro...

Hydration is Pure Overhead

The creator of Qwik/Angular continues to question the hydration model of our server-side rendered SPAs, and pushes to adopt another more efficient model, based on "resumability".

Extras:
Items.Author: sebastienlorber

Items.Title: Javascript中的纯函数 | Pure Function in Javascript
Items.Description:
• 干净的代码 - barchaga tushinarli toza kod yozish；
  • 测试qilish juda oson；
  • 纯函数 - 琼脂 bir xil argumentlar beeriladigan bo'lsa doim bir xil natija qaytaradigan funksiya。 顺宁 uchun biz calculateGST funksiyasini pure function deb nomlashimiz mumkin。

    琼脂 keyinchalik o'zgaruvchining qiymati o'zgarsa biz funksiya parametriga eski productPrice ' ni bersak ham funksiya boshqacha qiymat qataradi。

    数学.随机（）；

  • Joriy vaqtni olish(getting current time). Joriy vaqtni olish（获取当前时间）。


原文:
  • clean code - barchaga tushinarli toza kod yozish;
  • Test qilish juda oson;
  • Koddagi xatoni tuzatishga oson;
  • Alohida ajratilgan va Mustaqil funksiya;

Pure Function - Agar bir xil argumentlar beeriladigan bo'lsa doim bir xil natija qaytaradigan funksiya. Bu Funksiyalar hech qanday holatga yoki dasturning bajarilishi davomida ma'mulotlarning o'zgarishiga bog'liq emas, ular o'z argumentlariga bog'liq.

Bundan tashqari, pure function network request yoki ma'lumotlar mutatsiyasi kabi kuzatilishi mumkin bo'lgan nojo'ya ta'sirlarni keltirib chiqarmaydi.

Quyidagi Javscript funksiyani ko'rib chiqaylik:

function calculateGST( productPrice ) { return productPrice * 0.05;
}

Agar biz productPrice uchun bir hil qiymat bersak, Yuqoridagi funksiya doim bir hil javob qaytaradi. Ya'ni funksiya qaytaradigan qiymatga boshqa tashqi qiymatlar yoki holatlar ta'sir o'tkaza olmaydi. Shuning uchun biz calculateGST funksiyasini pure function deb nomlashimiz mumkin.

Endi, quyidagi funksiyani ko'rib chiqamiz:

var tax = 20;
function calculateGST( productPrice ) { return productPrice * (tax / 100) + productPrice;
}

Ushbu funksiya pure function yoki yo'qligini taxmin qila olasizmi?

Toppa to'gri, bu pure function emas, sababi funksiya qaytaradigan qiymat o'zidan tashqarida turgan tax deb nomlanuvchi o'zgaruvchiga bog'liq. Agar keyinchalik o'zgaruvchining qiymati o'zgarsa biz funksiya parametriga eski productPrice' ni bersak ham funksiya boshqacha qiymat qataradi.

Ammo bir narsani yodda tutishimiz kerak.

Eslatma: Agar pure funksiya pure funksiyani chaqiradigan bo'lsa bu hech qanday yomon ta'sir olib kelmaydi va chaqirayotgan funksiya pure function hisoblanadi(Masalan: funksiyani ichida Math.max()'dan foydalanish).

Quyida funksiyalar pure function bo'lishi uchun keltirib chiqarmasligi kerak bo'lgan side effect'larni ko'rib chiqamiz:

  • HTTP so'rov yaratish;
  • Mutatsion ma'lumotlar(mutating data;
  • Ekran yoki konsolga chop etish;
  • DOM so'rov/manipulatsiyasi;
  • Math.random();
  • Joriy vaqtni olish(getting current time).
Items.Author: dawroun

Items.Title: Grepper，在编码时获得帮助的更好方法？ | Grepper, a better way to get help while coding?
Items.Description:
所以，我使用 Grepper 已经有一段时间了。对于那些你不熟悉的人，Grepper 是

轻松从网络上获取代码示例，然后无需思考即可访问您的代码示例。

基本上，它是我们遇到的问题的答案和解决方案的扩展。您可以搜索问题，如果您安装了 grepper，如果有对该问题的答案，您将首先弹出答案，否则您可以通过其他人解决它来源并将其添加到 grepper 以便将来如果有人遇到类似问题，他们可以找到您的
格勒珀

我每天都使用它，它非常有用，社区也很有帮助，不像 SO 那样以毒性着称与阅读分散在不同地方的文档和解决方案相比，它还节省了一些时间。
根据简短的摘要，你们如何看待它？



原文:

So, I've been using Grepper for some time now.For those you aren't familiar with it Grepper is

The Query & Answer System for the Coder Community
Grepper is the ultimate RAM upgrade for the software developers brain. Easily snag code examples from around the web, then access your code examples without having to think.

Basically it is an extension for answers and solutions to problems we run into.You can search the problem and if you have grepper installed if there is an answer to that prob you will get the answer pop up first else you can solve it by some other sources and add it to grepper so in future if someone runs into a similar problem they can find your
answer.Grepper can be integrated with IDEs as well.

Grepper

I use it everyday and it's quite useful and the community is helpful as well unlike S.O. which is not known for toxicity and it also saves a bit of time compared to reading docs and solutions scattered through different places.
What do you guys think about it based on the short summary?

在家里做周年庆装饰看起来有点复杂，需要做很多工作，但通过一定的技巧和努力，很容易就能得到惊喜。 这也是让您的挚爱惊喜的最佳方式之一，在家中为周年纪念提供迷人的装饰品。

如果您正在为周年纪念寻找一些浪漫的房间装饰创意来打动您亲爱的另一半，那么您已经为您准备好了。 滚动浏览一些很棒的房间周年纪念装饰创意，适合特殊的日子，既浪漫又漂亮。 因此，请查看并从我们庞大的周年纪念装饰创意列表中进行选择。



原文:

Anniversary is one of those important days of the year when both the couples celebrate their journey together and love. Items.Title: Jozef Behr 分享了学习 Python 编程的初学者技巧 | Jozef Behr shared Beginner Tips for Learning Python Programming
Items.Description:
提示＃1：每天编码

当您学习一门新语言时，一致性非常重要。 我们建议每天致力于编码。 难以置信，但肌肉记忆在编程中起着重要作用。 每天致力于编码将真正有助于发展这种肌肉记忆。 虽然一开始可能会让人望而生畏，但可以考虑从每天 25 分钟的小事开始，然后从那里开始。

提示＃2：与正在学习的其他人在一起

虽然编码可能看起来像是一项单独的活动，但当你一起工作时，它实际上效果最好。 当你学习用 Python 编写代码时，与其他正在学习的人一起学习是非常重要的。 这使您可以分享您在此过程中学到的技巧和窍门。

提示＃3：提出问题

人们总是说没有坏问题这回事，但是当谈到编程时，你可以问一个坏问题。 当您向与您要解决的问题几乎没有联系的人寻求帮助时，最好问好问题

提示＃4：构建一些东西，无论如何

对于初学者来说，有很多小练习可以真正帮助你对 Python 充满信心，以及培养我们上面谈到的肌肉记忆。 一旦您掌握了数据的基本结构（字符串、列表、字典、集合）、面向对象的编程和写作课程，就该开始构建了！



原文:

Tip # 1: Code every day

Consistency is very important when you are learning a new language. We recommend committing to coding every day. It can be hard to believe, but muscle memory plays a big part in programming. Committing to coding daily will really help in developing this muscle memory. Although it may seem daunting at first, consider starting small with 25 minutes each day and working your way up from there.

Tip # 2: Surround yourself with others who are learning

While coding may seem like a solitary activity, it actually works best when you work together. It is very important when you are learning to code in Python that you also surround yourself with other people who are learning. This allows you to share the tips and tricks you learn along the way.

Tip # 3: Ask questions

People always say that there is no such thing as a bad question, but when it comes to programming, you can ask a bad question. When you are asking for help from someone who has little or no connection to the problem you are trying to solve, it is best to ask GOOD questions

Tip # 4: Build something, whatever

For starters, there are many little exercises that will really help you become confident with Python, as well as developing the muscle memory we talked about above. Once you've mastered the basic structure of data (strings, lists, dictionaries, sets), object-oriented programming and writing lessons, it's time to start building!
Items.Author: behrjozef

Items.Title: 启动 Hackpack：通过社区克服倦怠 | Launching Hackpack: Conquering burnout through Community
Items.Description:
当我学习编程面试时，我感到孤独和沮丧。 我是我认识的唯一一个专注于在 Big Tech 找到工作的人。 没有人理解我为什么不参加社交活动或在工作前 3 小时起床学习。 更糟糕的是，在学习了将近一年之后，我的谷歌面试失败了。 沮丧开始了，我遵循了所有传统的建议（大量的 leetcode 和 CTCI），我做错了什么？ 更糟糕的是，我经常精疲力竭。 我的周期为 3 个月：2 个月准备 + 1 个月精疲力竭。 每次我经历 1 个月的倦怠时，我都会想放弃。

我终于用我的算法评分标准、每日清单系统、模拟面试和大量练习题进入了一个状态。 我花了很多的自我激励和一致性才能到达那里，但我最终收到了 Facebook 和谷歌的报价。

快进到 YC 支持的公司 Hackpack。 创建 Hackpack 的目的是为研究大型技术面试的工程师提供一群可以求助的人。 我们知道独自完成这个过程是多么困难，以及让周围的人像你一样认真对待这个过程是多么重要。 我们会在您的积极性下降时提供支持，并在您通过 Facebook 技术面试时庆祝。

加入 Hackpack 会得到什么？

我们每天都会发送关于我们正在做什么的签到，并有较小的同步来讨论我们的每周目标。 我们还与大型科技公司成员进行模拟面试、解决问题的会议和活动。 这是您在通往成功的道路上结识未来同事的地方。

当您加入 Hackpack 时，您将在社区的支持下对自己的学习负责，从而大大减少那些倦怠期的脱轨。

这使您可以更一致地学习，并减少您花在学习上的总时间。

你如何加入 Hackpack？

每个 Hackpack 成员都会经历一个面试过程。 欢迎大家申请。 我们与认真、热情的工程师合作，他们希望认真对待他们的学习。

目前，我们的 Discord 中有超过 2,000 名工程师。 我们希望接触尽可能多的工程师，并为他们提供帮助克服倦怠的社区。

如果您有兴趣加入社区，请加入 discord或通过我们的网站申请。

你不必一个人去！



原文:

When I was studying for programming interviews, I felt lonely and depressed. I was the only person I knew that was focused on landing a job at Big Tech. No one understood why I was skipping out on social events or getting up 3 hours before work to study. To make things worse, after studying for almost a year, I failed my Google interview. Frustration set in, I was following all of the conventional advice(lots of leetcode & CTCI), what was I doing wrong? To make matters worse, I’d frequently burnout. I was on a 3 month cycle: 2 months preparing + 1 month burnt out. Every-time I was going through the 1 month of burnout I would be tempted to give up.

I finally got into a groove using my Algorithm Grading Rubric, daily checklist system, mock interviews, and a lot of practice problems. It took a lot of self motivation and consistency to get there, but I eventually received offers from Facebook & Google.

Fast forward to Hackpack, a YC backed company. Hackpack was created to provide engineers studying for big tech interviews with a community of people to turn to. We know how hard it is to go through this process alone and how important it is to have people surrounding you who are just as serious about this as you are. We provide support as your motivation dips and celebrate when you ace your Facebook technical interview.

What do you get when you join Hackpack?

Hackpack members hold each other accountable and support one another. We send daily check-ins on what we are working on and have smaller syncs to discuss our weekly goals. We also do mock interviews, problem solving sessions, and events with members of big tech. Everyone in Hackpack is working towards the same goal. This is your place to meet your future fellow colleagues on your way to success.

When you join Hackpack, you are holding yourself accountable to your studying and with the support of the community, making those periods of burnout significantly less derailing.

This allows you to study more consistently, and reduce the overall time you spend studying.

How do you join Hackpack?

Each Hackpack member goes through an interview process. All are welcome to apply. We work with earnest, passionate engineers who are looking to take their studying seriously.

Future of Hackpack

Currently we have over 2,000 engineers in our discord. We want to reach as many engineers as possible and give them the community to help conquer burnout.

If you are interested in joining the community, join the discord or apply through our website.

You don’t have to go it alone!
Items.Author: dannyhabibs

Items.Title: 元界在各行业的使用案例 | Use Cases of Metaverse in Various Industries
Items.Description:
哈里·威廉

发表于对于很多人来说，元宇宙有很多含义。 然而，在最基本的层面上，它是现实世界和互联网相遇的虚拟空间。 在这些虚拟环境中，元界提供了身临其境的体验。 这些世界可以由用户（如在 Minecraft 中）或企业或组织创建。

换句话说，它是一个元界平台，不连接任何人的应用程序或位置，允许用户共享动态、逼真和协作的虚拟 3D 环境。

今天，在这篇博客中，我们将讨论元界及其用例。 但是，在此之前，这里有一个快速介绍，

此外，它与现实世界有着错综复杂的联系。 您可以穿过走廊，而不是盯着屏幕上的像素化​​复制品； 您将在虚拟服装店试穿新衣服，而不是阅读商店的测量指南。 还将有全新的艺术画廊以独特的方式充分利用 Metaverse 的扩展功能，以及为您的虚拟化身而非有形服装出售最新时尚的服装店。

最近，Nike 刚刚申请了商标，以便在 Metaverse 中创建和销售虚拟鞋和服装！ 他们正在谨慎地准备征服虚拟时尚世界。

该公司提出了其元界愿景，同时承诺向全球研究和项目合作伙伴投资 5000 万美元，以确保虚拟世界“以合乎道德的方式生产”。

当它的全部潜力得到充分发挥时，元界可能会成为我们遇到 Web3 的综合虚拟世界，就像我们在笔记本电脑和智能手机显示器上使用 Web 2.0 时所做的那样。

元界的用例是什么？

  1. 解锁营销前景
  2. 区块链用例
  3. 虚拟旅游
  4. 网络实时通讯
  5. 虚拟办公室和学习空间
  6. 元界有什么好处？
  • 医疗保健创新
  • 元界和激动人心的新游戏
  • 全新经济

去中心化平台开发

  • 元界市场开发
  • 不可替代的开发和集成
  • 元空间营销
  • 元界应用开发

    • 阅读更多： https ://www.whatech.com/social-media/news/716958-use-cases-of-metaverse-in-various-industries



    原文:

    Harry William

    Posted on Apr 27

    To several people, the Metaverse has many meanings. However, at its most basic level, it’s a virtual space where the real world and the internet meet. In these virtual environments, the Metaverse provides an immersive experience. These worlds could be created by users (as in Minecraft) or by businesses or organizations.

    In other words, it's a Metaverse platform that isn't connected to anyone's app or location and allows users to share virtual 3D environments that are dynamic, realistic, and collaborative.

    Today, in this blog, we will discuss Metaverse along with its use cases. But, before that, here's a quick introduction to,

    The Metaverse is intended to be fully immersive, with virtual reality headsets and motion controllers directing your avatar through the virtual world. In addition, it is intricately linked to the real world. You can walk through the corridors rather than staring at a pixelated reproduction on a screen; you'll try on a new outfit in a virtual clothing store rather than reading the store's measuring guide. There will also be whole new art galleries that take full advantage of the Metaverse's enlarged capabilities in unique ways, as well as clothing stores that sell the newest fashions for your virtual avatar rather than tangible apparel.

    Recently, Nike just applied for Trademarks in order to create and sell virtual shoes and clothing in the Metaverse! They're discreetly preparing to conquer the world of virtual fashion.

    Facebook is developing its Metaverse projects with vitality.

    The business laid forth its Metaverse vision while pledging a $50 million investment in worldwide research and program partners to ensure that the virtual world is "produced ethically."

    **In virtual reality, social interactions would be far more interesting than Messenger, and Facebook Marketplace might become the Metaverse's big bazaar. When its full potential is fulfilled, the Metaverse may become the comprehensive virtual world where we encounter Web3, much like we did with Web 2.0 on laptop and smartphone displays.

    What Are the Use Cases of Metaverse?

    1. Unlocking Marketing Prospects
    2. Blockchain Use Cases
    3. Virtual Tourism
    4. Web Real-time Communication
    5. Virtual Office and Learning Spaces
    6. What Are the Benefits of Metaverse?
    • Innovation in Healthcare
    • Metaverse and Exciting New Games
    • Completely New Economy

    What Metaverse development opportunities does Quytech offer?

    • Decentralized platform development
    • Metaverse marketplace development
    • Non-fungible development and integration
    • Metaspace marketing
    • 3-D space development
    • Metaverse app development

Items.Title: 结对编程：两个头比一个更好吗？ | Pair Programming: are two heads better than one?
Items.Description:
结对编程是一种开发实践，其中两个程序员在一个工作区中一起工作。 它允许开发人员通过知识共享和讨论相互学习并提高代码质量。

我们何时以及为什么要使用它？

对于软件的困难或最重要的部分
在更复杂的任务中，您更有可能犯错误或遗漏某些东西。 第二个人可以帮助提高代码的质量，从不同的角度来看，实时审查你的代码，或者讨论一个问题。

招聘新员工时
结对编程可以成为招聘过程中的一个很好的工具。 它使您可以轻松验证潜在员工的技术和软技能。 但是，值得记住的是，这种方法可能会在候选人中产生更多的压力，这可能会对结果产生不利影响。

为项目介绍新人
适应新项目总是需要一点时间。 如果一个已经在其中的人向您展示代码并向您解释它，它确实可以提高您习惯项目中使用的方法并增加您对它的理解的速度。

培训技术和软技能时
这不仅是不太高级的程序员向高级程序员学习的好方法，而且高级程序员也可以通过讨论相互学习。

成对编程的变化

根据相关人员的经验，可以选择三种结对工作：

  • 专家-新手：作为一种教学方法很有用，因为专家可以轻松地分享他们的知识
  • 专家-专家：创建软件非常重要的部分的好选择

主要技术

有很多类型的配对，其中一些是：

  • 一名开发人员（“司机”）编写代码，而另一名（“导航员”）审查每一行代码，提出问题并提前思考
  • 后座导航器：当我们在解决问题时碰壁时，有时我们看不到替代方法。 然后当有人从不同的角度看待问题时，这很有用
  • 导游：为项目实施新程序员的好方法。 已经在项目中的开发人员编码并解释应用程序的工作原理，而新开发人员观察并提出问题
  • 非结构化：这是没有任何模式的完全自由泳。 如果开发人员相互信任，这是有效的

结对编程的优点

以下是您应该考虑使用此方法的一些原因：

  • 通常会立即发现错误
  • 代码更短，质量更高
  • 问题得到更快解决
  • 开发者互相学习
  • 程序员训练他们的软技能
  • 整个团队更加融合
  • 应用程序的各个部分不依赖于一个开发人员，这使得缺席成为问题
  • 更容易了解其他开发人员

结对工作没有缺点吗？

像其他一切一样，这种工作也有其缺点：

  • 讨论时编码较慢
  • 它很快就会变得很累
  • 开发者之间可能会发生冲突
  • 内向的程序员可能很难
  • 编码人员可能会因为一直被监视而感到压力
  • 导航员可能不愿意向驾驶员提供反馈
  • 在静态结对编程中，问题可能是由椅子高度、屏幕亮度、鼠标、键盘或 IDE 的差异引起的
  • 需要一些时间和练习来适应它

远程结对编程

如今，我们更频繁地远程工作，但这并不是结对工作的障碍，除非程序员异步工作。

它不仅可以让您看到其他程序员正在编写什么代码，而且我们还可以与他们一起编写代码、交谈并共享终端和端口。 它使工作更舒适，更容易上手。

应该遵守哪些规则？

要使结对编程起作用，必须遵循以下规则：

  • 互换角色：不要一直占着键盘，让你的同事证明自己
  • 友善一点：在轻松的氛围中一起工作更容易
  • 使用熟悉的环境：当你们俩都舒服时，沟通会更容易
  • 提问：不要害怕提问
  • 休息：每小时或需要时休息 5 分钟
  • 互相信任：不要提前批评，要有耐心
  • 控制你的自我：你的伙伴不会批评你，而是你的代码，所以听他们的，如果你不同意，可以讨论
  • 说对不起：有时我们会被带走，或者我们在不知不觉中伤害了某人，请注意
  • 不要怀疑：如果你有消极的态度，你会失败
  • 将您的差异视为好事：不要将他人思想上的差异视为劣势，而应视为优势
  • 注意时间：长时间的训练会失去效力，会让你筋疲力尽。 会话应持续 2 至 2.5 小时
  • 改变你的伴侣：了解新人的技能是值得的，这样你就可以向他们学习
  • 记住——你是一个团队：你所生产的一切都是共同努力的结果

那么我应该从结对编程开始我的冒险吗？

这要看情况。 每种情况都是不同的，方法应该适应问题，而不是相反。

如果您认为这可能适合您的情况，您应该考虑尝试一下！

佩德罗·菲格雷多 - Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

Pair programming is a development practice in which two programmers work together in one workspace. It allows developers to learn from each other and improve code quality through knowledge sharing and discussion.

When and why should we use it?

For the difficult or most important parts of the software
You are more likely to make mistakes or to miss something in the more complex tasks. The second person could help to improve the quality of the code, by looking from a different perspective, reviewing your code live, or discussing a problem.

When recruiting new employees
Pair programming can be a great tool in the recruitment process. It allows you to easily verify both the technical and soft skills of the potential employee. However, it is worth remembering that this method may generate more stress in the candidate, which may adversely affect the result.

For the introduction of a new person to the project
It always takes a little time to adapt to a new project. If a person that is already in it, shows you the code and explains it to you, it can really increase the speed at which you will get accustomed to methodologies used in the project and increase your understanding of it.

When training technical and soft skills
Not only is this a good method for less advanced programmers to learn from more advanced programmers, but also more advanced programmers can learn from each other through discussion.

Variations of programming in pairs

There are three options for working in pairs based on the experience of the people involved:

  • Newbie-newbie: Novice programmers can effectively resolve the hardest problems as a pair
  • Expert–newbie: Useful as a teaching method, because an expert can share their knowledge easily
  • Expert–expert: A good choice for creating very important parts of the software

The main techniques

There are quite a few types of pairings, some of which are:

  • Driver-navigator: The most popular technique. One developer (“driver”) writes code while the other (“navigator”) reviews each line of code, asks questions and thinks ahead
  • Backseat navigator: When we hit the wall while solving a problem, sometimes we don't see alternative ways. Then when someone looks at the problem from a different perspective, this canl be useful
  • Tour Guide: A good way to implement a new programmer to the project. A developer who was already in the project codes and explains how the application works whilst a new developer observes and asks questions
  • Ping-Pong: Perfect when we use Test-Driven Development (TDD) methodology, one programmer writes the tests while the other the code to be tested
  • Unstructured: This is total freestyle without any patterns. It is effective if the developers trust each other

Advantages of pair programming

Here are some reasons why you should consider using this methodology:

  • Mistakes are usually detected immediately
  • Code is shorter and more qualitative
  • Problems are resolved faster
  • Developers learn from each other
  • Programmers train their soft skills
  • The whole team is more integrated
  • The individual parts of the application don’t depend on one developer, which makes absence problematic
  • It's easier to get to know the other developers

Is working in pairs free from disadvantages?

Like everything else, this kind of work also has its downsides:

  • Coding is slower while discussing
  • It can quickly becomes tiring
  • Conflicts between developers may occur
  • It can be difficult for introverted programmers
  • The coder may feel stressed by being watched all the time
  • The navigator may be reluctant to give feedback to the driver
  • In stationary pair programming, problems may be caused by the difference in chair height, screen brightness, mouse, keyboard, or IDE
  • It takes some time and practice to get used to it

Remote pair programming

These days we work remotely more often, but this is not an obstacle to working in pairs unless programmers work asynchronously.

Visual Studio Code has an awesome tool called “Live Share” which is Made by Microsoft. It allows you not only to see what another programmer is coding, but also we can code with them, talk, and share the terminals and ports. It makes work more comfortable and easier to get started.

What rules should be followed?

For pair programming to work, the following rules must be followed:

  • Swap roles: Don't occupy the keyboard all the time, let your co-worker prove themselves
  • Be nice: It's easier to work together in a relaxed atmosphere
  • Use a familiar environment: Communication will be easier when both of you are comfortable
  • Ask questions: Don't be afraid to ask questions
  • Take a break: Take a 5-minute break every hour or when you need to
  • Trust each other: Don't criticize in advance, be patient
  • Control your ego: Your partner doesn’t criticize you, but your code, so listen to them and if you disagree, have a discussion
  • Say I'm sorry: Sometimes we get carried away, or we unknowingly hurt someone, be mindful
  • Don't be skeptical: If you have a negative approach, you will fail
  • Think about your differences as something good: Don't take the difference in another person's thinking as a disadvantage, but as an advantage
  • Watch the time: Long sessions lose their effectiveness and can tire you out. Sessions should last between 2 to 2.5 hours
  • Change your partner: It is worth getting to know the skill sets of new people so you can learn from them
  • Remember - you are a team: Everything you produce is a joint effort

So should I start my adventure with pair programming?

Well, it depends. Each case is different and the methods should be adapted to the problem, never the other way around.

If you think this might work for your situation, you should consider trying it out!

Pedro Figueiredo - Apr 25

Items.Title: 在 ubuntu 中将数据库还原到 SQL Server | Restore database to SQL Server in ubuntu
Items.Description:
发表于成功安装 SQL Server 后，有时需要恢复数据库。 下面我们将看看如何使用终端来做到这一点。 为此，我们将使用微软公开提供的 AdventureWorks2019 示例数据库。

您可以使用此链接AdventureWorks2019下载它

先决条件：

  • 在 Ubuntu 中成功安装 SQL Server
  • 程序：

    第一步：打开终端使用快捷键 Ctrl + alt + t 打开终端

    步骤 2：确保数据库文件在当前目录中



    步骤 2：连接到 SQL Server
    将“YourPassword”替换为您在安装过程中设置的密码

    sqlcmd -S localhost -U SA -P '<YourPassword>'

    第 3 步：恢复数据库
    将/home/skn/Downloads/替换为您拥有数据库文件的路径。
    注意：您可以在终端中使用 pwd 命令来获取您当前的工作目录。

     USE [master] RESTORE DATABASE [AdventureWorks2019] FROM DISK = '/home/skn/Downloads/AdventureWorks2019.bak' WITH MOVE 'AdventureWorks2017' TO '/var/opt/mssql/data/AdventureWorks2019.mdf', MOVE 'AdventureWorks2017_log' TO '/var/opt/mssql/data/AdventureWorks2019_log.ldf', FILE = 1, NOUNLOAD, STATS = 5 GO

    恭喜，还原成功

    凯文·希尔德霍恩—— Apr 26 4月26日



原文:

Seffu Kioi Nyambura

Posted on Apr 27

After successful installation of SQL Server, sometimes there is need to restore a database. Below we will look at how to do that using terminal. For this, we will use AdventureWorks2019 sample database which is a publicly provided by microsoft.

You can download it using this link AdventureWorks2019

Prerequisites:

  • Successfully installed SQL Server in Ubuntu
  • File of database to be restored in .bak format

Procedure:

Step 1: Open terminal Use the shortcut Ctrl + alt + t to open the terminal

Step 2: Ensure the database file is in the present directory



Step 2: Connect to SQL Server
Replace 'YourPassword' with the password you set during installation

sqlcmd -S localhost -U SA -P '<YourPassword>'

Step 3: Restore the Database
Replace /home/skn/Downloads/ with the path to where you have your database file.
NB:You can use the pwd command in the terminal to get your present working directory.

USE [master]
RESTORE DATABASE [AdventureWorks2019]
FROM DISK = '/home/skn/Downloads/AdventureWorks2019.bak'
WITH MOVE 'AdventureWorks2017' TO '/var/opt/mssql/data/AdventureWorks2019.mdf',
MOVE 'AdventureWorks2017_log' TO '/var/opt/mssql/data/AdventureWorks2019_log.ldf',
FILE = 1,  NOUNLOAD,  STATS = 5
GO

Congratulations, restoration successful

Kevin Schildhorn - Apr 26

Items.Title: 在 Astro 中使用 Netlify Edge 功能保护资产 | Protecting assets with Netlify Edge Functions in Astro
Items.Description:
我最近为 Astro 1.0 Hackathon 开展了一个项目，其中包含一些身份验证和付费部分。 自然，我希望付费部分中的内容仅对……付费客户可用。 例如，有大量图像，还有降价文件和其他我想保护的静态资产。

由于 Astro 目前并没有真正的中间件系统，因此要弄清楚如何保护静态资产，或者如何……拦截请求是一件很棘手的事情。

但！ 然后出现了Netlify Edge Functions 。 例如检查身份验证状态！

边缘函数和正则函数的区别

可能是更接近您的用户的区域。 它们还具有更快的启动时间，因为它们使用 Deno 而不是 Node.js。 这也意味着您必须记住，在编写 Edge Functions 时，您不能使用任何依赖于 Node.js 内置的依赖项，这可能会或可能不会真的很不方便。

此外，Netlify Edge Functions 有一个非常好的转发或next()请求的概念，这实际上会将处理程序推迟到排队的下一个处理程序；

 export default async function ( req , context ) { return await context . next (); }

没错，您可以让多个 Edge Functions 处理响应。 从Netlify 文档中，对/admin的请求将首先调用auth函数，然后是injector函数：

 [[edge_functions]] path = "/admin" function = "auth" [[edge_functions]] path = "/admin" function = "injector" [[edge_functions]] path = "/blog/*" function = "auth"

保护静态资产

现在我们知道如何使用 Edge Functions 充当各种中间件，我们可以开始动手了。 首先，我创建了我的项目：

 npm init astro@latest -- --template minimal

这将搭建一个准系统的 Astro 入门项目。 接下来，安装@astrojs/netlify适配器：

 npm i -S @astrojs/netlify

并将适配器添加到您的配置中：

 import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config'; + import netlify from '@astrojs/netlify'; // https://astro.build/config export default defineConfig({ + adapter: netlify() });

我们还将在由 Astro 搭建的public文件夹中创建一个protected/文件夹，并向其中添加一个图像，例如： my-app/public/protected/my-image.png 。

伟大的！ 我们现在可以创建我们的边缘函数。 在项目的根目录中，创建一个edge-functions/文件夹，其中包含一个protected.js ：

在这个文件中，我们使用URLPattern来查看请求是否匹配受保护的资产。 如果是，我们想看看用户是否通过了身份验证。 出于演示目的，我们将只检查auth查询参数是否存在（例如： https://my-app.netlify.app/protected/foo.png?auth=true ://my-app.netlify.app/protected/foo.png?auth=true ）。 如果存在auth查询参数，我们调用await ctx.next() ，它将请求推迟到行中的下一个处理程序，在这种情况下，这意味着图像将仅提供给浏览器。 如果auth查询参数不存在，用户将收到403 Forbidden响应，并且不会提供静态资产。

在实际应用程序中，您可能希望通过验证 JWT 或您使用的任何其他身份验证机制来检查用户的身份验证状态。

接下来，我们必须在项目的根目录中创建一个netlify.toml文件，并声明我们的 Edge Function：

 [[edge_functions]] path = "/protected/*" function = "protected"

这意味着对于每个匹配/protected/*的请求，它将执行我们的protected.js边缘函数。

部署

太好了，我们现在应该一切就绪，并对我们的静态资产进行一些保护。

最后，我们要做的就是将以下deploy脚本添加到我们的package.json中：

 "scripts": { "dev": "astro dev", "start": "astro dev", "build": "astro build", + "deploy": "astro build && cp -R ./edge-functions ./netlify && netlify deploy --prod --build --dir=netlify", "preview": "astro preview" },

我们在项目的根目录中有edge-functions/文件夹的原因是运行astro build会擦除netlify/文件夹的内容，所以如果我们在netlify/文件夹中有边缘函数的源代码，每当我们运行astro build时，它都会被覆盖。 为了解决这个问题，我们只需将edge-functions/文件夹保留在根目录中，在构建时，我们使用一个简单的命令将它们复制到netlify/文件夹： cp -R ./edge-functions ./netlify 。

请注意，如果您使用 Netlify CLI 进行部署，您现在还必须使用--build标志来正确部署您的 Edge 功能，例如： netlify deploy --prod --build --dir=netlify 。

当我们最终运行npm run deploy时，我们可以看到我们的应用程序完全按照预期运行：

  • 您可以在此处找到演示链接，并在此处找到源代码链接。



原文:

I recently worked on a project for the Astro 1.0 Hackathon, that has some authentication and a paid section. Naturally, I'd like the content in the paid section to only be available to... paying customers. For example, there are loads of images, but also markdown files, and other static assets that I'd like to protect.

Since Astro currently doesn't really have a middleware system, it's been kind of tricky to figure out how to protect static assets, or how to kind of... intercept a request.

TL;DR: Source code here, live demo here

Netlify Edge Functions to the rescue

But! Then along came Netlify Edge Functions. Netlify Edge Functions can be used in a similar way to middleware — we can essentially put an Edge Function in front of the Netlify Function that serves our Astro application, and do something with the request; like for example check authentication status!

The difference between Edge Functions and Regular Functions

Edge Functions are run on the edge, which I've taken to mean they're available in more regions than regular functions, or; potentially a region that's closer to your user. They also have a faster start up time, because they use Deno instead of Node.js. This also means that you have to keep in mind that you can't use any dependencies that depend on Node.js built-ins when writing Edge Functions, which may or may not be really inconvenient.

Additionally, Netlify Edge Functions have a really nice concept of forwarding or next()ing a request, which will essentially defer the handler to the next handler in line;

export default async function(req, context) { return await context.next();
}

That's right, you can have multiple Edge Functions handling a response. From the Netlify Documentation, a request for /admin would invoke the auth function first, and then the injector function:

[[edge_functions]] path = "/admin" function = "auth" [[edge_functions]] path = "/admin" function = "injector" [[edge_functions]] path = "/blog/*" function = "auth"

Protecting static assets

Now that we know how to use Edge Functions to act as a middleware of sorts, we can start getting our hands dirty. First of all, I've created my project with:

npm init astro@latest -- --template minimal

This will scaffold a barebones Astro starter project. Next up, install the @astrojs/netlify adapter:

npm i -S @astrojs/netlify

And add the adapter to your configuration:

import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
+ import netlify from '@astrojs/netlify';

// https://astro.build/config
export default defineConfig({
+ adapter: netlify()
});

We'll also create a protected/ folder inside the public folder that has been scaffolded by Astro, and add an image to it, for example: my-app/public/protected/my-image.png.

Great! We can now create our Edge Function. In the root of your project, create a edge-functions/ folder, that contains a protected.js:

my-project/edge-functions/protected.js:

export default async (req, ctx) => { const url = new URL(req.url); const protectedRoutes = new URLPattern({pathname: '/protected/:img'}); const match = protectedRoutes.exec(url); const auth = url.searchParams.has('auth'); if(match && !auth) return new Response(null, {status: 403}); return await ctx.next();
};

In this file we use an URLPattern to see whether the request matches a protected asset or not. If it does, we want to see if a user is authenticated. For demoing purposes, we'll just check if the auth query parameter is present (for example: https://my-app.netlify.app/protected/foo.png?auth=true). If the auth query parameter is present, we call await ctx.next(), which will defer the request to the next handler in line, which in this case means the image will just be served to the browser. If the auth query parameter is absent, the user will get a 403 Forbidden response, and the static asset will not be served.

In a real application, you'll probably want to check the authentication status of the user by verifying the JWT, or whatever other authentication mechanism you use.

Next up, we have to create a netlify.toml file in the root of our project, and declare our Edge Function:

[[edge_functions]]
path = "/protected/*"
function = "protected"

This means that for every request that matches /protected/*, it will execute our protected.js Edge Function.

Deploying

Excellent, we should now be all set, and have some protection of our static assets in place.

To wrap things up, all we have to do is add the following deploy script to our package.json:

 "scripts": { "dev": "astro dev", "start": "astro dev", "build": "astro build",
+ "deploy": "astro build && cp -R ./edge-functions ./netlify && netlify deploy --prod --build --dir=netlify",
    "preview": "astro preview"
  },

The reason we have our edge-functions/ folder in the root of our project is that running astro build will wipe the contents of the netlify/ folder, so if we'd have the source code of our edge function in the netlify/ folder, it would get overwritten whenever we run astro build. To work around this, we just keep the edge-functions/ folder in our root, and at build-time, we copy them to the netlify/ folder with a simple command: cp -R ./edge-functions ./netlify.

Do note that if you're deploying with the Netlify CLI, you'll now have to use the --build flag as well to correctly deploy your Edge Function, e.g.: netlify deploy --prod --build --dir=netlify.

And when we finally run npm run deploy, we can see our application works exactly as expected:

  • https://astro-protected-assets.netlify.app/protected/foo.png -> 403 Forbidden, image is not shown
  • https://astro-protected-assets.netlify.app/protected/foo.png?auth=true -> 200 OK, image is shown

You can find a link to the demo here, and a link to the source code here.

Items.Title: 代码审查的 5 条黄金法则 | The 5 Golden Rules of Code Reviews
Items.Description:
代码审查在开发过程中起着如此关键的作用。 除了节省宝贵的时间和金钱外，它们还为开发团队提供了以人为本的价值。 它们也是教学和指导新员工的绝佳工具，也是大公司发誓的整体良好实践。

但当然，由于时间紧迫、销售预期和组织压力，一些团队和公司正在放弃提高代码质量的机会。

无论您的公司属于代码审查狂热的哪个领域，这篇文章都将介绍您如何（并且应该）使用代码审查来帮助开发过程和组织文化的其他方面，最重要的是，为您的公司带来更多价值开发商

以下是代码审查的 5 条黄金法则：

代码审查有五个黄金法则。 如果您参与过培训，其中一些可能听起来很熟悉。 这是完全正确的——代码审查只是在特定领域提供反馈的一种特定格式。

永远记住——评论的另一端是人

代码审查的第一条黄金法则很简单：审查其他人的代码，就像您希望自己的代码被审查一样。

代码审查应该：

重要的是，如果您有建设性的反馈要给出，请直截了当。 在提出改进建议之前，请避免以对代码的积极反馈开始的“垃圾三明治”，即使它是真实的。
当我们这么多人远程工作或彼此相距数百或数千英里时，这可能很难做到。

为了确保您的沟通正确，请大声朗读您的代码审查并问自己，这是我想对我说的话吗？ 如果没有，请考虑改变语气或内容。

给出明确的建议或建议

永远不要告诉某人代码需要修复，而没有就修复什么或如何修复它提出建议或建议。

不知道为什么？ 想象一下有人来到你家说：“我不喜欢你的装饰。 修理它。”

这是令人难以置信的烦人。

在不提供更多解释的情况下写下“解决这个问题”绝不是一个好主意。 为什么需要修复？ 你有什么建议来解决它？ 有人怎么可能弄清楚？

如果您留下的代码审查只说“解决这个问题”或“做得更好”，我们将代表代码审查权力亲自到您家敲击您的指关节。

始终假设良好的意图。

代码可能不会按照您的编写方式编写。 让我们更清楚地说：代码很少由两个不同的人以相同的方式编写。 毕竟，代码是一门手艺，而不是流水线上的任务。

在审阅过程中激发好奇心和欣赏感——好奇地了解审阅者的想法，并对 Coder 所做或尝试做的事情表示感谢。

明确行动和重要程度。

如果您要提出可选建议，例如，在代码被批准用于生产之前不需要的“nit”，请清楚地说出来。

如果你想知道为什么这个人做出了特定的选择，但这并不影响代码是否应该投入生产，请说清楚。

如果您确信代码需要在准备好投入生产之前进行修复，请说清楚。

专业提示：写作时，我们经常认为我们的意图很明确。 毕竟，我们知道我们想说什么。 但请记住，我们的文章对读者而言可能并不总是像对我们一样清晰，并确保您最基本的指导是简单明了的。

不要忘记代码反馈——以及所有反馈——包括表扬。

不用说，进行代码审查的主要好处是使代码更好并解决问题。

但这只是其中的一半。 另一方面，代码审查提供了一个很好的机会来感谢你并欣赏你同事的工作。

如果有人编写了特别优雅或可维护的代码，或者对使用库做出了重大决定，请告诉他们！

在 Sema，我们认为给出积极、具体的反馈总是正确的时机。 但是，当然，在代码审查方面，我们也会说话。

在我们的白皮书中了解更多关于代码审查的细节及其对公司文化的影响： www.semasoftware.com/blog/code-reviews-101-the-basics

清洁代码工作室 - 穆罕默德·夸尼特 - Jul 19 '21 21 年 7 月 19 日



原文:

Code Reviews have such a key role in the development process. In addition to saving valuable time and money, they also give a human-centric value to development teams. They are also a great tool for teaching and mentoring new recruits and an overall good practice big companies swear by.

But of course because of tight deadlines, sales expectations, and organizational pressures, some teams and companies are passing up opportunities to increase code quality.

Regardless of where your company falls on the spectrum of code reviews fandom, this piece will cover how you can (and should) use code reviews to help the development process and other aspects of the organization’s culture, and most importantly, give more value to your developers

Here are the 5 Golden Rules of Code Reviews:

There are five Golden Rules of Code Reviews. Some of them might sound familiar if you've been involved in training. And that's exactly right – code reviews are just one specific format of giving feedback in a particular domain.

‍

Always remember - it’s a human being on the other end of the review

The first Golden Rule of Code Reviews is simple: Review other people’s code like you’d like your code to be reviewed.

Code reviews should:

- Be kind– even if there’s room for improvement, the message can be delivered with empathy
- Be clear– make it easy for the reviewer to understand what you are saying. Importantly, if you have constructive feedback to give, be direct about it. Avoid a “crap sandwich” that starts with positive feedback about the code, even if it’s genuine, before getting to your suggestion for improvement.
- Be specific – The more granular your feedback can be, the more helpful it is to the Author. That can be hard to do when so many of us work remotely or hundreds or thousands of miles away from each other.

To make sure you are communicating correctly, read your code review to yourself out loud and ask yourself, is this something I would want to be said to me? If not, think about changing the tone or content.

‍

Give clear suggestions or recommendations

Never tell someone that the code needs to be fixed without giving suggestions or recommendations on what to fix or how to fix it.

Not sure why? Imagine someone came to your home and said, “I don’t like your decor. Fix it.”

It is incredibly annoying.

It is never a good idea to write “Fix this” without giving more explanation. Why does it need to be fixed? What suggestions do you have to fix it? How might someone figure it out?

On behalf of the Code Review powers, we will personally come to your home to rap your knuckles if you ever leave a code review that only says “Fix this” or “Do better.”

‍

Always assume good intent.

Code may not be written how you would write it. Let’s say that more clearly: code is rarely written the same way by two different people. After all, code is a craft, not a task on an assembly line.

Tap into a sense of curiosity and appreciation while reviewing – curiosity to understand what the reviewer had in mind and gratitude for what the Coder did or tried to do.

‍

Clarify the action and the level of importance.

If you are making an optional suggestion, for example, a “nit” that isn't necessary before the code is approved for production, say so clearly.

If you wonder why the person made a particular choice, but it doesn’t affect whether the code should make it to production, say so clearly.

If you are confident that the code needs to be fixed before it is ready for production, say so clearly.

Pro tip: When writing, we frequently think that our intent is clear. After all, we know what we are trying to say. But remember that our writing may not always be as clear to the reader as it is to us, and make sure that your most fundamental guidance is plain and straightforward.

‍

Don't forget that code feedback – and all feedback – includes praise.

It goes without saying that the key benefit of doing code reviews is to make the code better and fix issues.

But that's only half of it. On the flip side, code reviews present an excellent opportunity to thank you and appreciate your colleagues' work.

If someone has written particularly elegant or maintainable code or has made a great decision about using a library, let them know!

At Sema, we say it is always the right time to give positive, specific feedback. But, of course, we walk the talk when it comes to code reviews too.

Find out more about the ins and outs of code reviews and their impact on company culture in our whitepaper: www.semasoftware.com/blog/code-reviews-101-the-basics

Items.Description:
这篇文章包含会议资源 Resilient and well-architeded apps with chaos engineering

分布式系统复杂且不可预测，可能导致不良结果。 传统的测试方法不适用于这种复杂性，并根据现有知识做出断言。 许多公司将混沌工程视为一种获得系统新知识的实践，但不知道从何处或如何开始实际应用它，因为它被认为很难开始。

架构良好的应用程序旨在确保安全、高性能和弹性。 您需要测试您的应用程序并验证它是否按设计运行，并且对故障具有弹性。

亚马逊建设者图书馆

具有混沌工程研讨会的弹性和架构良好的应用程序

观看 AWS 弹性和混沌工程日的录制会议，了解有关混沌工程和弹性的更多信息。
如果您有任何问题，请在Twitter或LinkedIn上联系。 去实验！



原文:

This post contains resources for the session Resilient and well-architected apps with chaos engineering

Distributed systems are complex and unpredictable, which can lead to undesirable outcomes. Traditional testing methods do not apply well for this complexity and makes assertions based on existing knowledge. Many companies see the need for chaos engineering as a practice to gain new knowledge about the system, but does not know where or how to begin practically applying it as it is viewed as difficult to get started with.

Well-architected applications are designed and built to be secure, high-performing and resilient. You need to test your application and validate that it operates as designed, and is resilient to failures.

AWS Well-Architected Framework

https://aws.amazon.com/architecture/well-architected/

AWS Fault Injection Simulator documentation

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/fis/latest/userguide/

Amazon Builders' Library

https://aws.amazon.com/builders-library/

Resilient and Well-Architected Apps with Chaos Engineering workshop

https://catalog.us-east-1.prod.workshops.aws/workshops/44e29d0c-6c38-4ef3-8ff3-6d95a51ce5ac/

AWS Well-Architected labs reliability

https://bit.ly/wa-chaos/

AWS Fault Injection Simulator Samples

https://github.com/aws-samples/aws-fault-injection-simulator-samples

AWS Resilience and Chaos Engineering Day

Watch the recorded session from AWS Resilience and Chaos Engineering Day to learn more about chaos engineering and resilience.
https://pages.awscloud.com/EMEA-field-OE-resilience-chaos-engineering-day-202111-reg-event.html

If you have any questions, please reach out on Twitter or LinkedIn. Go experiment!

Items.Title: 在 Fedra 上安装 Winbox | Install Winbox on Fedra
Items.Description:
安装wine fedora 35

下载Winbox

赢盒

下载图标

创建快捷方式

创建winbox目录并移动winbox.exe

 cd /opt sudo mkdir winbox sudo mv ~/Downloads/winbox.exe /opt/winbox

将图标重命名为 winbox 并移动到 winbox 目录

sudo mv ~/Downloads/winbox.jpg /opt/winbox

创建 .desktop 文件

cd /usr/share/applications sudo nano winbox.desktop

将此代码添加到 winbox.desktop

 [Desktop Entry] Encoding=UTF-8 Name=Winbox Comment=winbox app Exec=wine /opt/winbox/winbox.exe Icon=/opt/winbox/winbox.jpg Terminal=false Type=Application Categories=GNOME;Application;Utility;

现在抽屉里有winbox

参考



原文:

Install wine fedora 35

Download Winbox

Winbox

Download icon

Icon

Create Shortcut

create winbox directory and move winbox.exe

cd /opt
sudo mkdir winbox
sudo mv ~/Downloads/winbox.exe /opt/winbox

Rename icon as winbox and move to winbox directory

sudo mv ~/Downloads/winbox.jpg /opt/winbox

Create .desktop file

cd /usr/share/applications
sudo nano winbox.desktop

Add this code to winbox.desktop

[Desktop Entry]
Encoding=UTF-8
Name=Winbox
Comment=winbox app
Exec=wine /opt/winbox/winbox.exe
Icon=/opt/winbox/winbox.jpg
Terminal=false
Type=Application
Categories=GNOME;Application;Utility;

Now winbox available on drawer

Ez to run
