Items.Title: 使用 JWT 黑名单/拒绝名单撤销访问权限 | Revoking Access with a JWT Blacklist/Deny List
Items.Description:
根据你听谁的意见，JWT 要么是解决所有身份验证问题的灵丹妙药，要么应该像瘟疫一样避免。 在 SuperTokens，我们有两种想法。

什么是 JWT 令牌？

它最常见的用例是身份验证（OAuth 2.0 + Open ID Connect）和会话管理。

顾名思义，JWT 可以在其中包含 JSON 格式的任何信息。 这也称为“JWT 声明”。 例如，对于会话管理，JSON 至少需要包含登录用户的userId ：

 { "userId" : "..." , "expiry" : 1646472008501 , ...
}

如果包含此信息的 JWT 需要过期，则 JSON 还可以包含令牌的过期时间（如上所示）。 这些字段的键名也有约定。 在我们上面的例子中，这些是：

  • 以登录过程为例，将包含上述信息的 JSON 发送给客户端，然后在每次请求时，客户端都可以将这个 JSON 发送回服务器。 这样，服务器就知道哪个用户 ID 正在查询其 API。 如果 JSON 已过期，则服务器可以拒绝请求，用户必须重新登录。

    但是，从安全的角度来看，客户端很容易更改 JSON 中的userId值并假冒另一个用户。 为了防止这种情况，服务器将原始 JSON 的“签名”与 JSON 一起发送。 这个“签名”是使用只有服务器知道的秘密创建的，因此客户端不能自己创建 JSON 的签名。 因此，如果客户端更改 JSON，服务器将无法将原始 JSON 的签名与传入/更改的 JSON 的签名匹配（这称为验证签名），然后可以拒绝请求。

    服务器可以使用多种算法为 JSON 生成签名：

    • 选择用于创建签名的签名方法必须以某种方式在 JWT 中进行编码，以便在验证签名时使用相同的方法。

      总而言之，一个 JWT 包含三个部分：

      • 包含有关使用的签名算法的信息的标头字符串。
      • 包含实际 JSON 的正文字符串。
      • 可用于验证 JWT 未被客户端更改的签名字符串。

      这三个部分用. 分隔符以形成完整的 JWT 令牌。 稍后可以在此博客文章中看到示例 JWT。

      与不透明令牌相比，JWT 方法当然有其优势。 自包含：JWT 可以包含用户的详细信息（不仅仅是会话 ID，如 cookie，还可以包含其他自定义数据，如用户名甚至权限），以及令牌的到期时间，因此您无需查询数据库对于该信息。 这完全不同于一个不透明的令牌，就其本质而言，它只是一堆毫无意义的字母数字字符。

    • 安全：JWT 使用密钥 (HMAC) 或公钥/私钥对（RSA 或 ECDSA）进行数字签名，以保护它们不被客户端或攻击者修改。
    • 仅存储在客户端：您在服务器上生成 JWT 并将它们发送到客户端。 然后客户端随每个请求提交 JWT。 这节省了数据库空间。
    • 高效：验证 JWT 很快，因为它不需要数据库查找。

    那就是：您如何撤销用户的访问权限？

    当然，JWT 有一个到期时间，这可以是你喜欢的短暂的。 但是，一旦访问令牌过期，JWT 就无效，客户端必须重新向您的服务器进行身份验证。 当然，这对用户体验有负面影响。

    但是假设用户有意退出您的系统？ 或者你想把他们踢出去，因为你害怕安全受到威胁？ 你不能[1]：如果他们仍然有未过期的令牌，他们仍然可以访问。

    这个问题有一个解决方案，但它确实需要您做一些额外的工作。 这是一种需要维护 JWT 黑名单/拒绝名单的技术。 在本文中，我们将向您展示 JWT 黑名单/拒绝名单是什么，如何实施，并讨论它是否是解决此问题的好方法。

    什么是 JWT 黑名单/拒绝名单？

    您在哪里维护此列表取决于您。 您可以使用传统数据库，但更好的方法是使用内存数据缓存，例如 Redis。 与存储在磁盘上的数据相比，内存中的数据缓存提供了更快且更可预测的寻道时间。

    这就是我们将在本示例中使用的内容，我们将使用 Node.js 和 Express 编写我们的解决方案。 如果这不是您选择的技术堆栈，请不要担心：无论您选择如何构建它，基本方法都是相同的。

    好的，那我该怎么做呢？

    首先，您需要实例化您的 Express 服务器应用程序并设置 Redis，以便您可以维护活动 JWT 列表：

     import express from " express " ; import bodyparser from " body-parser " ; import jwt from " jsonwebtoken " ; import redis from " redis " ; const JWT_SECRET = " Ultra-secure-secret " ; const app = express (); app . use ( bodyparser . urlencoded ({ extended : false })); app . use ( bodyparser . json ()); let redisClient = null ; ( async () => { redisClient = redis . createClient (); redisClient . on ( " error " , ( error ) => { console . log ( error ); }); redisClient . on ( " connect " , () => { console . log ( " Redis connected! " ); }); await redisClient . connect (); })(); // listen for requests const listener = app . listen ( 3000 , () => { console . log ( " Server running " ); });

    要测试 JWT 的存储和后续撤销，您需要一种方法来创建用户并为该用户生成 JWT。 让我们为此添加createUser端点。 您可以使用新用户的名称向此端点发出 POST 请求：

     import jwt from " jsonwebtoken " ; app . post ( " /createUser " , ( request , response ) => { const token = generateAccessToken ({ username : request . body . username }); response . json ( token ); }); const generateAccessToken = ( username ) => { return jwt . sign ( username , JWT_SECRET , { expiresIn : " 3600s " }); };

    点击该端点，您将看到为用户发出的 JWT：

     curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:3000/createUser' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "username": "Derek" }' "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1c2VybmFtZSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiaWF0IjoxNjQxMzA3MTgxLCJleHAiOjE2NDEzMTA3ODF9.3yrIJpWMS952QVakifjviiTs0ANJOmxZDovQO0N5brQ"

    如果将该令牌粘贴到在线调试器中，您会看到 JWT 中包含用户名编码，以及其他详细信息，例如它的发布时间 ( iat ) 和过期时间 ( exp )：

     { "username" : "Derek" , "iat" : 1641307181 , "exp" : 1641310781
}

    您需要做的下一件事是每当该用户尝试对您的应用程序执行任何操作时，使用该 JWT 对该用户进行身份验证。 这是一个三步过程。 首先，您需要确保已提供 JWT。 然后，您需要测试提供的 JWT 是否在存储在 Redis 中的黑名单/拒绝列表中。 如果不是，那么您最终需要验证 JWT 是否有效。

    我们可以将这些步骤编码为 Express 中间件：

     // JWT middleware const authenticateToken = async ( request , response , next ) => { const authHeader = request . headers [ " authorization " ]; const token = authHeader && authHeader . split ( " " )[ 1 ]; // token provided? if ( token == null ) { return response . status ( 401 ). send ({ message : " No token provided " , }); } // token in deny list? const inDenyList = await redisClient . get ( `bl_ ${ token } ` ); if ( inDenyList ) { return response . status ( 401 ). send ({ message : " JWT Rejected " , }); } // token valid? jwt . verify ( token , JWT_SECRET , ( error , user ) => { if ( error ) { return response . status ( 401 ). send ({ status : " error " , message : error . message , }); } request . userId = user . username ; request . tokenExp = user . exp ; request . token = token ; next (); }); };

    现在将会发生的是，每当您之前创建的用户尝试访问您的主路由 ( / ) 时，他们提供的 JWT 将首先根据黑名单/拒绝列表进行检查，然后在被授予访问权限之前进行验证。

    这一切都很好，但目前您无法撤销 JWT，因此黑名单/拒绝列表中没有任何内容。

    让我们创建另一个模拟用户注销的路由。 当这个端点被命中时，用户的 JWT 被持久化到 Redis，使用格式bl_<token>作为键，值是实际的令牌。 当令牌本身过期时，我们会将密钥设置为过期，以免 Redis 被大量过期令牌填满。

     app . post ( " /logout " , authenticateToken , async ( request , response ) => { const { userId , token , tokenExp } = request ; const token_key = `bl_ ${ token } ` ; await redisClient . set ( token_key , token ); redisClient . expireAt ( token_key , tokenExp ); return response . status ( 200 ). send ( " Token invalidated " ); });

    向/logout端点发出POST请求：

     curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:3000/' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1c2VybmFtZSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiaWF0IjoxNjQxMzA4NTQwLCJleHAiOjE2NDEzMTIxNDB9.nlJJe7XtK3PJVgvjevrHabeImqJyRoUDiXejDhVK5yM'

    现在用户的 JWT 在技术上仍然有效（直到过期），但现在它在黑名单/拒绝列表中，因此会在后续请求中被拦截。 对上面的主路由重复POST请求，您将收到 HTTP 403 Forbidden 错误，并显示消息"JWT Rejected" 。

    但是，如果您想拒绝访问尚未注销且其令牌仍然有效的用户，这将无济于事。 问题是您当时不知道用户的访问令牌是什么，因此您不知道将什么放入缓存中。

    要解决此问题，您应该添加通过 redis 将用户的userID列入黑名单的功能，并在 JWT 验证期间检查该黑名单。 与 JWT 黑名单不同，此条目不会有与之关联的到期时间。

    您可以在Github上找到此示例的工作版本。

    这是一个好主意吗？

    所以现在你看到了 JWT 黑名单/拒绝名单的概念相对容易实现。 但这是处理这种情况的最佳方式吗？

    在我们看来，事实并非如此。

    如果您维护一个黑名单/拒绝列表，并且必须在每次 API 调用时查询它，那么您就失去了这种优势。

    相反，我们提倡的是一种会话管理解决方案，它结合了 JWT 和不透明令牌各自的优势。

    在此工作流程中，当用户登录时，服务器会发出一个短期 JWT（访问令牌）和一个长期不透明令牌（刷新令牌）。 当用户被授予访问权限时，这两个令牌都会发送到客户端。

    当用户尝试访问资源时，他们会随每个请求一起发送 JWT 访问令牌。 当 JWT 过期时，客户端然后使用不透明刷新令牌来请求新的 JWT 和新的不透明刷新令牌。 此过程称为刷新令牌轮换。

    然后，客户端使用这个新的 JWT 发出后续请求，该过程继续进行。

    发行两个令牌和实现刷新令牌轮换的优缺点

    这种方法的好处是，如果您想撤销访问权限，那么您需要做的就是使服务器端的不透明令牌无效。 然后，当调用刷新端点时，服务器查找不透明令牌，发现它已过期，并将用户注销。

    请注意，这并不能解决客户端上存在仍然有效的 JWT 的问题。 但是，因为您可以使这些 JWT 的寿命更短（甚至只有几分钟），所以您应该会发现这不是什么大问题。

    通过这种方式，您保留了使用 JWT 的主要好处之一，那就是您不必在每次 API 调用时继续访问数据库来验证 JWT - 您只需要在刷新会话时进行数据库查找- 这很少发生。

    如果这听起来很适合您的用例，请查看 SuperTokens 对此会话流的实现。 此外，在每次使用时更改刷新令牌会增加额外的安全优势，例如能够检测会话劫持。

    由SuperTokens的人们撰写 — 希望您喜欢！ 我们始终在我们的Discord服务器上可用。 如果您有任何问题或需要任何帮助，请加入我们。



原文:

Depending on who you listen to, JWTs are either a panacea for all your authentication problems or should be avoided like the plague. We're in two minds here at SuperTokens.

What is a JWT token?

A JWT, or JSON Web Token, is a string / token issued by the server that asserts properties contained in its "payload". Its most common use case is for authentication (OAuth 2.0 + Open ID Connect) and session management.

As the name suggests, a JWT can contain any information inside it in JSON form. This is also known as "JWT claims". For example, for session management, the JSON would at least need to contain the logged in user's userId:

{ "userId": "...", "expiry": 1646472008501, ...
}

If the JWT containing this information needs to expire, the JSON can also contain the token's expiry time (as shown above). There is also a convention for the names of the keys to these fields. In our example above those are:

  • userId -> sub
  • expiry -> exp

Taking an example of the login process, a JSON containing the above information will be sent to the client, and then on each request, the client can send this JSON back to the server. This way, the server knows which userID is querying its APIs. If the JSON has expired, then the server can reject the request, and the user must login again.

However, from a security point of view, it's very easy for the client to change the userId value in the JSON and spoof being another user. To prevent this, the server sends the original JSON's "signature" along with the JSON. This "signature" is created using a secret that is known only by the server, so the client cannot create a signature of a JSON by itself. Therefore, if the client changes the JSON, the server will fail to match the original JSON's signature with the incoming / changed JSON's signature (this is known as verifying the signature) and can then reject the request.

There are several algorithms that a server can use to generate a signature for a JSON:

  • HMAC + SHA256
  • RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 + SHA256
  • ECDSA + P-256 + SHA256

The signing method chosen to create the signature must somehow be encoded in the JWT so that the same method is used when verifying the signature.

All in all, a JWT contains three parts:

  • The header string containing information about the signing algorithm used.
  • The body string containing the actual JSON.
  • The signature string that can be used to verify that the JWT has not been changed by the client.

These three sections are concatenated with a . separator to form the full JWT Token. An example JWT can be later seen in this blog post.

Advantages of JWT Tokens

The JWT approach certainly has its advantages over opaque tokens. JWTs are:

  • Self-contained: The JWT can contain the user's details (not just a session ID, like a cookie but other custom data such as user name and even permissions), together with the token's expiry time so you don't need to query a database for that information. This is completely unlike an opaque token which, by its very nature, is just a meaningless jumble of alphanumeric characters.
  • Secure: JWTs are digitally signed using either a secret (HMAC) or a public/private key pair (RSA or ECDSA) which safeguards them from being modified by the client or an attacker.
  • Stored only on the client: You generate JWTs on the server and send them to the client. The client then submits the JWT with every request. This saves database space.
  • Efficient: It’s quick to verify a JWT, because it doesn’t require a database lookup.

Disadvantages of JWT Tokens

The fact that JWTs are only stored client-side leads to a fundamental disadvantage with JWTs. And that is: how do you revoke a user's access?

Sure, JWTs have an expiry time and this can be as short-lived as you like. As soon as the access token expires however, the JWT is invalid and the client must re-authenticate with your server. This, of course, has a negative effect on the user experience..

But suppose the user intentionally logs out of your system? Or you want to kick them out because you fear that security has been compromised? You can't[1]: if they still have an unexpired token, they still have access.

There is a solution to this problem, but it does involve some extra work on your part. It is a technique that requires maintaining a JWT blacklist/deny list. In this article we'll show you what a JWT blacklist/deny list is, how to implement one, and discuss whether it is a good solution to this problem or not.

What is a JWT blacklist/deny list?

A JWT blacklist/deny list is a list of tokens that should no longer grant access to your system.

Where you maintain this list is up to you. You could use a traditional database, but a much better approach is to use an in-memory data cache, like Redis. An in-memory data cache provides much faster and more predictable seek times than data stored on disk.

That’s what we’ll use in this example, and we’ll code our solution using Node.js and Express. If that’s not your chosen technology stack then fear not: the fundamental approach is the same regardless of how you choose to build it.

OK, so how do I do it?

First, you’ll need to instantiate your Express server application and set up Redis so that you can maintain a list of active JWTs:

import express from "express";
import bodyparser from "body-parser";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
import redis from "redis"; const JWT_SECRET = "Ultra-secure-secret"; const app = express();
app.use(bodyparser.urlencoded({ extended: false }));
app.use(bodyparser.json()); let redisClient = null; (async () => { redisClient = redis.createClient(); redisClient.on("error", (error) => { console.log(error); }); redisClient.on("connect", () => { console.log("Redis connected!"); }); await redisClient.connect();
})(); // listen for requests
const listener = app.listen(3000, () => { console.log("Server running");
});

To test the storage and subsequent revocation of a JWT, you’ll need a way to create a user and generate a JWT for that user. Let’s add the createUser endpoint for this. You can make a POST request to this endpoint with the name of the new user:

import jwt from "jsonwebtoken"; app.post("/createUser", (request, response) => { const token = generateAccessToken({ username: request.body.username }); response.json(token);
}); const generateAccessToken = (username) => { return jwt.sign(username, JWT_SECRET, { expiresIn: "3600s" });
};

Hit that endpoint, and you’ll see a JWT being issued for the user:

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:3000/createUser' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{ "username": "Derek"
}' "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1c2VybmFtZSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiaWF0IjoxNjQxMzA3MTgxLCJleHAiOjE2NDEzMTA3ODF9.3yrIJpWMS952QVakifjviiTs0ANJOmxZDovQO0N5brQ"

If you paste that token into the online debugger, you’ll see that the JWT has the user name encoded within it, along with other details such as the time it was issued (iat) and the time it expires (exp):

{ "username": "Derek", "iat": 1641307181, "exp": 1641310781
}

The next thing you need to do is authenticate the user with that JWT whenever that user attempts to do anything with your application. This is a three-step process. First, you’ll want to ensure that the JWT has been supplied. Then, you’ll want to test whether the JWT supplied is in the blacklist/deny list stored in Redis. If it’s not, then you’ll finally want to verify that the JWT is valid.

We can encode these steps as Express middleware:

// JWT middleware
const authenticateToken = async (request, response, next) => { const authHeader = request.headers["authorization"]; const token = authHeader && authHeader.split(" ")[1]; // token provided? if (token == null) { return response.status(401).send({ message: "No token provided", }); } // token in deny list? const inDenyList = await redisClient.get(`bl_${token}`); if (inDenyList) { return response.status(401).send({ message: "JWT Rejected", }); } // token valid? jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET, (error, user) => { if (error) { return response.status(401).send({ status: "error", message: error.message, }); } request.userId = user.username; request.tokenExp = user.exp; request.token = token; next(); });
};

Now what will happen is that any time the user you created earlier attempts to visit your home route (/), the JWT they supply will be checked against the blacklist/deny list first and then verified before being granted access.

That’s all well and good, but at the moment you have no way to revoke a JWT, so there’s nothing in the blacklist/deny list.

Let’s create another route that will simulate a user logging out. When this endpoint is hit, the user’s JWT is persisted to Redis, using the format bl_<token> for the key, and the value being the actual token. We’ll set the key to expire when the token itself expires, so as not to fill up Redis with lots of expired tokens.

app.post("/logout", authenticateToken, async (request, response) => { const { userId, token, tokenExp } = request; const token_key = `bl_${token}`; await redisClient.set(token_key, token); redisClient.expireAt(token_key, tokenExp); return response.status(200).send("Token invalidated");
});

Issue a POST request to the /logout endpoint:

curl --location --request GET 'http://localhost:3000/' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ1c2VybmFtZSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiaWF0IjoxNjQxMzA4NTQwLCJleHAiOjE2NDEzMTIxNDB9.nlJJe7XtK3PJVgvjevrHabeImqJyRoUDiXejDhVK5yM'

Now the user’s JWT is technically still valid (until it expires), but now it’s in the blacklist/deny list and will therefore be intercepted on subsequent requests. Repeat the POST request to the home route above and you’ll get an HTTP 403 Forbidden error, with a message "JWT Rejected".

However, this won’t help if you want to deny access to a user who hasn’t logged out and whose token is still valid. The problem is that you won’t know what the user’s access token is at that time, and therefore you won’t know what to put in the cache.

To solve this issue, you should add the ability to blacklist a user’s userID via redis and check that blacklist as well during JWT verification. Unlike the JWT blacklist, this entry won't have an expiry time associated with it.

You can find a working version of this example on Github.

Is this a good idea?

So now you see that this concept of a JWT blacklist/deny list is relatively easy to implement. But is it the best way of handling this situation?

In our opinion, it’s not.

The biggest advantage of JWTs is that they make session verification fast. If you maintain a blacklist/deny list and have to query it on every API call, then you’ve lost that advantage.

Instead, what we advocate is a solution for session management that combines the respective strengths of both JWTs and opaque tokens.

In this workflow, when the user logs in, the server issues a both short-lived JWT (the access token), and a long-lived opaque token (the refresh token). When a user is granted access, both of these tokens are sent to the client.

When the user attempts to access a resource, they send the JWT access token along with every request. When the JWT expires, the client then uses the opaque refresh token to request a new JWT and a new opaque refresh token. This process is known as refresh token rotation.

The client then uses this new JWT to make subsequent requests and the process continues.

The advantages and disadvantages of issuing two tokens and of implementing refresh token rotation

The benefits of this approach is that if you want to revoke access, then all you need to do is invalidate the opaque token on the server side. Then, when the refresh endpoint is called, the server looks up the opaque token, sees that it has expired, and logs the user out.

Note that this doesn’t solve the problem of still-valid JWTs existing on the client. But, because you can make those JWTs much more short-lived (even just a few mins), you should find that it’s not too big a problem.

In this way, you keep one of the key benefits of using JWTs and that’s the fact that you don’t have to keep accessing the database on each API call to verify the JWT - you only need to do a database lookup when refreshing the session - which happens relatively rarely.

If this sounds ideal for your use case, check out SuperTokens’ implementation of this session flow. SuperTokens uses this method to harness all the benefits of using JWTs while mitigating many of their disadvantages by combining them with opaque tokens.

Furthermore, changing the refresh token on each use adds additional security benefits like being able to detect session hijacking.

Written by the Folks at SuperTokens — hope you enjoyed! We are always available on our Discord server. Join us if you have any questions or need any help.
Items.Author: supertokens

Items.Title: 如何使用 PHP cURL 从远程站点下载文件 | How to download a file from a remote site using PHP cURL
Items.Description:
首先，您需要从 sourceforge 下载 html dom 解析器文件。 那么这段代码最适合你。
这是您可以从momix apk站点下载 momix.apk 文件的代码。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 23 4月23日

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Apr 23 4月23日



原文:

First of all you need to download html dom parser file from sourceforge. Then this code will work for you best.
Here is the code you can download momix.apk file from momix apk site.

Items.Title: 什么是 DOM？ | What is DOM ?
Items.Description:
首先，我正在考虑回答“什么是 DOM？”。 正如命名的文档对象模型是关于文档结构的标准化。 核心 DOM

  • 您所需要的只是用于操作 HTML 元素的 HTML DOM。
    所有 HTML 元素都是对象。

     # HTML is document element, child of root node. <html> <head> # HEAD is a child of document element. <title> Welcome! </title> </head> <body> # BODY is a child of document element. <p> Hello, programming world! </p> </body> </html>

    它允许根据您的需要进行操作，如添加、删除、修改。

    文档是根节点。 根节点作为一个名为 HTML 的子节点。 您可以使用不同的方式访问元素，也可以操作文档。 例如，通过上述方式，您可以在加载正文时执行诸如发送警报之类的操作，或者您可以使用其 id 访问元素。 样品可能会有所不同，但我们可以对它做任何事情。 您还可以禁用有关文档的功能以防止出现安全问题。 完全访问可能会导致 XSS 等严重漏洞。

    您可以通过编写函数来创建新元素。 例如，让我们用一个小函数创建一个简单的下拉列表。 之前，我们有一个 id 为 my_select 的空列表。 当窗口加载时，函数将运行并填充下拉列表，直到我达到 9 。

     <html> <head> <script> window.onload = function() { const select_element = document.getElementById("my_select"); for(let i=0; i< 10; i++){ let option = document.createElement("option"); option.text=`Option ${i}`; select_element.add(option); } } </script> </head> <body> <select id="my_select"></select> </body> </html>

    我采样了一些你可以通过复制粘贴自由地尝试上面的代码并开始操作 DOM。 你可以做任何事情； 改变背景，隐藏元素，创建表格，随心所欲！

    节点和元素（不一样吗？）

    节点和元素之间的区别很容易，但如果您不知道区别，就会感到困惑。 节点可以是内置的，也可以是“div”之类的 HTML 标签之一，元素是特定类型的节点。

    节点功能取决于它的子类，没有节点对象之类的东西，它是一种通用类型的元素。 主要的东西是 Document、Element 和 DocumentFragment。

    节点与 DOM 树中的其他节点有关系。 在下面的示例中，HTML 节点是 BODY 的父节点，而 BODY 是 HTML 的子节点。 HEAD and BODY nodes are siblings because of both have same parent as HTML. HEAD 和 BODY 节点是兄弟节点，因为它们与 HTML 具有相同的父节点。

     <html> <head> </head> <body> </body> </html>

    getElementById(), querySelector() returns an Element type of object, getElementById()，querySelector() 返回一个 Element 类型的对象，
    getElementsByTagName(), querySelectorAll() returns collection of nodes. getElementsByTagName()、querySelectorAll() 返回节点集合。

    [SOURCE] [来源]
    https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Document_Object_Model/Introduction https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Document_Object_Model/Introduction
    https://www.javascripttutorial.net/javascript-dom/ https://www.javascripttutorial.net/javascript-dom/



    • 原文:

    First of all I’m thinking about to answer to “what is DOM?”. As named Document Object Model is the standardization about the document structures. DOM allows to reach the content, update something on it and also style on document.

    There’re 3 types of DOM

    • Core DOM
    • XML DOM
    • HTML DOM

    HTML DOM is the standart for HTML. HTML DOM defines objects, properties, methods and events.

    All you need is HTML DOM for the manipulate HTML elements.
    HTML DOM can be manipulated with JavaScript or another languages, JavaScript. DOM is not a part of JS, but you can use it.

    All HTML elements are the objects.

    # HTML is document element, child of root node.
<html> 
   <head> # HEAD is a child of document element.
      <title> Welcome! </title> 
   </head>
   <body> # BODY is a child of document element.
      <p> Hello, programming world! </p>
   </body>
</html>

    HTML document is formed with tree of nodes to represent by DOM. It allows to manipulate as your needs as add, remove, modify.

    Document is the root node. Root node as a child named HTML. HTML element is named document element, child of the root node.

    <html>
   <head>
      <title>Welcome!</title>
      <script>
         console.log(document.getElementById('hello').innerHTML)
      </script>
   </head>
   <body onload="alert('Welcome to my home page!');">
      <p id="hello">Hello, programming world!</p>
   </body>
</html>

    You can access the elements with different ways, manipulate the document also. For example by above, you can do something like send alert while body loads or you can reach the element with its id. Samples can be vary, but simply we can do anything on it. You can also disable the functionality about document to prevent security issues. Full access may cause critical vulnerabilities like XSS.

    You can create new elements by writing functions. For example, let’s create a simple dropdown list with a small function. Before, we have an empty list given id as my_select. When the window loads, function will run and fill the dropdown list till i reach to 9 .

    <html>
  <head>
    <script>
      window.onload = function() {
        const select_element = document.getElementById("my_select");
        for(let i=0; i< 10; i++){
          let option = document.createElement("option");
          option.text=`Option ${i}`;
          select_element.add(option);
        }
      }
    </script>
  </head>
  <body>
    <select id="my_select"></select>
  </body>
</html>

    I sampled a few You can freely try above codes by copy&paste and start to manipulate DOM. You can do anything; change background, hide elements, create a table, whatevery you want!

    Node and Element ( Isn’t it same? )

    Distinction between node and element is easy but if you don’t know the difference it would be confusing. Any object in the DOM tree is named as node. Node can be built-in or one of the HTML tags like “div”, element is specific type of node.

    Node functionality depends on its subclasses, there is no such thing as Node object, it’s a generic type of element. Main things are Document, Element and DocumentFragment.

    Nodes have relationships with other nodes in DOM tree. With below sample, HTML node is parent of BODY, and BODY is child node of HTML. HEAD and BODY nodes are siblings because of both have same parent as HTML.

    <html>
  <head>
  </head>
  <body>
  </body>
</html>

    getElementById(), querySelector() returns an Element type of object,
    getElementsByTagName(), querySelectorAll() returns collection of nodes.

    [SOURCE]
    https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Document_Object_Model/Introduction
    https://www.javascripttutorial.net/javascript-dom/

原文:

Vue is a versatile technology that can be used to build applications without requiring a lot of resources. It is a progressive JavaScript framework used for designing any user interface, with 195k stars on GitHub.

Vue is extremely customizable when compared to other frameworks on the market. This implies you don’t have to fully comprehend all of the principles. It has received a lot of attention from programmers in a short space of time.

Vue framework is used for creating progressive user interfaces. It is built from the ground up and can function as both a library and a framework, depending on your needs. It is made up of a view layer-focused core library and an ecosystem of supplementary libraries. Evan You is the creator of Vue. Vue is a progressive JavaScript framework that is a faster alternative to Angular. VueJS, unlike Angular and React, is not funded by major behemoths such as Facebook and Google. Despite this, VueJS has become the most popular js framework because to its numerous features and benefits.

It is relatively compact, lightweight, and simple to use when compared to more complicated front-end frameworks such as Angular, Backbone, and React. Furthermore, Vue.js is gaining popularity among developers at a rapid pace.

Gitlab, Alibaba, Adobe, Xiaomi, Euronews, Behance, Nintendo, Grammarly, Codeship, and many others are among the brands that use VueJs.

Furthermore, Vue is trusted by the majority of well-known brands for their online apps. As a result, it could be a good strategy for you to use it for your business. A custom web application development company is equipped with the necessary skill sets to provide the best of the development world for your business.

Let’s get started with the Best Vue Devtools for Developers. These developer tools for VueJS are really useful. So, keep an eye on these top VueJS devtools.

Official Vue.Js Guide
The official Vue.js tutorial will assist you in getting started with the framework. A thorough learning guide, the Vue.js API documentation, the official coding style guide, code samples, and more are also included.

Storybook
Storybook is the top in our list of the greatest vue devtools. It’s a UI development and testing environment which supports Vue, React, React Native, Angular, and Ember, among other JavaScript frameworks. Storybook allows you to develop your own component library in the form of a series of stories. A story is a representation of a single state of a component (like a visual test case). A component can have a lot of stories, and a Storybook generally has a lot of them.

Source: Storybook

The Vue tool will make it simple for developers to create and test beautiful UI components. Even the isolated environment is supported by the tool. Furthermore, Storybook will enable developers to construct components apart from the main project. There is always the option to hire dedicated developers for your web development requirements, to get it right on the first go without wasting precious time and funds.

Element
Element UI is one of the most popular Vue.js 2.0 UI toolkits at the moment. It comes with a plethora of customizable Vue components that you can use to create a professional-looking web app. Element UI, on the other hand, has a large community and is actively developed, with new changes being pushed every day. You may also utilize Element UI’s starting kit, which was built by Vue CLI, to get started quickly.

Source: Element

You can build a web application using these components without compromising any of your ideas. You can also use Element UI components and functionalities to construct the web application precisely how you envisioned it. To start, you can utilize the Element UI starting kit, and then upgrade to the full toolkit created by Vue CLI later. For VueJS developers, this toolkit might be regarded one of the top Vue devtools.

Features:

  • Interactive
  • Customizable themes
  • Downloadable resources to increase efficiency

NuxtJS
NuxtJS is a Vue framework that is simple to use. After you’ve set up VueJS, the first step in constructing your apps is to put up a suitable boilerplate that allows you to prevent writing code from start. There are a variety of boilerplate development alternatives, but NuxtJS allows you to create Single Page Applications (SPAs), Progressive Web Applications (PWAs), Server-Side Rendered (SSRs), and Static Sites.

Source: NUXT

Furthermore, Nuxt speeds up your development process because of its modular architecture and over 50 modules which help you do things like integrate Google Analytics, get PWA advantages, and generate a sitemap. Nuxt’s bundle analyzer also supports VueJS and NodeJS best practices and provides out-of-the-box performance tweaking.

Vuetify
Vuetify is a well-known Vue.js component framework that adheres to the Material Design standards set out by Google. Cards, carousels, buttons, icons, toolbars, and other design elements are among the 80+ semantic and reusable Vue components included. Vuetify works with all current web browsers. It also includes pre-built project scaffolding so you can get started constructing your Vue.js app with only one command.

Source: Vuetify

Features:

  • WCAG Accessibility (a11y) / WCAG / Section 508 / RTL support
  • 30+ supported languages
  • Smaller bundle sizes with automatic tree-shaking
  • A massive Discord community
  • 18 months of Long-term support for Major releases
  • Extensive customization options with SASS/SCSS and Presets
  • Responsive support down to 320px width

Materio
Materio Vuetify Vuejs Admin Dashboard Template — is the latest Vuetify-based Admin Dashboard Template that is both developer-friendly and extremely customizable. Furthermore, the highest industry standards are taken into account to deliver users one of the best VueJS admin templates that are not only quick and simple to use, but also highly scalable. With maximum ease and versatility, you’ll be able to create any application you desire with no effort. You may also check out at Laravel Admin Templates built with VueJS.

Source: Materio

Materio also has a number of useful features aimed at assisting you in creating high-quality single-page apps precisely as you want them. Fuzzy search, Dark, Semi-Dark, and Bordered layout choices, Advanced cards, and Charts are just a few of the interesting and valuable features. It also has several fantastic features, such as the ones listed below.

Features:

  • Pure Vue js, No Jquery Dependency
  • Created with Vue CLI
  • Utilizes Vuex, Vue Router, Webpack
  • Code Splitting, Lazy loading
  • JWT Authentication
  • Access Control (ACL)
  • i18n Page
  • Multi-lingual Support
  • 2 chart libraries
  • 3 Dashboard
  • SASS Powered and many more.

ViteJS
ViteJS is a build tool for contemporary web projects that attempts to make development easier and faster. Vite is likewise uncompromising and ships with good defaults out of the box, but it’s also very extendable because because of its Plugin API and JavaScript API, both of which have complete type support. It is divided into two sections:

Source: ViteJs

A development server with several features above native ES modules, such as highly fast Hot Module Replacement (HMR). A build command which bundles your code using Rollup and sets it up to produce highly optimized static assets for deployment.

Features:

  • NPM Dependency Resolving and Pre-Bundling
  • Hot Module Replacement
  • TypeScript
  • Vue
  • JSX
  • CSS
  • Static Assets
  • JSON

Vue-CLI
Vue-CLI is a set of tools for working with VueJS. It allows you to quickly develop project structures and boilerplates. It also allows you to create rapid prototypes to demonstrate new features. In addition to the typical bells and whistles, the Vue CLI has a lot more support for web development tools like Babel, ESLint, TypeScript, PostCSS, PWA, Unit Testing, and End-to-end Testing. The tool is also compatible with community-created third-party plugins that may be extended.

Source: Vue Cli

Furthermore, ejecting is not required with Vue CLI. In comparison to other frameworks, the Vue CLI allows for a greater level of flexibility. Vue CLI’s Graphical User Interface (GUI) is one of the most useful features, allowing you to easily create new projects and manage them.

Vuex
Vuex is a Vue.js application state management paradigm and library. It’s one of the finest Vue devtools since it acts as a centralized store for all of an application’s components, with rules guaranteeing that the state may only be changed in a predictable way. Advanced capabilities like zero-config time-travel debugging and state snapshot export/import are also available thanks to integration with Vue’s official devtools plugin.

Source: Vuex

All state management tasks are centralized in this vuejs development tool, and states may only be altered in a predictable manner. Furthermore, mutations depending on State, View, and Actions are permitted. Furthermore, the state specifies the application’s truth, which the user sees through the view, and actions cause the state to change, beginning the mutation cycle. Vuex additionally includes functions such as state snapshot export/import and zero-config time-travel debugging.

IView
Another excellent Vue UI library to consider is View UI. It includes a plethora of useful and attractive Vue components. It will also assist you in creating a fantastic web application. It’s also a high-quality Vue UI component library that includes hundreds of useful and beautiful components. As a result, it’s simple to get started with, and you can even develop new projects visually.

Source: iView

Features:

  • Dozens of useful and beautiful components.
  • Friendly API. It is appropriate for individuals of all skill levels.
  • Extensive documentation and demos.
  • It is quite beautiful.

Vue Performance DevTool for Performance Testing
Performance testing is a method of measuring and identifying all of the aforementioned performance bottlenecks. Vue Performance DevTools is a browser plugin that improves the performance of your website. The purpose of this plugin is to examine the performance of Vue components.

Source: Devtools

Furthermore, it does a statistical analysis of a Vue component’s performance using Vue’s ‘window.performance’ API. The following are a couple of wide range of applications of Vue Performance DevTools:

  • Remove or unmount any component instances that aren’t in use.
  • Examine the reasons that cause an operation to stop or take longer once it has begun.
  • Examine the component that takes the longest to load.

Nativescript
NativeScript allows you to use native APIs straight from JavaScript. For rich mobile programming, the framework presently includes iOS and Android runtimes. It can also be used for a variety of different uses.

Quasar
Quasar is a high-performance Material Design component package with extensions for SPAs, SSRs, PWAs, Mobile (Cordova & Capacitor), Desktop (Electron), and Browsers. As a web developer, this enables you to swiftly design responsive websites/apps in a variety of styles.

Source: Quasar

A UMD (Unified Module Definition) version is also available from Quasar. It can be added to the current project via CSS and JS HTML elements. It also has built-in support for SSR (Server-side Rendered App). As a result, it is one of the most highly recommended vue devtools.

Features:

  • Performance-focused framework
  • PWAs (Progressive Web App)
  • BEX (Browser Extension)
  • Mobile Apps (Android, iOS) through Cordova or Capacitor

So, if you’re going to be doing Vue development, we recommend making a list of your requirements first, and then picking the best Vue Devtools that fit your project’s demands. We recommend working with a professional custom web application development company if you’re working on a project that requires dedicated frontend developers. A professional organization will employ the most up-to-date VueJs technology to provide you with the best results.

View Original : Top 13 Vue Devtools

Items.Title: Armazenando dados sensíveis em código Terraform utilizando KMS | Armazenando dados sensíveis em código Terraform utilizando KMS
Items.Description:
详细信息：aqui nos estamos utilizando uma policy do tipo resource-based 。

  • 注意：没有数据源how_to_terraform_kms_pd ，没有定义 o帐户 ID como 111111111111 eo usuário como meu- usuario 。 利用 para isso o seguinte comando：

     aws kms encrypt --key-id ${ key_id } \ --plaintext ${ texto_a_ser_criptografado } \ --output text \ --query CiphertextBlob

    注意：não se esqueça que você deve ter exportado as variáveis como AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID corretamente para acessar sua conta。

    参考文献

    • 使用 KMS 在 terraform 代码库中存储机密


原文:

Tratando-se de IaC, chegará cedo ou tarde o fatídico dia em que você terá que colocar uma senha, chave, ou qualquer outro dado sigiloso no seu código. Algumas pessoas não se preocupam muito quando estão versionando seus projetos, levando em consideração coisas como o fato de seus repositórios serem privados, por exemplo. Entretanto, sejamos sinceros: não há lá muita garantia que o código fique só no seu repositório, não é?

A grande maioria das ferramentas hoje conta com pelo menos um mecanismo para resolver esse pequeno probleminha. O Ansible, por exemplo, conta com o Ansible Vault. Já o Terraform conta com vários como, por exemplo, a integração com o Vault (também da Hashicorp).

Neste breve artigo, demonstrarei como manter seus dados seguros utilizando o AWS KMS (Key Management Service), serviço da AWS utilizado para armazenamento e criptografia da AWS que conta com 20.000 requisições gratuitas no free tier, e que pode ser utilizado entre múltiplos serviços da AWS.

O primeiro passo é a criação de uma chave dentro do KMS, que pode ser feita por meio do próprio Terraform ou através da Console. Caso opte pela primeira opção, serão necessários três tipos diferentes de objetos Terraform: um data source (aws_iam_policy_document) e dois resources (aws_kms_key e aws_kms_alias). Abaixo uma breve descrição do papel que cada um desempenhará:

  • aws_iam_policy_document: este é o data source onde definiremos o documento da política informando usuários e suas respectivas permissões referentes às chaves e recursos dentro do KMS.

Detalhe: aqui nós estamos utilizando uma policy do tipo resource-based.

  • aws_kms_key: a chave propriamente dita. Aqui informaremos o tipo da chave, se ela é simétrica ou assimétrica, se ela pode ser utilizada para criptografar ou descriptografar, assim como associar o documento criado no data source para definir uma política de acesso à ela';

  • aws_kms_alias: um alias nada mais é do que um apelido utilizado para referenciar a chave.

Abaixo o código Terraform:

data "aws_iam_policy_document" "how_to_terraform_kms_pd" { statement { sid = "Enable IAM User Permissions" effect = "Allow" principals { type = "AWS" identifiers = [ "arn:aws:iam::111111111111:root" ] } actions = [ "kms:*" ] resources = [ "*" ] } statement { sid = "Allow use of the key" effect = "Allow" principals { type = "AWS" identifiers = [ "arn:aws:iam::111111111111:user/meu-usuario" ] } actions = [ "kms:Encrypt", "kms:Decrypt", "kms:ReEncrypt*", "kms:GenerateDataKey*", "kms:DescribeKey" ] resources = [ "*" ] } statement { sid = "Allow attachment of persistent resources" principals { type = "AWS" identifiers = [ "arn:aws:iam::111111111111:user/meu-usuario" ] } actions = [ "kms:CreateGrant", "kms:ListGrants", "kms:RevokeGrant" ] resources = [ "*" ] condition { test = "Bool" variable = "kms:GrantIsForAWSResource" values = [ "true" ] } }
} resource "aws_kms_key" "how_to_terraform_kms" { description = "Teste de criptografia com KMS" key_usage = "ENCRYPT_DECRYPT" customer_master_key_spec = "SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT" policy = data.aws_iam_policy_document.how_to_terraform_kms_pd.json
} resource "aws_kms_alias" "how_to_terraform_kms_alias" { name = "alias/how_to_terraform_kms" target_key_id = aws_kms_key.how_to_terraform_kms.key_id
} output "how_to_terraform_kms_id" { value = aws_kms_alias.how_to_terraform_kms.key_id
}

Nota: no data source how_to_terraform_kms_pd, nós definimos o account ID como 111111111111 e o usuário como meu-usuario. Ajuste esses valores para o seu caso.

Após um terraform plan e um terraform apply, os recursos já devem estar presentes na sua conta AWS. Note que há também um output que imprime o ID da chave recém-criada e caso você opte por consultá-lo diretamente na Console, o mesmo pode ser removido.

Com a chave pronta para uso, agora é hora de criptografar algo. E é aí que o nosso velho amigo AWS CLI entra em cena. Utilize para isso o seguinte comando:

aws kms encrypt --key-id ${key_id} \  --plaintext ${texto_a_ser_criptografado} \ --output text \ --query CiphertextBlob

Nota: não se esqueça que você deve ter exportado as variáveis como AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID corretamente para acessar sua conta.

Se tudo der certo, o valor criptografado deve aparecer na tela.

Neste momento você deve estar se perguntando "OK, mas o propósito não era esconder as coisas no código do Terraform?". Estamos chegando lá. Este valor criptografado que nós geramos utilizando o AWS CLI pode ser resgatado dentro do próprio Terraform, que consulta o KMS durante sua execução utilizando o data source aws_kms_secrets da seguinte forma:

data "aws_kms_secrets" "teste_kms" { secret { name = "valor_resgatado" payload = "AQICAHhAVXI4s1jnWVzWUxKnuk9yI2/aDoEQu8vyYkhh0tUC2QEMKXf54sQFje6ImNjDyraEAAAAcjBwBgkqhkiG9w0BBwagYzBhAgEAMFwGCSqGSIb3DQEHATAeBglghkgBZQMEAS4wEQQMoKPEjuATAOXqrzraAgEQgC9Kwf2DiUTxrDy0g8kggiLc+bhWhuYLDQaB/DhsMxyV9pD6ROsT/boZztyJFD30oQ==" }
} output "valor_resgatado" { value = data.aws_kms_secrets.teste_kms.plaintext["valor_resgatado"]
}

Dentro do bloco secret do data source você deve dar um nome único utilizado para referenciá-lo e o seu valor criptográfico. Não é necessário informar a chave, uma vez que ela já está contida no payload. Você pode então referenciar o secret em qualquer parte do seu código utilizando a notação data.aws_kms_secrets.teste_kms.plaintext["valor_resgatado"]. Você pode incluir quantos blocos secret quiser dentro do data source.

Referências

  • Storing secrets in terraform codebase with KMS
Items.Author: stefanomartins

Items.Title: 制造业中的机器学习 | Machine Learning in Manufacturing
Items.Description:
机器学习 (ML) 是人工智能 (AI) 的一个子领域，它研究和理解数据中的模式、关系和主题，以支持无需人工输入的学习、处理和决策制定。 简而言之，机器学习是使计算机和机器足够“智能”以发展他们的理解并使他们能够像任何人一样做出决策和预测的过程。

这个使机器能够进行类人学习的过程是通过向机器学习算法提交大量数据来实现的，然后机器学习算法仅根据提供的数据分析并提出数据驱动和以数据为中心的建议。 由于多种原因，机器学习技术的使用在过去二十年中激增，以及现有机器学习技术的可访问性和能力得到改善。 如今，机器学习广泛应用于生产的多个方面，例如管理、优化、监控、控制和诊断。

一项调查显示，92% 的商业行业顶尖企业已经在投资机器学习、自动化和人工智能。 在过去几年中，通过实施机器学习，流程生产力和效率提高了 55%。

这些统计数据清楚地表明了机器学习在制造业中对于增加利润、降低成本、提高产品质量、缩短生产时间和提高流程效率的重要性。 机器学习如何帮助制造业？

鉴于快速发展、不可预测的工业环境带来的挑战，作为 AI 的一部分，ML 可以学习和适应变化，因此所有者、经理、设计师和软件工程师不必预测所有潜在情况并给出答案这可能会在整个生产过程中出现。 因此，鉴于大多数第一性原理建模技术在处理适应性方面的困难，ML 为其在制造中的应用提供了一个令人信服的案例。 机器学习的一个重要特征是它能够有效地训练和适应不同的环境。

只有在检查数据并将其转换为可用信息之后，它才会变得有用。 这在制造业中尤其如此，因为在现场生产计划期间很难在平衡技术、经济和知识限制的同时获取实时数据。 在制造中使用机器学习可以从当前数据集中提取模式，这可用于建立对系统未来行为的估计。 这些额外的数据（知识）可能有助于流程所有者做出更好的决策，或者可以常规用于增强服务。

在各个行业中，ML 技术已成功用于制造中的性能改进、跟踪和管理应用以及预测性维护。 机器学习模型为改进制造系统中的质量控制优化提供了良好的潜力，特别是在识别故障点很棘手的复杂制造环境中。 在一些 NP-hard 制造挑战中，机器学习开发人员可以帮助减少周期时间和浪费，以及增加资源使用。 此外，机器学习为大型复杂制造过程的持续质量改进提供了强大的工具。‍

机器学习在工业中的应用在哪里？

制造业是世界经济中最重要的行业之一，因为它在全球创造了高达 13.9 万亿美元的产值。 最重要的生产目标之一是以尽可能低的成本制造更多高质量的产品。 然而，对于缺乏必要资源和技能来创造和制造高质量产品的公司来说，生产产品可能是一个成本极高且要求极高的过程。 然而，由于在制造业中使用机器学习，制造业的面貌在过去几年中发生了很大变化。 下面提到的列表突出了制造业中机器学习正在实现的最普遍的领域。

与传统组织相比，使用机器学习技术的制造商能够更快地行动。 借助连接、复杂分析、自动化等的潜力，从制造效率到产品个性化的一切都可以改变。 制造商可以通过结合这些技术来提高上市速度、提高服务效率并建立新的商业模式以提高生产力。‍

数据已成为一种非常宝贵的资产，现在收集和处理数据比以往任何时候都更实惠。

在人工智能特别是机器学习的帮助下，制造商正在利用这些数据来大幅提高利润率。 机器学习可以帮助制造商以多种方式利用他们的关键生产数据。 该数据可以是任何类型的数据。 它可能是消费数据、运输数据、历史数据、运营数据或库存数据。 机器学习算法擅长从结构化数据中获得有用的见解。 通过在制造中使用数据分析，人们可以在工作场所创造一个平静的环境，因为它通过评估生产过程的每个组成部分、提供数据和修改过程的每个阶段来处理所有事情。‍

制造商可以使用重要数据来构建具有更高消费者价值的产品，并降低将新产品推向市场的相关风险。 在对产品进行规划、战略制定和建模时，会考虑可行的见解，这有助于做出判断。

机器学习模型为消费者预测提供了一定程度的复杂性。 这些模型将包括来自各种来源的历史和当前数据，例如 ERP 系统、销售点系统和社交媒体营销活动。 此信息可能与其他相关变量（例如原材料定价、供应商冲突和天气中断）配对，以确定产品的市场相关性。 与传统预测系统不同，机器学习解决方案将根据新数据不断更新。

这些算法可以通过考虑能源定价、员工工资、设备维护和存储最小化来安排进行能源密集型运营的最佳时间，从而最大限度地降低成本。‍

据福布斯报道，使用机器学习来自动化质量测试可以将检测率提高多达 90%。 对于产品检查和故障检测，正在使用机器学习。 制造商可以通过快速准确地诊断生产设备过程问题和潜在威胁来减少设备停机时间，从而获得竞争优势。 随着越来越多的消费者要求制造商以合理的成本快速提供高质量的商品，机器学习算法越来越多地用于制造业，以检测生产系统的问题。‍

生产人员确定与特定制造过程相关的理想需求集的努力可能会通过 ML 方法的规范分析能力得到增强，从而允许改进和优化制造过程。 在未来几年中，机器学习在制造和流程优化中的融合领域将产生工业分析发现，从而能够以最小的浪费实现更快的大规模和个性化生产。‍

设备维护是制造业务中最大的支出之一，使工厂和工厂因意外停机而损失约 600 亿美元，其中 42% 是由于资产故障造成的。 因此，预测性维护已成为可能有助于节省大量资金的关键选项。 复杂的 ML 模型正在生成有关资源和机器状况的可靠预测。 在预测性维护的帮助下，设备的剩余使用寿命 (RUL) 显着延长。 如果必须修复或更改某些内容，专家会提前知道，甚至会知道要采用哪些程序。

机器学习算法可以使用来自植入机器和设备的传感器的数据来生成预测性维护模型，从而消除维护操作中的猜测。 可以使用机器学习自动发起服务请求，以通知人员和工程师即将出现的问题模型还可以将实时运营数据与历史数据进行比较，以预测问题何时发生。

机器学习在提高制造过程质量方面发挥着关键作用。 机器学习算法可以帮助提高装配设备的可用性、性能、质量和漏洞。 在当今期限紧迫和产品复杂性日益增加的时代，满足最优质的指导方针和要求变得更加困难。 客户需要完美无瑕的商品。 因此，严格监控和保持质量控制极为重要。 此外，产品故障可能导致召回，从而严重损害公司的品牌和形象。 公司可能会使用人工智能来检测制造过程中可能导致质量问题的故障。 这些缺陷可能是严重的或轻微的，但它们都会对整体性能产生影响，并且可以及早消除。

通过利用机器学习来改善制造车间，可以在整个供应链中部署 ML，以产生实际的业务优势，例如提高运营效率和削减成本。 通过使用机器学习来预测需求和供应并改进生产计划，可以大大减少库存水平和浪费。

当代公司的供应链需要人工智能 (AI) 和物联网 (IoT) 的有效集成才能发挥最佳功能。

随着供应网络变得更加全球化，供应网络也变得更加复杂。 天气或燃料成本的单一变化可能会在任何组织中反弹，对底线产生重大影响。 机器学习可以检查影响供应链的每个变量，改变整个操作并实施所需的调整。 简单地说，机器学习算法可能会分析数十个甚至数百个变量，然后才能为公司做出最佳决策。

基于尺寸、材料、成本等多个参数，人工智能和机器学习可以为任何问题或商品生成几乎无穷无尽的设计解决方案。 工程师可以在产品投入生产之前使用这些信息来发现产品的最佳设计解决方案。 通过采用先进的机器学习解决方案来了解事物的使用方式、操作方式以及导致它们失败的原因，ML 还可以改进新产品设计和产品质量。 人工智能如何改善制造业？

在工业业务中利用人工智能时，可能性是无穷无尽的。 尽管该技术产生了重大影响，但仍有许多未使用的潜力可以帮助制造商改善其运营的各个方面。 人工智能公司使所有类型的制造商能够更高效、更有效地开展工作。

人工智能可以帮助制造商以多种方式赚钱，从预测和预测需求到开发智能互联设备，以产生新的和创造性的商业模式。

人工智能不仅可以增加收入，还可以大大降低成本。 制造商可以使用它来预测维护需求，提高制造和供应链效率，并评估服务趋势，以改进产品设计并降低保修成本。

另一个不应忘记的优势是风险最小化，它具有巨大的影响力。 制造商可以使用人工智能及早发现产品缺陷并加强质量控制程序，以减少责任、控制生产风险、在问题蔓延之前解决客户服务问题，甚至通过预测某些行为造成伤害的可能性来降低安全风险。‍

机器学习在制造业中的优势

制造业中的机器学习有助于开发新的高效制造工艺。 机器学习技术正在彻底改变整个制造业和制造过程。 在制造业中使用机器学习的主要好处的简短列表如下：

  • 由于工艺改进，显着减少损失增加了利润率。
  • 由于预测性维护而节省成本
  • 由于智能制造，消费者驱动的产品开发可能产生收入。
  • 通过优化各种生产工艺提高生产能力。
  • 能够通过流程简化和优化来扩大产品线。
  • 通过预测剩余使用寿命 (RUL) 延长机器和设备的使用寿命
  • 借助实时监控和交付路线优化，更好地管理供应链
  • 通过优化的质量控制提高产品质量。

制造业的未来将由机器学习和人工智能塑造。

总体而言，在制造业中使用机器学习可以改进流程、减少开销和提高生产力。 它还使制造商能够做出快速判断并提高其客户服务的质量。

在制造过程的各个阶段实施这些技术有助于发现开发更高效工艺、消除浪费和节省资金的新潜力。 这些技术将不断进步，它们将成为面对市场变化的重要工具。 制造必须转变为在人力资源、时间和材料方面尽可能高效的过程。 在不久的将来，不在制造过程中使用人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习 (ML) 的公司将失去竞争优势。

本文最初发表于https://techreviewer.co/blog/machine-learning-in-manufacturing



原文:

Machine learning (ML) is a subfield of artificial intelligence (AI) that studies and understands patterns, relations, and themes in data to support learning, processing, and decision-making without human input. In simplified terms, machine learning is the process of making computers and machines “intelligent” enough to develop their understanding and enable them to make decisions and predictions like any human would.

This process of making a machine capable of human-like learning is achieved by submitting massive quantities of data to the machine learning algorithms, which then analyze and make data-driven and data-centric suggestions based only on the supplied data. Due to several causes, the use of machine learning techniques has proliferated during the last two decades as well as the improved accessibility and capability of existing machine learning technologies. Today, machine learning is widely used in several aspects of production, such as management, optimization, monitoring, control, and diagnostics.

According to a survey, 92% of the top players of the business industry are already investing in machine learning, automation, and artificial intelligence. Process productivity and efficiency have improved by 55% in the past few years by implementing machine learning.

Global machine learning in manufacturing is expected to grow to $16 billion by 2025 from $1 billion in 2018, according to a Global Market Insights analysis. These statistics are a clear indication of how important machine learning in manufacturing can be to increase profits, cut costs, improve product quality, reduce production time, and enhance process efficiency. ‍

How does machine learning help in the manufacturing industry?

Given the challenges of a rapidly evolving, unpredictable industrial setting, ML, as part of AI, can learn and adapt to changes, so that the owners, managers, designers, and software engineers do not have to predict and give answers for all potential circumstances that might arise throughout the entire production process. As a result, given the hardships of most first-principle modelling techniques to deal with adaptability, ML provides a compelling case for its application in manufacturing. A significant characteristic of machine learning is its ability to effectively train from and adjust to different environments.

Techreviewer can help you get proposals from top agencies for FREE

Only after data is examined and transformed into usable information does it become helpful. This is especially true in manufacturing, given the difficulty of acquiring real-time data during a live production program while balancing technological, economical, and knowledge constraints. Using machine learning in manufacturing can lead to the extraction of patterns from current data sets, which can be used to build estimations about the system's future behavior. This additional data (knowledge) might help process owners make better decisions or can be used routinely to enhance the service.

In various industries, ML techniques have been successfully used in performance improvement, tracking and management applications in manufacturing, and predictive maintenance. Machine Learning models offer good potential for improved quality control optimization in manufacturing systems, particularly in complex manufacturing environments where identifying the trouble spots is tricky. In some NP-hard manufacturing challenges, Machine Learning developers can help mitigate cycle time and wastage, as well as increase resource usage. In addition, machine learning provides strong tools for continual quality improvement in big and complicated manufacturing processes.‍

Where is machine learning used in industry?

Manufacturing is one of the most significant businesses in the world's economy as it generates a worldwide output of a whopping $13.9 trillion. One of the most important production objectives is to manufacture more high-quality items at the lowest possible cost. However, for firms that lack the necessary resources and skills to create and manufacture high-quality items, producing products may be an extremely costly and demanding procedure. However, the face of the manufacturing industry has altered considerably during the last few years due to the use of machine learning in manufacturing. The below-mentioned list highlights the most prevalent areas where machine learning in manufacturing is being materialized.

When compared to conventional organizations, manufacturers which use machine learning technologies are better positioned to move quicker. With the potential of connection, sophisticated analytics, automation, and more, everything from manufacturing efficiency to product personalization can be altered. Manufacturers may enhance speed to market, improve service efficacy, and build a new business model for increased productivity by combining these technologies.‍

1. Intelligent data processing through data analytics

Data has become a very precious asset, and collecting and processing it is now more affordable than ever.

With the help of Artificial Intelligence specifically Machine Learning — manufacturers are harnessing that data to drastically boost their profit margins. Machine learning may help manufacturers exploit their key production data in a variety of ways. This data can be any sort of data. It may be consumption data, shipping data, historical data, operational data, or inventory data. Machine learning algorithms excel in deriving useful insights from structured data. With the use of data analytics in manufacturing, one can create a calm environment at the workplace since it takes care of everything by evaluating every component of the production process, providing data, and modifying each stage of the process.‍

2. Pattern Detection and predictions.

Manufacturers may use important data to build a product with higher consumer value and reduce the risks associated with introducing a new product to the market. When planning, strategizing and modelling the product, actionable insights are considered, which aids in the judgment call.

ML can also be used to forecast energy consumption, consumer demand, and maintenance needs. Machine learning models offer a degree of intricacy to consumer forecasting. These models will include historical and current data from a variety of sources, like ERP systems, point-of-sale systems, and social media marketing campaigns. This information may be paired with other relevant variables such as raw material pricing, supplier conflicts, and weather interruptions to determine a product's market relevancy. Machine learning solutions, unlike traditional forecasting systems, will be continually updated based on fresh data.

These algorithms can arrange the best time to undertake energy-intensive operations for optimum cost reductions by taking into account energy pricing, employee salaries, equipment maintenance, and storage minimization.‍

3. Fault and Error Detection

According to Forbes, using machine learning to automate quality testing may raise detection rates by up to 90%. For product inspection and fault detection, machine learning is being used. Manufacturers may gain a competitive edge by decreasing equipment downtimes by diagnosing production equipment process issues and potential threats quickly and accurately. Machine learning algorithms are increasingly being used in the manufacturing industry to enable problem detection of production systems as more consumers demand manufacturers to offer high-quality goods quickly and at a reasonable cost.‍

4. Manufacturing process optimization and monitoring.

Production personnel's efforts to identify the ideal set of requirements associated with a specific manufacturing process may be augmented by the prescriptive analytics capabilities of ML approaches, allowing the improvement and optimization of manufacturing processes. In the coming years, the field at the confluence of machine learning in manufacturing and process optimization that results in industrial analytics discoveries that enable quicker mass and personalized production with minimal wastage will develop manifold.‍

5. Predictive Maintenance

Maintenance of equipment is one of the largest expenditures in the manufacturing business, costing plants and factories about $60 billion in unexpected downtime, 42 percent of which was due to asset failure. As a result, predictive maintenance has emerged as a critical option that may assist save a significant amount of money. Complex ML models are producing reliable forecasts about the condition of resources and machines. The equipment's Remaining Useful Life (RUL) extends dramatically with the help of predictive maintenance. If something has to be fixed or changed, experts will know ahead of time and would even know which procedures to employ.

Machine learning algorithms may use data from sensors implanted in machinery and equipment to generate a predictive maintenance model that eliminates guessing from maintenance operations. Service requests can be initiated automatically using machine learning to inform personnel and engineers of impending problems Models can also compare real-time operational data with historical data to forecast when the problems will occur.‍

6. Quality Control and its optimization.

Machine Learning plays a critical role in improving the manufacturing process quality. Machine learning algorithms can aid in the availability, performance, quality, and vulnerabilities of assembly equipment. In today's day and age of tight deadlines and increasing product complexities, meeting the finest quality guidelines and requirements becomes even more difficult. Customers demand flawless goods. Thus, it is extremely important to strictly monitor and maintain quality control. Furthermore, product faults might result in recalls, which can severely harm the company's brand and its image. Companies may use AI to detect faults in the manufacturing process that could lead to quality difficulties. These defects might be severe or minor, but they all have an impact on total performance and can be removed early on.

7. Supply chain management and inventory management.

By utilizing machine learning to improve the manufacturing floor, ML can be deployed across the supply chain to produce actual business advantages such as improving operating efficiency and cutting costs. Inventory levels and wastage can be reduced drastically by using machine learning to predict demand and supply and improve production schedules.

ML also helps companies maximize their value by enhancing their logistical solutions, such as capital management, supply chain management, and inventory management procedures. Effective integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) is required for a contemporary company's supply chain to function at its best.

Supply networks have gotten more complicated as they have become more global. A single change in weather or fuel costs may ricochet throughout any organization, having a significant influence on the bottom line. Machine learning can examine each variable that affects the supply chain and alter the whole operation and implement the required adjustments. Simply said, a machine learning algorithm may analyze dozens—or even hundreds—of variables before making the optimal decision for a company.

8. Smart manufacturing

AI and machine learning can generate an almost endless number of design solutions for any issue or commodity, based on several parameters such as dimensions, materials, cost, and so forth. Engineers may use this information to discover the best design solution for a product before it has been put into production. By employing advanced Machine Learning solutions to learn how things are used, how they operate, and what causes them to fail, ML also enables the improvement of new product design and product quality. ML may also be used to forecast the performance of suggested design improvements, which can be communicated back to design teams.

How AI is improving manufacturing?

When it comes to harnessing AI in the industrial business, the possibilities are endless. While the technology has had a significant influence, there is still a lot of unused potential that may help manufacturers improve every aspect of their operations. Artificial intelligence companies enable all types of manufacturers to perform more efficiently and effectively.

AI can help manufacturers make money in a variety of ways, from anticipating and forecasting demand to developing intelligent, connected devices that produce new and inventive business models.

AI has the potential to not only increase income but also substantially reduce costs. Manufacturers can use it to anticipate maintenance needs, enhance manufacturing and supply chain efficiency, and evaluate service trends to improve product design and reduce warranty costs.

Another advantage that should not be forgotten is risk minimization, which has a huge influence. Manufacturers may use AI to discover product flaws early and enhance quality control procedures to decrease liability, control production risks, solve customer service concerns before they spread, and even reduce safety risks by forecasting the possibility of harm from certain actions.‍

Benefits of ML in manufacturing

Machine learning in manufacturing helps in the development of new and efficient manufacturing processes. Machine learning technologies are revamping the entire manufacturing industry and the manufacturing process completely. A very brief list of the major benefits of employing machine learning in manufacturing are as follows:

  • Significant loss reductions increased profit margins due to process improvements.
  • Cost savings due to predictive maintenance
  • Revenue generation due to consumer-driven product development possible due to Smart manufacturing.
  • Increase in production capacity through optimizing various production processes.
  • Ability to grow product lines via process simplification and optimizaton.
  • Extended life of machinery and equipment by predicting Remaining Useful Life(RUL)
  • Better Supply chain management with the help of real-time monitoring and delivery route optimizations
  • Better product quality with optimized quality control.

The manufacturing industry's future will be shaped by machine learning and artificial intelligence.

Overall, using machine learning in manufacturing provides process improvement, reduced overhead, and great productivity. It also enables manufacturers to make rapid judgments and increase the quality of their client service.

The implementation of these technologies at various stages of the manufacturing process aids in the discovery of the new potential for developing more efficient processes, eliminating waste, and saving money. These technologies will continue to advance, and they will become an important tool in the face of market shifts. Manufacturing must be transformed into a process that is as efficient as feasible in terms of human resources, time, and materials. In the close future, companies that do not use Artificial Intelligence(AI) and Machine Learning(ML) in their manufacturing processes will lose their competitive edge.

Items.Title: 突袭数据恢复迪拜 | Raid data recovery dubai
Items.Description:
为了从 RAID 磁盘和存储组件中恢复数据，它采用了自动和手动数据恢复过程的组合。 硬件和软件 RAID 均可用于迪拜的 RAID 恢复数据。 可以执行每个 RAID 级别的数据恢复，尤其是 RAID 0、2、3、4、5、6、7 和 10 级别。 软件损坏和硬盘故障等技术问题是数据丢失的最常见原因。 迪拜 RAID 数据恢复通常需要将所有 RAID 计算机的硬盘驱动器恢复为其原始设置和配置，以较新的为准。 为了正确恢复数据，我们熟练的专业人员了解设备、软件和固件级别的 RAID 配置。 RAID recovery requires that the appropriate RAID array be identified. RAID 恢复需要识别适当的 RAID 阵列。



原文:

RAID Data Recoveryrefers to the recovery and restoration of data from a RAID storage architecture or technology. In order to recover data from RAID disks and storage components, it employs a mix of automated and manual data recovery procedures. Both hardware and software RAID can be used for RAID Recovering Data in Dubai. The RAID storage design has a unique and sophisticated manner of storing and removing data, making this sort of data recovery distinct from typical data recovery methods. Data recovery for every RAID level can be performed, especially RAID 0, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10 levels. Technical issues such as corrupted software and a malfunctioning hard disk are the most common causes of data loss. Dubai RAID Data Recovery typically entails restoring all of the RAID computer’s hard drives to their original settings and configuration, whichever is more recent. In order to properly recover data, our skilled professionals have an understanding of RAID configuration at the device, software, and firmware level. RAID recovery requires that the appropriate RAID array be identified.

Items.Title: 在 Twitter 上检测趋势 | Detecting Trends on Twitter
Items.Description:
在当今社会，大多数人每天都通过社交媒体进行交流，从普通人到政客和企业。 如果战略性地使用，社交媒体论坛可用于接触世界各地的广泛人群。

此类社交媒体的一个例子是 Twitter，它是一个广泛使用的平台，在全球拥有 3.1 亿月活跃用户的庞大用户群。 当某个话题或事件在 Twitter 上成为热门话题时，它会吸引每月 10 亿独立用户的国际人群。

然后其他用户可以喜欢和转发他们的推文，这意味着他们与他们的追随者分享其他人的推文。 用户可以互相关注，不需要互相关注。 这允许某些用户拥有数百万的追随者，而其他人只有十几个，如果有的话。 为了使用户能够找到与特定主题相关的推文，使用了以“#”开头的短字符序列。 这些字符序列称为主题标签，允许用户参与和查看所有相关的推文。

这些趋势包括#FIFAWWC（FIFA 世界杯）和#ParisAttacks 以及#TheDress1。 当一个主题标签在 Twitter 上成为流行趋势时，它发生得很快，巴黎袭击事件就是一个例子，说明以前不存在的主题标签的活动是如何突然爆发的。 但是，一组主题标签的行为可能不同，但仍然都被归类为趋势。

在 Twitter 上发现和检测趋势不仅对希望了解最新趋势的公司或个人有用，而且对 Twitter 本身也很有用。 然后，Twitter 就有可能战略性地使用趋势标签来放置其广告，以便接触到大量人群。

以前的工作已经在 Twitter 提要中的趋势检测领域完成。 在 Twitter 的算法能够做到这一点之前，他开发了一种算法来发现 Twitter 数据源中的趋势。 研究小组能够
通过尝试不同的机器学习方法来做到这一点，并得出一种算法，该算法使用标签的频率来预测即将到来的趋势。

机器学习是计算机科学中一个相对较新的领域，它使用数据驱动的学习来训练程序，而无需对它们进行真正的编程。 机器学习中的方法用于图像识别和自动驾驶汽车等[13]。 在检测趋势的背景下，机器学习算法被训练来查看现有的 Twitter 数据，然后能够在新数据中找到趋势。

在机器学习中，有两类方法。 第一个是监督学习，通过提供一组输入数据和预期结果来训练算法。 第二个是无监督学习
接受输入数据的训练集，但没有预期结果的数据。 这使得无监督学习有所不同，因为算法必须在给定数据中找到结构和模式，而不知道输出应该是什么。

无监督学习方法在其他情况下也很有帮助，例如无监督预训练。 监督训练方法旨在找到全局最小值（以最小的误差拟合问题），但全局最小值
不保证每次都能到达。 无监督预训练可以帮助初始化监督方法，这样当它开始时，它更容易达到全局最小值。



原文:

In today’s society most people communicate through social media every day, ranging from ordinary people to politicians and corporations. Social media forums can be utilized to reach extensive crowds around the world if used strategically.

An example of such a social media is Twitter, which is a widely used platform with a large worldwide user base of 310 million monthly active users. When a topic or an event becomes trending on Twitter, it reaches out to an international crowd of 1 billion unique people per month.

Twitter is a social media platform where users post short messages. Other users can then like and retweet their tweets, which means that they share the other person’s tweet with their followers. Users can follow each other and no mutual following is required. This permits certain users to have millions of followers, whereas other people only have a dozen, if any at all. To enable users to find tweets related to a certain subject, a short sequence of characters starting with a “#” is used. These sequence of characters are called hashtags and allow users to participate with and view all related tweets.

In 2015, the trends ranged from world wide events and tragedies to social phenomenons[21]. These trends included #FIFAWWC (FIFA World Cup) and #ParisAttacks as well as #TheDress1. When a hashtag becomes trending on Twitter it happens fast, the Paris attacks is an example of how a previously nonexistent hashtag’s activity suddenly goes through the roof. However, a group of hashtags can behave differently and still all be classified as trends.

Finding and detecting trends on Twitter is not only useful for companies or people that wish to pick up on the latest trends, but also to Twitter themselves as a company. Twitter makes approximately 85% of its revenue based on selling advertisements. Twitter has developed an algorithm that finds trending hashtags, both global and local trends. Then it is possible for Twitter to strategically use the trending hashtags to place its advertisements such that it reaches out to a large crowd.

Previous work has been done in the area of trend detection in Twitter’s feed. In 2012, Nikolov et al. developed an algorithm that found trends in the Twitter data feed, before Twitter’s algorithms was able to do so. The research team was able to
do this by experimenting with different machine learning methods and arrive at an algorithm that used the frequencies of hashtags to predict an upcoming trend.

Machine learning is a relatively new area within computer science that uses data-driven learning to train programs without literally programming them to do so. Methods within machine learning are used for image recognition and selfdriving cars among others[13]. In the context of detecting trends, machine learning algorithms are trained to look at existing Twitter data and then be able to find trends in new data.

Within machine learning, there are two categories of methods. The first one is supervised learning that trains the algorithm by providing a training set of input data and the expected outcome. The second one is unsupervised learning that
takes in a training set of input data, but no data of the expected outcome. This makes unsupervised learning different, as the algorithm has to find a structure and patterns in the given data without knowing what the output is supposed to be.

Unsupervised learning methods can also be helpful in other contexts, such as unsupervised pre-training. Supervised training methods aim to find a global minimum (fitting the problem with the smallest error), but the global minimum
is not guaranteed to be reached every time. Unsupervised pre training can help initialize the supervised method such that when it begins, it is more prone to reach the global minimum.

Items.Title: OAuth 弹出窗口 ~ 实用指南 | OAuth popup ~ Practical Guide
Items.Description:
在上一篇文章中，我们从头开始实现了 GitHub OAuth 流程。 但是，重定向到身份验证页面是通过硬重定向进行的，这绝对不是想要的行为，尤其是在处理 SPA 时。

毫不奇怪，野外的 OAuth使用弹出方法。 很容易解决，重定向仍然会发生，但是在一个生成的弹出窗口中，在解决之前会将access_token传递回主选项卡。

这篇文章将被视为对上一篇文章的增强，因此它的节奏略高。 如有疑问，请参考随附的代码或上一篇文章。

 <a id= "oauth-github-popup" > Authenticate via GitHub (popup) </a> <script> const CLIENT_ID = " Iv1.395930440f268143 " ; const url = new URL ( " /login/oauth/authorize " , " https://github.com " ); url . searchParams . set ( " client_id " , CLIENT_ID ); const cta = document . getElementById ( " oauth-github-popup " ); cta . setAttribute ( " href " , url ); cta . addEventListener ( " click " , ( event ) => { event . preventDefault (); // ...

我们组装了 GitHub OAuth 授权链接，并将其附加到<a>标记以用于可访问性原因。 然而，我们也监听点击它，防止默认的硬重定向。

接下来，我们将使用 Web API window.open来生成弹出窗口。
它期望作为第三个参数的字符串包含width 、 height等。

就个人而言，我更喜欢对象的显式性，然后将其转换为上述字符串。

基本上，如果两者都是auto的，即使您更改width或height ，弹出窗口也将始终在中心产生。

// in the 'click' eventListener callback const features = { popup : " yes " , width : 600 , height : 700 , top : " auto " , left : " auto " , toolbar : " no " , menubar : " no " , }; const strWindowsFeatures = Object . entries ( features ) . reduce (( str , [ key , value ]) => { if ( value == " auto " ) { if ( key === " top " ) { const v = Math . round ( window . innerHeight / 2 - features . height / 2 ); str += `top= ${ v } ,` ; } else if ( key === " left " ) { const v = Math . round ( window . innerWidth / 2 - features . width / 2 ); str += `left= ${ v } ,` ; } return str ; } str += ` ${ key } = ${ value } ,` ; return str ; }, "" ) . slice ( 0 , - 1 ); // remove last ',' (comma) window . open ( url , " _blank " , strWindowsFeatures );

因此，用户单击您喜欢的 GitHub 按钮并通过弹出窗口进行身份验证。 但重要的是指示服务器发送回某个页面，其功能是：

  1. 确保它是一个弹出窗口
  2. 从查询参数推断access_token
  3. 将其发送到父窗口（ window.opener ）
  4. 关闭自己

记住：OAuth 回调
用户通过身份验证后，GitHub 将重定向到在 OAuth 应用程序/GitHub 应用程序创建时指定的回调 URL 。 这在 上一篇文章中有更详细的说明。

 server . get ( " /oauth/github/login/callback " , async ( request , reply ) => { const { code } = request . query ; const exchangeURL = new URL ( " login/oauth/access_token " , " https://github.com " ); exchangeURL . searchParams . set ( " client_id " , process . env . CLIENT_ID ); exchangeURL . searchParams . set ( " client_secret " , process . env . CLIENT_SECRET ); exchangeURL . searchParams . set ( " code " , code ); const response = await axios . post ( exchangeURL . toString (), null , { headers : { Accept : " application/json " , }, }); const { access_token } = response . data ; const redirectionURL = new URL ( " popup " , " http://localhost:3000 " ); redirectionURL . searchParams . set ( " access_token " , access_token ); reply . status ( 302 ). header ( " Location " , redirectionURL ). send (); }); server . get ( " /popup " , ( request , reply ) => { return reply . sendFile ( " popup.html " ); });

客户端被重定向到/popup并显示popup.html 。

 <script> if ( window . opener == null ) window . location = " / " ; const access_token = new URL ( window . location ). searchParams . get ( " access_token " ); window . opener . postMessage ( access_token ); window . close (); </script>

如果页面尚未通过window.open打开，则window.opener为null 。 这样，如果用户直接路由到/popup ，就会被重定向到/ 。

计算量很小，应该很快。 展示一个微调器只会对你有好处。

提示
好吧，您可以检查那里的window.opener值，以提供更好的体验。

快完成了！ 弹出窗口将access_token冲回父级，但它没有在听！

 window . addEventListener ( " message " , receiveMessage , false ); function receiveMessage ( event ) { if ( event . origin !== window . location . origin ) { console . warn ( `Message received by ${ event . origin } ; IGNORED.` ); return ; } const access_token = event . data ; }

作为预防措施，请忽略来自其他来源的任何消息。 请在您的代码中考虑removeEventListener 。
将access_token保存在某处。 从这一点开始，流程重新加入上一篇文章。

没有什么能阻止您使用此模式安装 GitHub 应用程序和更改权限。

相关文章

联系人



原文:

In a previous post we have implemented the GitHub OAuth flow from scratch. However, the redirection to the authentication page was via hard redirection, which is definitely not wanted behavior especially when you're dealing with a SPA.

Not surprisingly OAuth in the wild use a popup approach. Easy to solve, the redirection will still happen but in a spawned popup that, before dissolving will pass the access_token back to main tab.

This post is to be read as an enhancement of the previous one, so it has a slightly higher pacing. If in doubt, please refer to the accompanying code or the previous post.

<a id="oauth-github-popup">Authenticate via GitHub (popup)</a> <script> const CLIENT_ID = "Iv1.395930440f268143"; const url = new URL("/login/oauth/authorize", "https://github.com"); url.searchParams.set("client_id", CLIENT_ID); const cta = document.getElementById("oauth-github-popup"); cta.setAttribute("href", url); cta.addEventListener("click", (event) => { event.preventDefault(); // ...

we assemble the GitHub OAuth authorization link and attacch it to the <a> tag for accessibility reason. However, we also listen for click on it, preventing the default hard redirection.

Next we are going to use the Web API window.open to spawn the popup.
It expect as third parameter a string containing width, height and more.

Personally, I prefer the explicity of an object that is then converted to the above mentioned string.

top and left properties have the auto value - that's not in the specifications of the API, in fact it is interpreted by the following snippet as an instruction to place in the center of the relative axis. Basically if both are auto the popup will always spawn in the center, even if you change width or height.

// in the 'click' eventListener callback
const features = { popup: "yes", width: 600, height: 700, top: "auto", left: "auto", toolbar: "no", menubar: "no",
}; const strWindowsFeatures = Object.entries(features) .reduce((str, [key, value]) => { if (value == "auto") { if (key === "top") { const v = Math.round( window.innerHeight / 2 - features.height / 2 ); str += `top=${v},`; } else if (key === "left") { const v = Math.round( window.innerWidth / 2 - features.width / 2 ); str += `left=${v},`; } return str; } str += `${key}=${value},`; return str; }, "") .slice(0, -1); // remove last ',' (comma) window.open(url, "_blank", strWindowsFeatures);

Thus, the user clicks your fancy GitHub button and authenticate via the popup. But it is important to instruct the server to send back to some page whose function is:

  1. Ensure it is a popup
  2. Extrapolate access_token from query params
  3. Dispatch it to parent window (window.opener)
  4. Close itself

Remember: OAuth callback
Once the user gets authenticated, GitHub redirects to the Callback URL specified at OAuth App/GitHub App creation. This is addressed in more detail in the previous post.

server.get("/oauth/github/login/callback", async (request, reply) => { const { code } = request.query; const exchangeURL = new URL("login/oauth/access_token", "https://github.com"); exchangeURL.searchParams.set("client_id", process.env.CLIENT_ID); exchangeURL.searchParams.set("client_secret", process.env.CLIENT_SECRET); exchangeURL.searchParams.set("code", code); const response = await axios.post(exchangeURL.toString(), null, { headers: { Accept: "application/json", }, }); const { access_token } = response.data; const redirectionURL = new URL("popup", "http://localhost:3000"); redirectionURL.searchParams.set("access_token", access_token); reply.status(302).header("Location", redirectionURL).send();
}); server.get("/popup", (request, reply) => { return reply.sendFile("popup.html");
});

The client gets redirected to /popup and popup.html is shown.

<script> if (window.opener == null) window.location = "/"; const access_token = new URL(window.location).searchParams.get( "access_token" ); window.opener.postMessage(access_token); window.close();
</script>

window.opener is null if the page has not been opened via the window.open. This way, if the user directly routes to /popup, gets redirected to /.

The computation is minimal, it should be pretty fast. Showing a spinner though can only do you good.

Tip
SPA and their router solutions offers some beforeEnter method that can be associated with a route; well, you could check the window.opener value in there, in order to provide an even better experience.

Almost done! The popup is dashing the access_token back to the parent but it is not listening!

window.addEventListener("message", receiveMessage, false); function receiveMessage(event) { if (event.origin !== window.location.origin) { console.warn(`Message received by ${event.origin}; IGNORED.`); return; } const access_token = event.data;
}

As a precaution ignore any message coming from another origin. Please, consider removeEventListener in your code.
Save the access_token somewhere. From this point the flow rejoins the previous post.

There's nothing stopping you from using this pattern for GitHub App installation and permissions changes as well.

Related Posts

Contacts
