Items.Title: 轻松更改列表项上的图标 | Easily changing the icon on list items
Items.Description:
快速说明一下，要更改列表项的样式并添加酷图标（例如表情符号），请执行以下操作：

.foo { list-style: none; //removes old icons

然后用:before添加一个新图标

.foo li::before { content: '🏷 ';

} }



原文:

Just a quick note, to change the way list items are styled and add cool icons (like emoji for example) do this:

.foo { list-style: none; //removes old icons

}

then add a new icon with :before

.foo li::before { content: '🏷 ';

}
Items.Author: aretecore

Items.Title: 从 OpenAPI 规范生成文档 | Generating docs from OpenAPI Spec
Items.Description:
它从一个非常简单的设置开始。 两个 github 存储库：一个用于我们的后端，其中包括我们后端 API 的 OpenAPI 规范。 第二个是我们的文档网站，我们基于 Facebook 的Docusaurus 。

我为 docs 网站选择 Docusaurus 是因为大约 500 位我最亲密的朋友推荐了它。 这是一个不错的选择——我可以使用 Markdown 编写文档而无需担心其他任何事情，Docusaurus 神奇地将它们变成了一个漂亮的网站。

直到我们想将生成的 API 文档引入我们的文档。 我们希望生成的文档与文档站点的其余部分集成。 它应该真的是一次单一的体验。

第一次迭代涉及向 docs 存储库添加一个小型构建脚本，该脚本克隆了后端存储库并在每个文件上使用swagger-markdown来生成降价。 我对生成的文档不太满意，但它工作了一段时间，我们就不管它了。 直到星期一，当我们合并了一个导致我们触发swagger-markdown 中的错误的更改时。 该问题是在 6 个多月前报告的，但没有任何回复。 这引发了一些关于它是否是一个值得依赖的好项目的担忧——很少有活动，很少有分叉和星星……看起来不太好。 所以我到处逛了逛。 这是我尝试过的，有效的和无效的：

维德辛斯👎

这个项目看起来棒极了。 大量很酷的功能、自定义和大量 github 明星。 但是...我遇到了这个错误。 该错误已在大约 2 年前修复，但该项目从那时起就没有发布过一个版本。 我本可以想出一个不需要发布的脚本，但是……这个项目显然只是一个两年内没有时间发布的一个人。 我也感觉不好。

这看起来很正式。 文档不是很好。 当我尝试它时，它在我的工作主管中留下了很多“垃圾”。 但真正的问题是它为每个输入规范生成了一个完整的降价目录结构——将它与 Docusaurus 主站点联系起来似乎真的很痛苦。 我本来可以让它工作的——但是最小的文档和凌乱的体验让我环顾四周。

赤道龙✅

这个项目安装简单，使用简单，并且是为我的用例而构建的。 它没有很多 github 明星，但它包裹了非常流行的 redoc。 最重要的是 - 作者是积极的，反应灵敏的和善良的。 只需检查问题 - 他会遇到您想与之合作的人。 为了锦上添花 - 这个网站背后的引擎Forem使用了这个插件。 多么酷啊？

我已经准备好使用 Redocusaurus，但是有一个问题：

我们的规范分为几个 YAML 规范文件。 当我们这样做时，这看起来是个好主意——处理大文件并不好玩。 问题是 OpenAPI 生态系统中很少有为多个文件构建的。 我强烈建议您避免痛苦并采用单文件路线。 如果Stripe 可以有 4.5MB 的规范文件，我们也可以。

为了使用 Redocusaurus，我需要一个规范文件。 问题是其中一个需要“根规范文件”来驱动合并，而另一个需要额外信息来解决冲突。 我的规格都没有。

我最终得到了一个老式的解决方案——现在手动合并规范文件。 我们的工程团队正在考虑如何改进我们对 OpenAPI 规范的使用，因此我们将在一两周内找到更好的解决方案。

我希望这个博客可以帮助有类似需求的人，或者只是试图在 3 个都做类似事情的 JS 项目之间进行选择。 维护者的响应能力非常重要。



原文:

It started with a very simple setup. Two github repositories: One for our backend, which included OpenAPI specs of our backend APIs. The second for our documentation website, which we based on Facebook's Docusaurus.

I chose Docusaurus for the docs website because about 500 of my closest friends recommended it. And it was a great choice - I could write docs using Markdown and not worry about anything else, Docusaurus magically turned them into a great looking website.

Until the point where we wanted to introduce generated API documentation to our docs. We wanted the generated docs to be integrated with the rest of the docs site. It should really feel like a single experience.

The first iteration involved adding a small build script to the docs repo that cloned the backend repo and used swagger-markdown on each file to generate markdown. Docusaurus found the markdown files and did the rest.

I wasn't super happy with the generated docs, but it worked for a while and we left it alone. Until Monday, when we merged a change that caused us to trigger a bug in swagger-markdown. The issue was reported more than 6 month back and has no responses. Which raised some concerns about whether it is a good project to depend on - very little activity, very few forks and stars... it didn't look good. 👎

So I did some shopping around. Here's what I tried, what worked and what didn't:

Widdershins 👎

This project looks fantastic. Tons of cool features, customizations and lots of github stars. But... I ran into this bug. The bug was fixed almost 2 years ago, but the project didn't have a single release since. I could have probably figured out a script that didn't require a release, but... this project is clearly just a single person who doesn't have time to do a release in two years. I didn't feel good depending on that either.

OpenAPI Generator 👎

This looked very official. The documentation wasn't great. When I tried it, it left a lot of "junk" in my working director. But the real problem was that it generated an entire directory structure of markdown for each input spec - and it seemed really painful to tie this into the main Docusaurus site. I could have probably made it work - but the minimal docs and messy experience got me to look around a bit more.

Redocusaurus ✅

This project was simple to install, simple to use, and was built exactly for my use-case. API docs in Docusaurus with a unified experience for users. It didn't have many github stars, but it wrapped the hugely popular redoc. And most important - the author is active, responsive and kind. Just check out the issues - he comes across as someone you want to work with. To put the icing on the cake - Forem, the engine behind this very website, uses this plugin. How cool is that?

I was all ready to use Redocusaurus, but there was one problem:

Our specs were split between several YAML spec files. It looked like a good idea when we did that - large files are not fun to work with. The problem is that very little in the OpenAPI ecosystem was built for multiple files. I strongly recommend that you will save yourself the pain and go the mono-file route. If Stripe can have a 4.5MB spec file, so can we.

In order to use Redocusaurus, I needed a single spec file. Both Swagger cli and OpenApi cli offered an option to merge separate specs into one. The problem was that one of them required a "root spec file" to drive the merging and the other required extra information to resolve conflicts. My specs had neither.

I ended up with an old fashioned solution - manually merging the spec files for now. Our engineering team has thoughts on how to improve our use of OpenAPI specs, and we'll have a better solution in a week or two as a result of that.

I hope this blog helps someone who has similar requirements or just tries to pick between 3 JS projects who all do similar things. Responsiveness of maintainers is really important.
Items.Author: gwenshap

Items.Title: Codiga 的 Microsoft Visual Studio 代码终极指南 | Codiga’s Ultimate Guide to Microsoft Visual Studio Code
Items.Description:
在开始您的编码生涯时，必须选择正确的工具以取得成功。 集成开发环境(IDE) 对开发人员而言与生来平等一样重要。 选择合适的可能是一个艰难的选择！

为您的任务选择最合适的 IDE 只是一个开始，但是知道哪些工具可以与您选择的环境集成，这将使您的工作效率更上一层楼。 在 Codiga，我们的使命是通过我们的插件确保我们所有的智能开发人员工具都可以在多种环境中轻松使用。 在本文中，我们将向您介绍最常用的 IDE 之一，即Microsoft Visual Studio Code(VS Code) ，并讨论 Codiga 的 Coding Assistant 如何帮助您充分利用该工具以最大限度地提高工作效率。

为什么选择 VS 代码？

正如我们所说，并非所有 IDE 都是相同的，因此了解每个 IDE 的复杂性对于为您的项目做出正确选择至关重要。 基本上，VS Code 是适用于 Windows、Linux 和 macOS 的源代码编辑器。 它由 Microsoft 开发，可免费用于个人和商业用途，是刚开始软件开发职业的人的理想选择。

需要注意的一点是，Visual Studio Code 是一种轻量级工具，可在大多数硬件和软件配置上运行。 与 Apple iOS 更新一样，VS Code 每月都会发布一个包含新功能和错误修复的新版本。 他们的软件的最新版本一旦可用就会安装，但请注意，如果您希望等待以自己的节奏进行更新，您可以选择禁用这些自动更新。

入门

首先，下载适用于Windows 、 macOS或Linux的 VS Code。 该工具体积小，因此下载速度应该相当快。

接下来，双击下载的文件，选择运行，然后按照向导进行操作。

安装时，您可以选择安装其他扩展和工具。 一些常用的组件是用于将TypeScript转换为 JavaScript 的 TypeScript、用于与GitHub集成的 Git 以及用于运行 JavaScript 应用程序的Node.js。

至于代码扩展，您可以从市场上的数百个中进行选择，包括 Codiga 插件（但我们将在本文稍后再讨论）。

在开始将其他工具与 VS Code 集成之前，您可以通过多种方式自定义现有功能，使其更加个性化以满足您的需求或任务。

用户设置会有所不同，因为它们是适用于您从任何地方打开的每个 Visual Studio Code 实例的自定义设置。 或者，工作设置是对特定工作空间的自定义，这些设置仅适用于工作空间所在的特定设备。

此外，更方便的是这些工作区设置特定于项目，并且可以与与您从事相同任务的其他人共享。 要记住的是，工作区设置总是会覆盖用户设置。

查看和自定义您的设置非常简单； 在 Windows 和 Linux 上转到文件 -> 首选项 -> 设置，在 macOS 上转到代码 -> 首选项 -> 设置。 在左侧，您将看到常用首选项列表，您可以单击这些首选项进行进一步自定义。 请注意，您也可以更改特定于语言的设置。

显着特点

具有直观的键盘快捷键（下面详细介绍）

  • 允许您根据自己的个人喜好自定义编辑器选项。
  • 它是开源的，这意味着您也可以为其开发做出贡献
  • 更多关于所有 VS Codes 功能的信息

    • 捷径

    任何经验丰富的开发人员都会告诉您，捷径对于成为一名高效的编码人员来说绝对是必不可少的。 设置用户和工作设置后，设置键盘快捷键应该是您的下一个优先事项。

    您可以通过 Windows 和 Linux 中的 File -> Preferences -> Keyboard Shortcuts 和 macOS 上的 Code -> Preferences -> Keyboard Shortcuts 访问此键盘编辑器。 尽管您可以随时更改键盘快捷键命令，但我们强烈建议您在设置时立即这样做，以便您从使用此 IDE 开始就尽可能高效。 最后，这是您在使用 VS Code 时需要了解的有关数据隐私的信息。

    默认情况下，Microsoft Visual Studio Code 会收集您的数据以改进其产品。 但要知道，有一种方法可以选择退出而不向 Microsoft 发送更新。 为此，

    为此，请转到文件 -> 首选项 -> 设置并搜索“遥测”。 取消选中“遥测：启用遥测”。 这将阻止 Visual Studio Code 向 Microsoft 发送任何进一步的数据，尽管它不会删除已发送的数据，因此如果您想保持所有数据的私密性，从一开始就更新此设置是必不可少的。

    一旦你在 VS Code 上站稳脚跟并掌握了基础知识，那么从长远来看，集成可以为你节省时间的正确工具应该是最重要的。 从头开始编写所有工作不仅没有必要，而且会浪费宝贵的时间，而这些时间可以更好地用于其他任务。 这就是我们创建Codiga 的编码助手的原因。

    使用代码片段来帮助构建项目是工程中的一种常见做法——为什么其他人已经完成了工作？

    大多数工程师通常在线或从过去的项目中获取这些代码片段，这可能非常耗时并导致代码过时或有缺陷。 可以使用我们的Codiga 插件将编码助手添加到 VS Code 中。 这意味着您无需浏览多个 IDE 即可找到您要查找的内容，这使得该工具更加高效。

    在这里，您可以将它们添加为您喜欢的任何智能代码片段的收藏夹，这意味着当由名称或与代码关联的某些关键字触发时，它们将自动填充到 VS Code 中。 在 Hub 上查找正确的代码片段时，您可以按名称或语言定义搜索指标，或与您要查找的代码相关联的标签。

    添加您自己的智能代码片段以公开、私下或与指定团队共享只需几分钟，并且可以直接在 VS Code 中完成！

    • 突出显示您要共享的代码
    • 右键点击
    • 选择“创建 Codga 代码片段”，您将被要求添加名称、语言、简短描述，最重要的是，将触发填充配方的关键字
    • 然后，您可以选择“公开、私下或与团队共享”，一旦保存，您的代码片段将立即可供您或平台上的任何人使用（如果您选择公开共享）。 请注意，我们希望 Coding Assistant 成为一个互惠互利的环境，因此我们鼓励在可能的情况下公开共享智能代码片段。 请注意，Codiga 绝不会存储您的代码或数据来改进我们自己的产品，因此您可以放心地知道您的工作正在帮助他人进行自己的开发。

    包起来

    无论您选择何种 IDE 开始您的软件开发生涯，都知道 Codiga 的编码助手可以帮助您将工作效率提升到一个新的水平。 有关编码助手和我们的智能代码片段的更多信息可以为您节省宝贵的时间和精力，请单击此处开始使用或在此处与我们在 Discord 上聊天。



    原文:

    When getting started in your coding career, choosing the right tools to be successful is a must. Integrated development environments (IDE) are as important to developers as are created equal. Picking the right one can be a tough choice!

    Picking the most suitable IDE for your task is just the beginning, but knowing what tools to integrate with your chosen environment is what will take your productivity to the next-level. At Codiga, we have made it our mission to ensure that all of our smart developer tools can be easily utilized in multiple environments thanks to our plugins. In this article, we’ll be walking you through one of the most commonly used IDEs, Microsoft Visual Studio Code(VS Code), and discussing how Codiga’s Coding Assistant can help you get the most out of this tool to maximize your productivity .

    Why Chose VS Code?

    As we said, not all IDEs are the same, so understanding the intricacies of each is essential for making the right choice for your project. Basically, VS Code is a source code editor for Windows, Linux, and macOS. Developed by Microsoft, it is free for both personal and commercial use, making it an ideal choice for those getting started in their software development career.

    One thing to note is that Visual Studio Code is a lightweight tool and runs on most hardware and software configurations. Like an Apple iOS update, VS Code releases a new version with new features and bug fixes every month. The latest versions of their software are installed as soon as they become available, but please note that you can select to disable these auto-updates if you’d prefer to wait to conduct the updates at your own pace.

    Getting started

    Getting started with Visual Studio Code is very simple and will depend on your preferred computer program. To begin, download VS Code for Windows, macOS, or Linux. The tool is small in size, so the download should be fairly quick.

    Next, double-click the downloaded file, choose Run, and follow the wizard.

    While installing, you can choose to install additional extensions, and tools. Some commonly-used components are TypeScript for converting TypeScript to JavaScript, Git for integration with GitHub, and Node.js for running JavaScript applications.

    As for code extensions, you can choose from hundreds of them in the marketplace, including the Codiga plugin( but we’ll get back to this a little later in the article).

    Before beginning to integrate other tools with VS Code, there are a ton of ways that you can customize existing features to be more personalized to your needs or task.

    There are two settings in VS Code for customizations — user settings and workspace settings.

    User settings vary as they are customizations that apply to every Visual Studio Code instance you open from anywhere. Alternatively, work settings are customizations of a particular workspace, and these apply only to the specific device where the workspace exists.

    Also, what's even more convenient is that these workspace settings are specific to a project and can be shared with others who work on the same task as you. Something to keep in mind is that workspace settings override user settings always.

    Viewing and customizing your settings is very simple; go to File -> Preferences -> Settings on Windows and Linux and Code ->Preferences -> Settings on macOS. On the left-hand side, you’ll see a list of commonly-used preferences that you can click on for further customization. Note that you can change language-specific settings as well.

    Notable Features

    One of the main reasons why VS Code is such an attractive choice for software developers is because it has a pretty awesome suite of features that is constantly growing. VS Code has a user-friendly interface, so making the most of its features, as well as integrating other tools to enhance this IDE even further, couldn't be easier.

    • VS Code supports hundreds of programming languages by providing syntax highlighting, bracket matching and more for all the supported languages.
    • Has intuitive keyboard shortcuts( more on this in detail below)
    • VS Code has an interactive debugger that allows you to quickly go through your code for quality control, check the variables, execute specific blocks of text, and more.
    • VS Code integrates well with build and scripting tools to automate common tasks.
    • Allows you to customize the editor options based on your own personal preference.
    • It’s open-source, meaning you can also contribute to its development
    • More on all of VS Codes features here

    Shortcuts

    Any seasoned developer will tell you that shortcuts are absolutely essential to being a highly productive coder. Once you have set up the user and work settings, setting keyboard shortcuts should be your next priority.

    Visual Studio Code comes with a keyboard shortcuts editor that shows all the commands and the keyboard shortcuts affiliated with them.

    You can access this keyboard editor through File -> Preferences -> Keyboard Shortcuts in Windows and Linux and Code -> Preferences -> Keyboard Shortcuts on macOS. Though you can make changes to your keyboard shortcuts commands at any time, we highly recommend doing so right at the time of setup so you’re as productive as possible right from the beginning of using this IDE. Lastly, here’s what you need to know about data privacy when using VS Code.

    By default, Microsoft Visual Studio Code collects your data to improve its product. But know that there is a way to opt-out and not send updates to Microsoft. To do so,

    To do this, go to File -> Preferences -> Settings and search for “telemetry.” Uncheck the “Telemetry: Enable Telemetry.” This will stop Visual Studio Code from sending any further data to Microsoft, though it won’t delete the data that was already sent, so if you want to keep all data private, updating this setting from the start is essential.

    Codiga Coding Assistant

    Once you have established yourself on VS Code and have learned the ropes of the basics, integrating the right tools that can save you time in the long run should be top of mind. Coding everything you work on from scratch is not only unnecessary, but it can waste precious time that could be better spent on other tasks. That's why we created Codiga’s Coding Assistant.

    Using snippets of code to help frame a project is a common practice in engineering– why do work that has already been done by another?

    Most engineers typically source these code snippets online or from past projects, which can be time consuming and lead to outdated or defected code. Coding Assistant was created to help developers code ten times faster as it allows you to populate reusable blocks of code in a matter of seconds that have been added by other developers within our network, as well as share your own coding patterns with other users on Codiga.

    Coding Assistant can be added to VS Code using our Codiga plugin. This means you don’t need to navigate around multiple IDEs to find what you are looking for, which makes this tool a lot more efficient.

    Coding Assistant has many Smart Code Snippets that are readily available to use on the Coding Assistant Hub. Here, you can add them as a favorite to any Smart Code Snippets you like, which means they will automatically populate in VS Code when triggered by name or some keywords associated with the code. On Hub when looking for the right code snippet, you can define search metrics by name or language, or tags associated with the code you are looking for.

    Adding your own Smart Code Snippet to be shared publicly, privately or with a designated team takes a matter of minutes, and can be done directly in VS Code!

    • Highlight the code you want to share
    • Right click
    • Select “create Codga Code Snippet” and you’ll be asked to add a name, language, a brief description, and most importantly, the keywords that will trigger a recipe to be populate
    • Then, you can select “share publicly, privately or with a team” and once saved, your code snippet will be available instantly to you, or anyone on the platform if you choose to share publicly. Note, we want Coding Assistant to be a give and take environment, so we encourage public sharing of Smart Code Snippets when possible. Note that Codiga never stores your code or data to improve our own product, so you can feel secure knowing your work is helping others in their own development.

    Wrapping Up

    Whatever IDE you chose to get started in your software development career, know that Codiga’s Coding Assistant can help you take your productivity to the next level. For more on Coding Assistant and our Smart Code Snippets can save you valuable time and energy, click here to get started or chat to us on Discord here.

Items.Title: RPA-in-Python | RPA-in-Python
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我们将介绍一些使用 Python 进行机器人流程自动化的软件包，以及工业和其他业务流程中机器人流程自动化的全部工作。

科技世界每天都在不断发展，大型组织转向机器人流程自动化来执行日常任务，从而使员工专注于需要更多人际互动的其他任务。

有一种误解认为，随着 RPA 软件的进步，工作岗位会减少，并且不需要人类执行某些类型的日常任务，因为软件/机器人将能够更快更好地完成它.

什么是机器人过程自动化？

根据维基百科，机器人流程自动化 (RPA) 是一种基于隐喻软件机器人 (bots) 或人工智能的业务流程自动化技术。 成本效益： RPA 提供可衡量的业务收益； 例如降低成本、速度和更高的准确性。

  • 准确性：它为组织提供了降低人员成本和人为错误的能力。 机器人是可靠的，不会显示疲劳的特征。

  • 员工生产力：随着在大多数组织中引入 RPA，员工能够专注于客户和客户关系。

  • 业务数据安全：精心设计和管理的 RPA 系统有助于防止信息泄露，这一直是大多数大型组织关注的主要问题。

  • 改进的分析：来自各种来源的无错误、准确的数据将提高分析过程的质量。 这反过来又有助于业务经理做出更好的决策。

  • 一致性： RPA 是安全的，并且在跨多个板执行重复任务时保持一致。

  • 多功能性： RPA 用于各个行业，并具有执行多项任务的能力。 请注意，由逻辑流程支持并且通常重复的任何自动化流程都可以自动化以节省以专注于更重要的任务。

  • 更好的支持和管理：通过在工作流程中实施 RPA，运营流程得到改进。 它有助于提高运营质量并监控组织各个会话中的运营网络。

    • 现在，让我们使用 RPA 框架为这个项目编写一些代码。 您可能已经知道，RPA 框架是一个开源库工具。

    安装所有必要的 Python 包

    开始了：

    应用

    • 站点导航RPA 能够在 Web 中导航并获取特定信息。 这个过程可以节省时间和压力。 现在我们可以初始化 RPA 以打开一个新的浏览器窗口。 启动 rpa 后，我们的目标是浏览特定的博客文章。

       r.url("https://www.urbizedge.com/custom-shape-map-in-power-bi/") #This is aimed at navigating to a website by using the rpa bot

      当您成功完成导航后，您只需关闭浏览器即可。

      • 自动从站点下载数据

      此阶段将使用的数据是 UCI 数据库提供的乳腺癌数据集。

       r.url("https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/breast-cancer/")

      这将自动打开您的 Chrome 浏览器并导航到 UCI 网站以下载乳腺癌数据集。

      点击网站上的下载按钮。 这可以通过运行 click 代码行来完成。 它将有助于下载代码。

       r.click("breast-cancer.data")

      首先激活 RPA 库。

       r.init() r.url('https://www.google.com') r.type('//*[@name="q"]', 'USA[enter]') print(r.read('result-stats')) r.snap('page', 'USA.png')

      所以，这就是这里实际发生的事情：

      • 初始化代码时，您的 chrome 浏览器会打开。
      • 它导航到 Google 并搜索我们插入的特定关键字。 它将搜索保存为 PNG 格式。

      在每行代码的末尾，您必须关闭 rpa 系统。 这种方式可以节省设备的能源。

       r.close() #This is meant to close the RPA system running of Chrome.

      注意：如果有两个带有相同标签“这里”的超链接，我们执行 rpa.click("breast-cancer.data")，那么机器人会点击它找到的第一个。

      结论

      在科技进步的世界里，AR、AI、NFT、BlockChain 和其他加密货币的兴起，你不必害怕技术。 相反，你必须接受它的崛起。 这将有助于节省在文档排序过程中通常会浪费的时间。



    原文:

    In this post, we'll cover a few packages for doing robotic process automation with Python and the entire work of Robotics Process Automation in Industry and other business processes.

    The world of Science and Technology keeps evolving daily with big organizations turning to robotic process automation to carry out their daily tasks, thereby making employees focus on other tasks that require more human interaction.

    There has been a misconception that with the advance of RPA software there would be a loss of jobs and the need for human beings to perform certain types of daily tasks would not be required as the software/bot would be able to do it faster and better.

    What is robotic process automation?

    According to Wikipedia, Robotic Process Automation (RPA) is a form of business process automation technology based on metaphorical software robots (bots) or artificial Intelligence. The RPA software can be said to be a robot or bot that can learn, mimic(copy), and then execute processes based on business processes.

    The RPA can also be said to be an application of Technology, governed by logic and the structural unit, aimed at recording steps and automating business processes as a required output.

    What are the benefits of RPA?

    1. Cost-Effective: RPA provides measurable business benefits; such as cost reduction, speed, and greater accuracy.

    2. Accuracy: It provides organizations with the ability to reduce staffing costs and human error. Robots are reliable and do not show features as fatigues.

    3. Employee Productivity: With the introduction of RPA in most organizations employees are able to focus on client and customer relationships.

    4. Business Data Security: A well-designed and managed RPA system help prevent the leak of information which has been a major concern for most big organizations.

    5. Improved Analytics: Error-free, accurate data from various sources would improve the quality of the analytics process. This in turn helps business managers make better decisions.

    6. Consistency: RPA is safe and is consistent in performing repeated tasks across multiple boards.

    7. Versatility: RPA is used across various industries and has the ability to perform multiple tasks. Note any process of automation that is backed up by logical processes and is usually repeated can be automated to save to focus on more important tasks.

    8. Better Support and Management: Operational processes are improved with the implementation of RPA in the work process. It helps improve operational quality and monitor operational networks across various sessions of an organization.

    Now, let's do some coding for this project using the RPA framework. As you probably already know, the RPA framework is an open-source library tool.

    Install All Necessary Python Packages

    Here we go:

    The rpa library is an open-source python package developed by GitHub: tebelorg

    Applications

    • Site Navigation RPA has the ability to navigate through the web and get specific information. The process saves time and stress. Now we can initialize the RPA to open a new browser window. The browser used by the RPA package is Google Chrome.

    After we initiate the rpa, our aim is to navigate through a particular blog post.

    r.url("https://www.urbizedge.com/custom-shape-map-in-power-bi/")
#This is aimed at navigating to a website by using the rpa bot

    When you have successfully achieved the navigating, it is only proper that you close the browser.

    • Automatically Download Data from a site

    The data that would be used for this stage is the breast cancer dataset that is provided by the UCI database.

    r.url("https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/breast-cancer/")

    This would automatically open your Chrome browser and navigate to the UCI website to download the breast cancer dataset.

    Click on the download button on the site. This can be done by running the click line of code. It will help in downloading the code.

    r.click("breast-cancer.data")

    Web automation is the concept of letting software robots perform pre-defined actions, tasks, and processes on a web browser or web application.

    Start by activating the RPA library.

    r.init()
r.url('https://www.google.com')
r.type('//*[@name="q"]', 'USA[enter]')
print(r.read('result-stats'))
r.snap('page', 'USA.png')

    So, here's what is actually happening here:

    • Your chrome browser opens when you initialize the code.
    • It navigates to Google and searches for the specific keyword we have inserted. (in this case, USA).
    • It saves the search in a PNG format.

    At the end of each line of code you have to close the rpa system. This way saves energy of your device.

    r.close()
#This is meant to close the RPA system running of Chrome.

    Note: That if there are two hyperlinks with the same label "here" and we execute rpa.click("breast-cancer. data"), then the robot will click on the first one it finds.

    Conclusion

    In the world of advancement of science and technology, the rise in AR, AI, NFT, BlockChain, and other cryptocurrencies, you don't have to be scared of technology. Rather, you have to embrace the rise of it. RPA would grow to have more applications, especially in the healthcare and finance sector. This would help save time that is usually lost during document sorting.
Items.Author: kiddojazz

Items.Title: Astrato 嵌入功能 | Astrato Embed Feature
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我将解释如何将带有已发布工作簿的 Astrato Analytic 应用程序嵌入到 Web 库或其他平台。

在一个快速技术和对数据进行大量投资的世界中，分析数据、获得快速洞察力并以更具可读性和图形格式呈现它对于 21 世纪的大多数组织和个人来说是必不可少的。

现在我们问自己商业智能的重要性是什么？ 商业智能 (BI) 是一种技术驱动的流程，用于评估数据并向领导者、经理和员工提供可操作的信息，以帮助他们做出更好的业务决策。

现在每个人都在谈论的一个大问题是， Astrato Analytic 应用程序是什么，功能是什么？

什么是 Astrato 分析

还旨在让客户数据对话并将数据移动到每个业务对话​​的核心。

协作功能：

换句话说，原始工作簿中的内容保持不变。 可嵌入性使您能够关闭分析循环； 汇集数据输入、工作流程和可视化。

使用 Snowflake 和 PostgreSQL 驱动的可视化并将其注入您最喜欢的应用程序中，例如 Notion、HubSpot、SalesForce、Confluence 和其他 CRM 工具。

嵌入链接可用于目标网页的标签（内嵌框架）中，也可用于在 Web 浏览器中单独显示内容。

要求：您必须具有以下角色之一才能创建嵌入链接：创建者、工作区所有者或管理员。

注意： Snowflake 和 PostgreSQL 支持使用 Astrato 数据源创建嵌入式链接

在基于 Web 的平台上嵌入 Astrato 的步骤：

首先，要使用嵌入功能，您需要启用位于大厅工作区左下方的嵌入链接。

单击管理选项卡，您将看到嵌入链接，其中提供了两个选项启用和查看。

要允许工作簿用户添加嵌入链接，请单击启用。 同样，当您单击禁用时会发生相反的情况。

第 2 步：添加为嵌入链接目标授权的域

可嵌入 Astrato 内容的域列表由管理员管理。 这确保只有允许的平台和应用程序才能访问实时数据源。

  • 单击 Embed 链接的View 选项卡，然后显示Item List 和 Allow List 。 单击允许列表。

  • 从允许列表中单击右上角的添加域，然后添加要使用的域。 对于博客，使用了 app.astrato.io。

  • 单击项目列表选项卡以查看您允许的所有网站。

第 3 步：发布您的工作簿

为 astrato 报告创建嵌入链接的重要步骤之一是我们首先需要发布我们的报告。 您将在报告的右上角看到发布按钮。 您还可以在 astrato 官方文档Publish on Astrato上阅读有关发布的更多信息。

第 4 步：嵌入已发布的工作簿

在您单击发布后，它会立即为您提供新选项卡中的视图。 然后，您必须右键单击并选择要使用的嵌入选项。

请注意，当您右键单击时，会为您提供不同的选项

  • 对象嵌入链接
  • 图纸嵌入链接
  • 导出为 CSV
  • 导出 XLSX 您可以选择适合您当前需要的选项。 但是对于本教程，我们将选择工作表嵌入链接。

第 5 步：选择可嵌入链接的查看权限：

在此阶段，您有两个选项可以查看您的嵌入式链接。 限制（只有受邀加入此组织的人可以查看）。

选择要使用的选项后，单击创建，它可以帮助您生成嵌入式链接。

要查看指定的源内容，复制显示的 Embed 链接； 嵌入查看链接

管理员可以访问所有嵌入链接； 但是，具有创建者角色的人只能看到他们创建的嵌入链接。

编辑已发表的作品

为您创建工作簿的副本以供您编辑； 当您重新发布时，会生成另一个嵌入链接，但当您将链接粘贴到 Web 浏览器时，您将被重定向到原始工作簿。

注意： Astrato 应用程序有许多工具可让您快速分析和生成见解。 我强烈建议您在嵌入时确保选择适当的查看权限进行嵌入。

结论：

从传统的 BI 和分析解决方案转向云优先战略是数字化转型的下一步。 在现代云数据仓库中，需要正确的人员、程序和技术。 阿斯特拉托在正确的轨道上，但从长远来看仍有发展空间。

单击此链接与报告公式 1进行交互

进一步研究：

阿斯特拉托社区：阿斯特拉托银河。

现在你可以在 LinkedIn 上与我联系： Temidayo Omoniyi和 Twitter @Kiddojazz



原文:

In this post, Ill explain how you can embed the Astrato Analytic app with a published Workbook to the web-base or other platforms.

In a world of fast technology and immense investment in data, analyzing data, getting quick insight, and presenting it in a more readable and graphical format is imperative to most organizations and individuals in the 21st century.

Now we ask ourselves what is the importance of Business intelligence? Business intelligence (BI) is a technology-driven process for evaluating data and delivering actionable information to leaders, managers, and employees to help them make better business decisions.

Now the big question on everyone's lips is what then is the Astrato Analytic app and what are the functionalities?

What is Astrato Analytics

Astrato, a cloud BI and analytics platform, equips everyone with real-time insights from Snowflake's Data Cloud, Google Sheet, and now PostgreSQL Database allowing them to keep up with the growing flow of business data and make real-time data decisions. Also with the aim to make customers data talk and move data to the heart of every business conversation.

Collaboration Feature:

Astrato allows for collaboration with colleagues or external members by inviting them via email to contribute and make changes and comments.

Astrato Embed Features:

The Embed feature in Astrato lets you take your analysis to the next level, by letting you take your Report from a published Workbook and enabling you to embed it into a web-based platform. In other words, content from your original Workbook remains constant. Embeddability enables you to close the analytics loop; bringing together data input, work processes and visualizations.

Take your Snowflake and PostgreSQL powered visualization and infuse it into your favorite apps like Notion, HubSpot, SalesForce, Confluence, and other CRM tools.

The Embed link can be used within an tag (Inline Frame) on a destination web page, or it can be used to show content in a web browser on its own.

Requirement: You must have one of the following roles to create an Embed link: Creator, Workspace Owner, or Administrator.

NOTE: Snowflake and PostgreSQL supports the creation of embedded links with Astrato data sources

Steps in Embedding Astrato on a Web-Based Platform:

Firstly, to work with the Embed feature you would need to enable the Embed link, which is located at the bottom left of your lobby workspace.

Click on the Administration Tab you would then see the Embed Link which offers two options Enable and View.

To allow users of the Workbook to add Embed links, click Enable. Likewise, the opposite happens when you click on the Disable.

Step 2: Adding the domains that are authorized for Embed links destinations

The list of domains that can be embedded in Astrato content is managed by administrators. This ensures that only permitted platforms and applications have access to the live data source.

  • Click on the View Tab of the Embed link, this then shows the Item List and Allow List. Click on the Allow List.

  • From the Allow List click on Add Domain on the top right, then add the domain you want to use. For the blog, the app.astrato.io was used.

  • Click the item list tab to see all of the websites you've allowed.

Step 3: Publish your Workbook

One of the vital steps of creating an embed link for the astrato report is we first need to publish our report. You will see the publish button at the top right corner of your report. You can also read more on Publishing on the astrato official documentation Publish on Astrato.

Step 4: Embed the Published Workbook

Right after you click on publish it provides you with a view in a new tab. You then have to right-click and select the embed option you want to use.

Notice when you right-click, youre provided with different options

  • Object embed Link
  • Sheet embed link
  • Export to CSV
  • Export XLSX You are allowed to select the option that suits your immediate need. But for this tutorial, we would select the sheet embed link.

Step 5: Select viewing permissions for embeddable links:

At this stage, you are provided with two options for who can view your embedded link. Restrict (Only people invited to this organization can view).

After you select the option you want to use, then click on create which helps you generate an embedded link.

To view the specified source content, copy the Embed link displayed; the < iframe> tag is included in the link, or simply paste the Embed link into a browser.

Embed links for viewing

Administrators have access to all Embed links; however, persons with the Creator role can only see Embed links that they have created.

Editing a Published Work

Astrato Analytics gives you the ability to edit an already published Workbook. A duplicate of your workbook is created for you to edit; when you republish, another Embed link is generated but you will be redirected to your original workbook when you paste the link on your web browser.

NOTE: The Astrato App has a number of tools that let you quickly analyze and generate insights. I strongly encourage you when embedding to make sure you select the appropriate viewing permission to embed.

CONCLUSION:

Moving away from conventional BI and analytics solutions toward a cloud-first strategy is the next step in digital transformation. In a modern Cloud Data Warehouse, the correct people, procedures, and technology are required. Astrato is on the right track, but there is still room for development in the long run.

Click on this link to interact with the report Formula 1

Further Study:

Astrato YouTube Channel: Astrato - YouTube.

Astrato Community: Astrato Galaxy.

Astrato Embed by Ameena Ahsan Pirbhai: Embeds - Overview and Key Steps.

Now you can connect with me on LinkedIn: Temidayo Omoniyi and Twitter @Kiddojazz

Items.Title: Entando 7.0 版本有什么新功能？ | What’s new with the Entando 7.0 Release?
Items.Description:
您想了解更多吗？ 如果是，那么此博客将为您提供我们在 7.0 版中发布的所有内容的完美预览。

可组合业务允许企业通过快速重组服务和产品来快速适应市场变化和机遇。 可组合应用程序允许支持业务所需的应用程序和基础设施以低成本和大大降低的风险快速变化，从而实现可组合业务。

除此之外，新的 Hub 还支持主要企业和 SI 合作伙伴的代码重用。 它还将亲代码开发与低代码组合相结合。 这里的问题是，你真的知道 Entando Hub 是什么吗？

集中组件和业务功能以供跨团队、组或客户使用

  • 发布、管理和交流组件功能、版本和元数据

  • 对组件准备情况进行业务级评估

    • 新的 Entando Hub 为应用程序模块提供了一个存储库。 模块可以是单独的应用程序组件、称为打包业务功能 (PBC) 的组件集合，或者是完整的解决方案或解决方案模板。 该中心提供了一个简单的界面或市场，用于存储模块并使它们可在多个开发团队和应用程序中发现。 该集线器与 Git 结合使用，允许开发团队以与现在相同的方式工作，但添加了一个 Web 界面以简化原始代码模块的创建者与从集线器组装应用程序的应用程序编写者之间的交互。 该中心还为模块管理者提供了一个实施 IT 治理的位置。 该中心可供开发人员、企业和解决方案提供商作为 SaaS 使用，也可以在云端进行私有安装。

    我们的合作伙伴计划正在帮助系统集成商 (SI) 为在 kubernetes 上采用可组合应用程序的客户开发新客户和新收入流。 如果您有兴趣了解有关我们合作伙伴计划的更多信息，请发送电子邮件至 < partner@entando.com >。

    现在，让我们来看看 Entando 组件生成器 (ECG)。 随着新版 Entando 7 的发布，ECG 现已升级为使用 Jhipster 7.2！

    许多有趣的功能对吗？ 好吧，如果您是这里的新手，为什么不开始呢？ 您可以通过我们的文档开始。

    在我们做所有这些令人惊奇的事情的同时，我们也在建立一个专注于可组合性和模块化的社区。 这个社区的目标是提高对可组合应用程序的认识和文化。 如果没有您的支持、爱和贡献，这一切都不可能实现。 如果您真的有兴趣了解更多信息并开始使用我们，请加入我们的discord 服务器并打个招呼！

    请让我们知道您对 Entando 7.0 的反馈！

    谢谢！



    原文:

    Entando is all set with its new release! Would you like to learn more about it? If yes, then this blog would give you a perfect sneak peak of everything we released with version 7.0.

    Entando 7 lets us build a composable business model using composable applications. Composable business allows enterprises to rapidly adapt to market shifts and opportunities by allowing rapid recomposition of services and products. Composable applications enable composable business by allowing the applications and infrastructure required to support the business to change quickly, at low cost, and at greatly reduced risk.

    Apart from this, the new Hub supports code reuse for major enterprises and SI partners. It also aligns pro-code development with low-code composition. The question here is, do you really know what the Entando Hub is?

    The Entando Hub enables a team to share components across Entando Applications. The Hub allows users to:

    • Centralize components and business capabilities for use across teams, groups, or clients

    • Publish, manage and communicate component features, versions and metadata

    • Perform business-level assessment of component readiness

    The new Entando Hub provides a repository for application modules. Modules can be individual application components, collections of components called Packaged Business Capabilities (PBCs), or complete solutions or solution templates. The hub provides a simple interface, or marketplace, to store modules and make them discoverable across multiple development teams and applications. The hub works in conjunction with Git allowing development teams to work in the same way as they do today, but adding a web interface to simplify the interaction between creators of pro-code modules, and application composers assembling applications from the hub. The hub also enables module curators a location to enforce IT governance. The hub is available to developers, enterprises, and solution providers as a SaaS or for private installation on prem on in the cloud.

    Our partner program is helping System Integrators (SIs) develop new customers and new revenue streams for customers adopting composable applications on kubernetes. If you are interested in learning more about our Partner Program, drop us a line at <partner@entando.com>.

    Now, let’s also take a look at the Entando Component Generator (ECG). The ECG is a Jhipster blueprint that generates Entando compatible Java applications and micro frontends. With the new release of Entando 7, the ECG has now been upgraded to use Jhipster 7.2!

    The Entando CLI has also been improved. The ent CLI provides a set of commands that accelerate the developer experience by assisting the developer with common tasks such as quickly installing a new copy of Entando, generating an Entando project via JHipster, deploying an Entando Bundle, etc. To learn more about the fixes and changes take a look at our release notes

    Many interesting features right? Well, if you’re pretty new here, why don’t you get started? You can get started through our documentation.

    While we are doing all these amazing things, we are also building a community that deeply focuses on Composability and Modularity. The goal of this community is to grow an awareness and culture about composable applications. And none of this would be possible without your support, love and contribution. If you are really interested to learn more and get started with us, join our discord server and drop us a hi!

    Do let us know your feedback on Entando 7.0!

    Thank you!
Items.Author: sohinip

Items.Title: 过去一周的 7 大特色 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
Items.Description:
每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从周一开始，到周日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

一台显示器来统治他们

关于一台显示器是否优于多台显示器的争论已经持续多年。 在争论的另一端，@aparyus 向我们展示了在同时完成两件事时如何使用多个监视器更有效。 你在这场辩论中处于什么位置？

不知何故，CSS 样式表总是混杂着类和 id。 本周， @itstrueintheory来看看是什么让 CSS 变得复杂。

所有关于应用内购买

在这篇文章中， @yuliiakondr将介绍应用内购买的功能、消耗性和非消耗性 IAP 之间的区别，以及如何使用 Qonversion 进行设置。 有用的信息！

如今，Osome 的代码库中没有 Flow 或 JS 代码，成功将超过 20 万个 JavaScript 文件迁移到 TypeScript。 探索@frolovdev和他们的团队是如何做到这一点的。

追溯前端软件开发的步骤

在这篇详尽的文章中，David 分享了他们是如何到达现在的位置的，希望它能帮助早期职业软件开发人员✨

导航、获取、解析、渲染，天哪！

奖励：很棒的插图！

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 本周关注 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注这个系列。 你可能只是在其中！



原文:

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

One Monitor to Rule Them All

The debate over whether one monitor is better than multiple monitors has been going on for years. @wiseai exposes the drawbacks of using a multiple monitor set-up and shows how you can make using a single monitor a breeze, especially on Ubuntu.

Is 2 Greater Than 1?

On the other end of the argument, @aparyus shows us how using multiple monitors can be much more efficient when getting two things done at once. Where do you land in this debate?

CSS is Meant to be Messy

Somehow, CSS stylesheets are always a jumbled mess of classes and ids. This week, @itstrueintheory takes a look at what makes CSS complex.

All about in-app purchases

In this post, @yuliiakondr takes a look at the features of in-app purchases, the difference between consumable and non-consumable IAPs, and how to set it all up with Qonversion. Useful info!

How Osome migrated from Flow to Typescript

Today, there is no Flow or JS code in Osome’s codebase, successfully migrating more than 200k files of JavaScript to TypeScript. Explore how @frolovdev and their team accomplished this.

Retracing the steps to frontend software development

@davidmorais is currently on their fourth job in IT — seems like a pretty good person to learn from if you're hoping to get into software development! In this thorough post, David shares a look at how they got where they are in the hopes that it will help an early-career software developer ✨

Navigation, Fetching, Parsing, Rendering, oh my!

@arikaturika goes into the right level of depth on web browsers — they explain what happens from the moment we type a web address into the address bar until the page we are trying to access gets displayed on our screen. Bonus: awesome illustrations!

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Items.Title: Material-UI 入门 | Getting Started with Material-UI
Items.Description:
乔塞特加西亚

发表于嗨开发社区！ 我带着另一个新手演练回来了，这可能会为你们中的一些人节省我试图理解Material-UI(MUI)的时间。

是的，您可以说 MUI 有自己的入门部分，怎么可能分解得更多呢？ 好吧，作为 React 的新学生，我仍然花了几个晚上的学习时间与我的代码作斗争，只是为了让最简单的网页设计能够工作。

这并不是专门针对 MUI 的，只是承认学习新平台的“零基础”在新开发人员和可能有更多经验的人之间是不同的。

一些初步提示：

什么是 Material-UI，新的开发者何时会遇到它？

对于一个新的开发者来说，这个框架将帮助你设计一个更干净、更美观的 React 应用程序。 在你最初的几个 React 项目中，你可能会被建议使用这个框架。

  • 如果您是 React 的新手并且正在处理您的第一个项目，我强烈建议您使用普通的 JSX 元素构建您的平台，并在出现之前关注功能。 这将帮助您在实现 Material-UI 元素之前记住常规结构。
    • 如果您有 JSX 经验或对 HTML 元素有信心，那么从 MUI 元素开始并专注于完成之后的工作可能会更容易
  • 在加入之前，请考虑研究一下主题。 主题将使您的颜色、版式和字体在整个项目中保持一致（本博文不涉及主题）。
    • 如果没有主题，您可能会在为每个部分试用 CSS 道具时遇到困难。
    • 在我的第一个项目结束时，回去重新设计元素的主题感觉有点浪费时间，我希望我能先看到这一点。

初始设置

令人惊讶的是，这是该过程中最简单的部分！ 您可以在此处找到入门页面。

  1. 您将需要安装package dependencies 。 阿卡。 您正在安装包含 MUI 包和依赖项的文件，因此您可以获取样式化的组件而不必担心文件路径
// with npm npm install @mui/material @emotion/react @emotion/styled // with yarn yarn add @mui/material @emotion/react @emotion/styled
  • 关注此页面上的初步建议（上面发布）！ 除非您更熟悉 Material-UI，否则此页面上的 styled-components 选项不是必需的。
  • 我使用yarn还是npm ？ 这是个人喜好。 我建议默认使用您以前使用过的那个，以将新事物保持在最低限度。 例如，我使用npm是因为我在整个项目中都在使用它，但是在后续尝试使用yarn并没有太大的不同。
  1. 这就是安装！ 此页面上还有一些其他建议，但这些不是入门所必需的。 如果您决定以后需要这些，请查看这些，然后您就会知道在哪里查找。
  2. 查看左侧导航栏上的可用组件，看看是什么吸引了您的眼球以开始使用！

样式化 MUI 元素：

一旦你有了一个想要开始使用的组件，你需要在你的 React 组件上添加以下内容：

  1. 第一步安装的 MUI 包中的import标头
    • 与您的项目组件类似，您需要从已安装的包中导入样式组件
    • 每个组件都有不同的依赖项需要导入
    • 要获取正确的导入，请单击示例代码上的< >图标以展开完整代码以满足您的复制和粘贴需求

  1. 您正在渲染的组件的代码
    • 为确保您获得正确的代码示例，请使用“在沙盒中编辑代码”图标来使用提供的示例并收集正确的部分。
    • 您将无法复制和粘贴整个示例并使其完全按照您的需要工作。 您将需要挑选并提取特定的部分并将其应用于您的项目中。

结论：

从这里开始，您将需要继续前进，感受您需要和喜欢的东西！ 如开头所述，更改组件的默认主题需要单独的博客文章。 如果您有更多时间阅读这篇博文，我已经链接了一些我在下面找到的有用资源，而我的知识只是表面上的：

如何设计一个完美的 React Material UI 表单- CodAffect

React Material-UI Themes: Customize Material Components for your Project - Bruno Antunes React Material-UI 主题：为您的项目自定义 Material 组件- Bruno Antunes

Sohini Pattanayak - Sohini Pattanayak - Apr 13 4月13日

Mangabo Kolawole - Mangabo Kolawole - Apr 10 4月10日



原文:

JosetteTGarcia

Posted on Apr 27

Hi Dev Community! I’m back with another newbie walk-through that may save some of you the hours I spent trying to understand Material-UI(MUI).

Yes, you could say that MUI has its own Getting Started section, how could it possibly be broken down more than that? Well, as a new student to React, I still spent more than a few study nights fighting with my code just to get the most simple of web designs to work.

This is not a knock on MUI specifically, just an acknowledgement that “ground zero” for learning a new platform is different between a new dev and someone who may have more experience.

Some Initial Tips:

What is Material-UI and when will a new Dev come across it?

"MUI offers a comprehensive suite of UI tools to help you ship new features faster. Start with Material UI, our fully-loaded component library, or bring your own design system to our production-ready components."
-Material-UI Home page

For a new dev, this framework will help you design a cleaner, more aesthetic React app. You will likely be suggested this framework during your first few React projects.

  • If you are brand new to React & working on one of your first projects, I highly recommend building your platform using normal JSX elements and focussing on function before appearance. This will help you memorize the normal structure before implementing Material-UI elements.
    • MUI components differed enough from JSX elements that I believe I would have been unable to recall the difference at later times if I jumped into design first as I have with other projects.
    • If you have experience with JSX or feel confident in HTML elements, it could be easier to start with MUI elements and focus on making the work after
  • Before jumping in, consider looking into Theming. Themes will keep your colors, typography, and fonts consistent across the project (This blog post will not cover themes).
    • Without themes, you may get stuck trialing CSS props for each section.
    • By the end of my first project, it felt like a bit of time waster to go back and re-design the themes of my elements and I wish I would have saw this first.

Initial Set Up

Surprisingly, this was the easiest part of the process! You can find the getting started page here.

  1. You will need to install the package dependencies. Aka. You’re installing the file that holds the MUI packages and dependencies so you can grab the styled components without worrying about file paths 
// with npm
npm install @mui/material @emotion/react @emotion/styled

// with yarn
yarn add @mui/material @emotion/react @emotion/styled
  • Focus on the initial suggestion on this page (posted above)! Unless you are more familiar with Material-UI, the styled-components option on this page is not necessary.
  • Do I use yarn or npm? This is a personal preference. I recommend defaulting to the one you’ve used before to keep the new things at a minimum. For example, I used npm because I’ve used it throughout my project, but experimenting with yarn further down the line did not make much of a difference.
  1. That’s it for install! There are some other recommendations on this page, but these are not necessary to get started. Review these in case you decide you need these later down the line and then you’ll know where to look.
  2. Review the available components on the left, nav-bar to see what catches your eye to get started!

Styling MUI Elements:

Once you have a component in mind you want to get started with, you will need the following on your React component:

  1. The import headers from your MUI package installed on the first steps
    • Similar to your project components, you need to import the styling components from the installed package
    • Each component will have different dependencies you need to import
    • To grab the correct imports, click the < > icon on the example code to expand the full code for your copying and pasting needs

  1. The code for the component you are rendering
    • MUI will show you multiple examples of controlled and uncontrolled elements and how to set default settings. To make sure you got the correct code example, use the “Edit Code in Sandbox” Icon to play with provided examples and collect the correct section.
    • You will not be able to copy and paste the whole example and have it work exactly as you need. You will need to pick and pull specific pieces and work it into your project.

Conclusion:

From here, you will need to just keep going and get a feel for what you need and like! As mentioned at the beginning, altering the default themes of the components would need a separate blog post. I’ve linked some helpful resources I’ve found below if you have some additional time as this blog post, and my knowledge, just scratch the surface:

How to Design a Perfect React Material UI Form - CodAffection

React Material-UI Themes: Customize Material Components for your Project - Bruno Antunes

Sohini Pattanayak - Apr 13

Mangabo Kolawole - Apr 10

Items.Title: 我对初学者的建议 | My Advice For You, A Beginner Programmer
Items.Description:
我的名字是 Matt Stead，我和你一样，是一名初学者。 您正在阅读本文，因为您正在考虑学习如何编码，或者您已经开始学习。 无论哪种方式，您都在这里，因为您知道您将要阅读的内容是来自诚实、有意识和英俊的人的宝贵建议。 开个玩笑……但说真的，我要给你一些宝贵的建议。

我的编码生涯只有几个月的时间，我希望它会很长、充实、有趣、令人兴奋等。这个清单还在继续。 我喜欢编码。 嗯，爱是一个很强烈的词。 实际上，这让我想到了我的第一条建议。

建议 1：您不必像喜欢吃或喜欢听自己喜欢的歌曲那样喜欢编码。

你不必吃饭，睡觉，梦想编码（虽然我警告你，这正在成为我梦想中的主题，我什至解决了我梦想中的编码问题..）。 我的观点更多是针对那些质疑他们是否属于这个领域的人。 重要的是要意识到很少有人痴迷于他们的全部工作……答案是没有人。 一项工作需要重复使用的某种技能。 重复导致无聊。 无聊会导致不爱你的整个工作。 有道理？ 热爱工作的人，热爱他们所做的 70%，这太棒了。 你必须找到一份让你乐于加强并与工作中繁琐、重复的部分（剩下的 30%）作斗争的职业。 你愿意经历这一切，是因为你对 70% 的热爱。

举个例子，我喜欢建立网站并向我的朋友和家人展示，我真的很喜欢解决问题和学习实现不同结果的新方法。 我被驱使加入一家公司并构建了一个行业颠覆者的应用程序，我每天都在努力工作以实现这一目标。 但我不喜欢上周五的晚上，我盯着屏幕看了 2 个小时，试图找出我的代码出了什么问题，却发现这是因为我忘记在我的一个函数中添加一个单数点. 我会 100% 喜欢吃披萨然后整晚弹吉他。 但世界不是这样运作的。 找一些足以刺激你的东西，让你整天磨练它，并让你兴奋地在完成后去享受你的爱好。

建议 2：提出问题

不要害怕提问。 很多问题。 但是，我意识到要成为一名编码员，你必须能够在谷歌上研究你的询问并自己弄清楚。 这将很难开始，特别是当 google 上的解决方案似乎有更复杂的代码围绕它时。 但你会得到它的窍门。 花 10 分钟尝试自己弄清楚。 如果你成功了，那就太好了。 如果你失败了，伸出手去问问别人，你已经受够了，是时候学习新东西了。

忠告3：没有专家是专家，所以不要追求完美。

在过去的几个月里，我看到导师、讲师和讲师不断地在谷歌上查找东西。 当我第一次开始时，我心想……“哦，哦，也许我来错地方了。” 这是完全错误的。 没有专家，所以不要给自己施加压力要完美并记住一切。 只需学习如何应用您的知识并解决您遇到的问题。 谷歌是唯一的专家。 对人好点。

建议 4：尽可能与他人一起编码

我不能强调这一点。 为了开始我的训练营，我想​​成为最好的。 计划是默默地编码，努力工作，我一直成为房间里最好的编码器。 但我很快意识到，我和朋友一起编码效率更高。 我和我的朋友经历了一个我们都无法独自完成的实验室。 我们进行了讨论并达成了解决方案。 与与我水平相同的人讨论并找出解决方案的过程非常宝贵。 这与一对一的导师坐下来花 100 美元买几片美味的比萨饼一样有价值。

建议5：学习基础知识！

学习基础知识。 学习 Github，了解 npm 到底意味着什么（在此处查看我的其他博客）。 不要在没有先学习音符的情况下开始拿起吉他和弹奏。 没有先学习音阶就不要去弹钢琴。 笔记、尺度是 git、循环、数据类型之类的东西。 所以学习他们！

最后一条建议：花点时间练习，练习，练习

不要急于求神的爱！ 假设您观看了教程或参加了有关循环数组的课程。 讲座很有道理，你也跟着学得很好。 你对此感觉很好。 酷，我们真的不在乎你的感受（对不起），我们在乎你能做什么。 所以回家吧，编写数组并迭代它们，直到迭代数组变得像手背一样熟悉。

能够阅读代码是一回事，这是一件很棒的事情。 但是能够编写代码是一个完全不同的球赛。 你需要练习。 耐心点。 它会通过足够的练习来找你。 编码是一种技能，世界上任何人只要有足够的耐心和奉献精神就可以学习。 一旦你掌握了技能，你就掌握了！ 您可以随时使用它，过上我们之前谈到的充实（当然也是奢华）的生活。

祝你好运！



原文:

My name is Matt Stead, and I am, like you, a beginner programmer. You are reading this article because you are contemplating learning how to code or you have already begun. Either way, you are here because you know that what you’re about to read is valuable advice from someone who is honest, aware, and handsome. Just kidding… but seriously, I’m about to give you some invaluable advice.

I am only a couple months into my coding career, one that I expect to be long, fulfilling, fun, exciting, etc. The list goes on. I love to code. Well, love is a strong word. Actually, this leads me to my first piece of advice.

Advice number 1: You don’t have to LOVE coding like you love eating or listening to your favorite song.

You don’t have to eat, sleep, dream coding (although I’m warning you, this is becoming a theme in my dreams, I’ve even solved coding problems in my dreams..). My point here is more for those questioning whether they belong in this field. It’s import to realize how few people are obsessed with the entirety of their job… the answer is no-one. A job requires a certain skill that is utilized repetitively. Repetition leads to boredom. Boredom leads to not loving the entirety of your job. Make sense? People who love their jobs, love 70% of what they do, and that’s AWESOME. You have to find the career where you are happy to toughen up and battle through the tedious, repetitive parts of your job (the other 30%). You are willing to go through this because of your love for the 70%.

To give you an example, I love building out websites and showing my friends and family and I really enjoy problem solving and learning new ways to accomplish different outcomes. I am driven to join a company and build an application that is an industry disrupter and I work hard each and every day to one day accomplish this. But I didn’t love my last Friday night where I stared at my screen for 2 hours trying to figure out what was wrong with my code, only to find out that it was because I forgot to put a singular dot in one of my functions. I would have 100% loved to eat pizza then play guitar all night. But the world doesn’t work that way. Find something that stimulates you enough to grind it out all day, and keep you excited to go enjoy your hobbies when you’re finished.

Advice number 2: Ask questions

Don’t be afraid to ask questions. A lot of questions. BUT, I realized that to become a coder, you have to be able to research your inquiry on google and figure it out yourself. This will be difficult starting out, especially when the solutions on google seem to have more complicated code surrounding it. But you will get the hang of it. Spend 10 minutes trying to figure it out yourself. If you succeed, great. If you fail, reach out and ask someone, you’ve suffered enough and it’s time to learn something new.

Advice number 3: There’s no such thing is an expert, so don’t strive for perfection.

In the past couple months, I have watched tutors, lecturers and instructors constantly looking up things in Google. When I first started, I thought to myself… “uh oh, maybe I’m in the wrong place.” This is totally false. There are no experts, so don’t put pressure on yourself to be perfect and memorize everything. Just learn how to apply your knowledge and solve problems you encounter. Google is the only expert. Google is your, and every developer’s friend. Be nice.

Advice number 4: Code with others when you can

I cannot stress this enough. To start my bootcamp, I wanted to be the best of the best. The plan was to code in silence, grind so hard I became the best coder in the room at all times. But what I quickly realized is I was much more productive coding with a friend. My friend and I went through a lab we both couldn’t do alone. We talked it out and reached the solution. The process of talking it out and figuring out the solution with someone the same level as me was invaluable. It is just as, if not more, valuable than sitting down with a one on one tutor and paying $100 you could’ve spend on a couple nice slices of pizza.

Advice number 5: Learn the basics!

Learn the fundamentals. Learn Github, learn what the hell npm means (check out my other blog here). Don’t start picking up the guitar and strumming without first learning the notes. Don’t go play the piano without first learning the scales. The notes, the scales are things like git, loops, data-types. So learn them !

Last piece of Advice: Take your damn time and practice, practice, practice

Don’t rush for the love of God! Let’s say you watch a tutorial or go to a class that talks about looping through arrays. The lecture made total sense and you followed along perfectly. You feel great about it. Cool, we don’t really care how you feel (sorry), we care what you can do. So go home, write up arrays and iterate over them until iterating arrays becomes as familiar to you as the back of your hand.

Being able to read code is one thing, and it’s a great thing. But being able to write code is a whole different ball-game. And you need to practice. Be PATIENT. It will come to you with enough practice. Coding is a skill, one that anybody in the world can learn if they are patient and dedicated enough. Once you HAVE the skill, you got it! You’ll be able to utilize it whenever you want and go live that fulfilling (and luxurious, of course) life we talked about earlier.

Good luck!

米克拉达技术

发表于毫米波雷达是利用天线发射波长为1-10mm、频率为24-300GHz的毫米波作为发射波的雷达传感器。 毫米波雷达通过处理目标反射信号，可以获取车辆与其他物体之间的相对距离、相对速度、角度和运动方向等物理环境信息。 毫米波的波长介于厘米波和光波之间，具有微波和光波的优点。

毫米波的波长介于厘米波和光波之间，具有微波和光波的优点。 相比之下，毫米波雷达具有体积小、易于集成、空间分辨率高、穿透雾、烟、尘能力强等特点。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

Micradar Teachnology

Posted on Apr 27

Millimeter wave radar is a radar sensor that uses the millimeter wave with the antenna transmitting wavelength of 1-10mm and frequency of 24-300ghz as the transmitting wave. Millimeter-wave radar can obtain physical environment information such as relative distance, relative speed, Angle and movement direction between vehicles and other objects by processing target reflected signals. Millimeter wave has a wavelength between centimetre wave and light wave and has the advantages of microwave and light wave.

Millimeter wave has a wavelength between centimetre wave and light wave and has the advantages of microwave and light wave. In contrast, millimeter wave radar has the characteristics of small size, easy integration, high spatial resolution, and strong ability to penetrate fog, smoke and dust.

Cristian Fernando - Apr 25

Items.Title: 亚马逊网络服务的好处 | Benefits of Amazon Web Services
Items.Description:
便于使用
每个服务都有自己的控制台，它提供分步说明来帮助您快速配置资源。 如果您愿意，可以使用 API 创建脚本来自动执行常见任务，例如启动新实例。

全球基础设施
该基础架构使客户能够在本地部署其应用程序并将数据存储在世界各地。 将资源放在靠近用户的位置可确保低延迟和高性能。

安全
您可以使用 AWS 托管的安全服务来监控您的数据、应用程序和资源是否存在潜在风险。 令人难以置信的多样化工具
可以使用的工具包括数据库、分析引擎、搜索引擎、计算能力、云存储设备等等？ 你的名字，他们可能有它。 可用资源的绝对数量和种类使该平台对几乎所有软件开发人员都很有用。

可扩展性
亚马逊云服务非常适合希望有效管理增长或希望避免购买昂贵硬件的企业。 亚马逊网络服务 (AWS) 提供的资源可以随着需求的变化而扩大或缩小，以帮助降低成本。 这对于具有不可预测工作负载的企业特别有用，例如提供软件即服务 (SaaS) 产品的企业

无服务器应用
无服务器计算使您能够构建可扩展的物联网、移动和 Web 应用程序平台后端，而无需考虑像亚马逊简单存储服务这样的服务器。 使用无服务器应用程序，您可以快速部署代码以响应事件并根据需求自动扩展或缩减。

无限服务器容量
亚马逊网络服务 (AWS) 云技术在全球 24 个地理区域内运营 77 个可用区，并宣布计划在印度、印度尼西亚、日本、西班牙和瑞士再增加 16 个可用区和 5 个区域。

降低总拥有成本
许多公司在将应用程序迁移到云基础设施时已经看到了 AWS 降低总拥有成本 (TCO) 的好处。 减少的幅度可能很大：一项研究发现，迁移到亚马逊网络服务(AWS) 后五年内减少了 62%，其中包括 91% 的硬件节省和 74% 的软件节省。

快速部署
在大多数情况下，只需几分钟即可开始使用亚马逊网络服务，而建立本地 IT 基础设施则需要数周或数月。 而且由于您不必预先购买服务器或其他设备，因此您可以以最少的投资开始使用。

提高性能
借助 AWS 服务，您可以提供所需的任何资源（例如存储或计算容量），并且只需为您使用的资源付费。

可靠性
此外，AWS 云服务将其服务设计为在多个级别（例如，组件、服务器机架、网络交换机）的故障中提供高可用性和弹性。

托管 IT 服务
托管服务提供商 (MSP) 可以为企业带来诸多好处，包括可扩展性、成本效益和减少内部资源负担。

随着公司的发展，IT 需求将会增加。 当企业增加新员工或扩展到 IT 需求增加的新市场时，这些需求会变得尤为明显。 这是因为 MSP 通常具有较低的间接成本，因为它们不需要自己的办公空间或设备。 此外，使用 MSP 意味着您无需雇用和培训自己的 IT 专业人员。

许多小公司可能没有足够的预算来吸引和留住 IT 领域的顶尖人才。 结论
在一天结束时，您可以选择在 AWS 上启动您的产品或服务，或者在内部运行您的软件。 它取决于多种因素，包括产品复杂性、技术要求、公司文化和财务状况。 如果您想发展您的组织，云计算可能非常适合您。 以随用随付模式部署应用程序比以往任何时候都更容易，可扩展性触手可及。 请记住，在外部硬盘驱动器或其他存储设备上维护数据的物理备份仍然很重要。

来源链接



原文:

Ease of Use
AWS cloud service provider is designed with ease of use at the forefront. Each service has its console, which provides step-by-step instructions to help you configure resources quickly. AWS also offers a variety of SDKs for popular programming languages, as well as command-line tools for configuration and deployment. If you prefer, you can use the APIs to create scripts that automate common tasks like spinning up new instances.

There are also many partner solutions available in the AWS Marketplace that provide additional functionality for your applications and infrastructure.

Global Infrastructure
AWS has a global infrastructure that is broken down into regions and availability zones. This infrastructure gives customers the ability to locally deploy their applications and store data in locations across the world. Having resources located close to users ensures low latency and high performance.

Security
AWS cloud provides you with the security tools needed to help protect your data, applications, and infrastructure. Amazon’s highly available and scalable architecture can help mitigate threats. You can use AWS-managed security services to monitor your data, applications, and resources for potential risks. AWS also offers tools like private clouds and virtual private networks (VPNs) to help you keep sensitive information protected.

Incredibly Diverse Array of Tools
Not only does the AWS cloud-based platform offers a plethora of services for you to use in creating your applications, but there are also over 70 different services available for you to use! Tools that can be used include databases, analytics engines, search engines, compute power, cloud storage devices, and more? You name it, they probably have it. The sheer number and variety of resources available make this platform useful to pretty much every software developer out there.

Scalability
Amazon cloud services are perfect for businesses that want to manage growth effectively or want to avoid buying expensive hardware. The resources offered by amazon web services (AWS) can be scaled up or down as requirements change to help reduce costs. This is particularly helpful for businesses with unpredictable workloads like those offering Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) products

Serverless apps
Serverless computing enables you to build scalable IoT, mobile, and a web application platform backend without thinking about servers like amazon simple storage service. With serverless apps, you can quickly deploy code to respond to events and automatically scale up or down based on demand.

Unlimited Server Capacity
AWS cloud services let you provision as much or as little capacity as you want to support your application needs at any time. The amazon web services (AWS) cloud technology operates 77 Availability Zones within 24 geographic Regions around the world, with announced plans for 16 more Availability Zones and five more Regions in India, Indonesia, Japan, Spain, and Switzerland.

Lower TCO
Many companies are already seeing the AWS benefits of the lower total cost of ownership (TCO) when they move their applications to the cloud infrastructure. The reductions can be significant: one study found that moving to amazon web services (AWS) resulted in a 62 percent reduction in five years, including savings of 91 percent on hardware and 74 percent on software.

Fast deployment
In most cases, it takes only minutes to get started with the amazon web service, compared with the weeks or months required to set up an on-premises IT infrastructure. And because you don’t have to purchase servers or another equipment upfront, you can get started with a minimal investment.

Improved performance
The AWS global infrastructure allows you to scale your resources up or down quickly and easily, so you can run your applications at optimum levels at all times. With AWS services, you can provide whatever resources you need (such as storage or compute capacity) and only pay for what you use.

Reliability
AWS data centers are built with redundant power supplies, backup generators, and multiple network connections for maximum reliability. In addition, AWS cloud services has designed its services to provide high availability and resiliency from failures at multiple levels (e.g., component, server rack, network switch).

Managed IT Services
Managed service providers (MSPs) can provide numerous benefits for businesses, including scalability, cost-effectiveness, and a reduction in the burden on internal resources.

As a company grows, IT needs are going to increase. These needs can become especially apparent when a business is adding new employees or expanding into new markets with increased IT requirements. MSPs can provide support at scale to ensure that a company’s IT needs are always being met.

MSPs are also often more cost-effective than hiring an internal IT department. This is because MSPs usually have lower overhead costs, as they do not require their own office space or equipment. Also, using an MSP means that you don’t need to hire and train your own IT professionals.

Many small companies may not have the large budgets necessary to attract and retain top talent from the IT field. An MSP allows these companies to gain access to high-quality expertise without going through the process of hiring and training the personnel themselves.

Conclusion
At the end of the day, you can choose to launch your product or service on AWS or run your software in-house. It depends on a variety of factors including product complexity, technical requirements, company culture, and financials. If you want to grow your organization, cloud computing could be a good fit for you. It’s easier than ever to deploy applications in a pay-as-you-go model with scalability at your fingertips. Just remember that it’s still important to maintain physical backups of your data on an external hard drive or other storage devices.

Source link

Items.Title: 控制您网站 AWS 上的传入流量 | Control your incoming traffic on your website AWS
Items.Description:
阿拉文德·库马尔·维穆拉

发表于您可以使用负载均衡器控制网站上的流量。

什么是负载均衡器？
负载均衡器在多个可用区中的多个 EC2 实例之间分配传入的应用程序流量。 这增加了应用程序的容错能力。

开始使用

让我们开始创建一个 EC2 实例

现在打开 Amazon EC2 控制台

现在在左侧菜单中，在 Instances 下选择一个实例

创建一个新实例并在配置页面下，您将看到实例编号输入2（我们正在创建两个具有相同配置的实例）

启动您的实例
完成您刚刚启动了 2 个实例。

让我们创建 TargetGroups

现在在左侧菜单中，在负载均衡下选择 TargetGroups

创建一个新的目标组

在实例下选择您的 2 个实例并将它们添加到待处理实例，然后单击创建目标组。

完成你刚刚创建了一个目标组

让我们创建一个负载均衡器

现在在左侧菜单中，在负载均衡下选择一个负载均衡器

创建新的负载均衡器

在安全组下选择您为实例提供的组

在 Targetgroup 下选择您刚刚创建的 Target Group。

在底部单击创建负载均衡器

完成 您刚刚创建了一个负载均衡器

现在转到实例页面并单击您的第一个实例。

现在单击连接并使用 EC2 实例连接，单击连接。


现在你会看到一个 Linux 控制台会弹出

现在我们需要在您的实例中加载一个示例网站，因为我们需要在您的控制台中访问 root 类型：这将移动到根目录

现在按照代码片段：

 yum update -y # it will update the system yum install httpd -y # it will install the Apache Hypertext transfer protocol server program systemctl start httpd # It will start the server cd /var/www/html/ # After install httpd you will see the html directory where we can add our html pages which will be displayed on the brower nano index.html # it will create a index.html file in html folder

将此 HTML 数据添加到您的 index.html 文件

<html> <head> <title> Welcome to server 1 </title> </head> <body> <h1>Welcome to AWS praticse web page</h1> </body> </html>

保存文件并输入此命令

systemctl enable httpd # server will be enable and it runs continuously

现在转到您的实例并选择您的第一个实例。 在底部，您将看到公共地址，复制该地址并将其粘贴到新选项卡中，您将看到标题为 Welcome to server 1 的示例网页，主要内容为 Welcome to AWS practice web page。

现在对另一个实例重复相同的过程在 index.html 文件中将标题更新为 Welcome to server 2

您可以拥有相同的标题，但是，在这里可以看到加载网页时正在运行的实例现在在左侧菜单中，在负载均衡下选择目标组并选择您的 TG 在底部，您将看到两个实例都是健康的。

现在转到负载均衡器并选择您的负载均衡器。 在底部，您将看到 DNS 名称复制该 URL 并将其粘贴到新选项卡中



刷新您的页面，您会注意到每次实例都在更改。

每次刷新都可以看到网页标题在变化。

这就是我们可以通过在每次刷新时交换实例来控制 Web 流量的方式。

注意：您需要在两个实例中保留相同的代码。

如果您有更多用户并且您的 CPU 超过 80%，您需要使用自动缩放。

我将写一篇关于自动缩放什么是 CPU 移动到 80% 的新文章……

我是 AWS 的初学者。 如果你觉得文章有问题，你可以评论它。 所以，我可以提高自己。

这仅用于测试一段时间后您可以删除您的实例。 如果您将其保持活跃几天，它可能会在练习删除您的实例后向您收费。

感谢您阅读文章……

adithyasrinivasan - adithyasrinivasan - Apr 2 4月2日



原文:

Aravind Kumar Vemula

Posted on Apr 27

You can control traffic flow on your website using a load balancer.

What is Load Balancer?
A load balancer distributes incoming application traffic across multiple EC2 instances in multiple Availability Zones. This increases the fault tolerance of your applications.

Get started

Let’s start creating a EC2 instance

Now open Amazon EC2 Console

Now on the left menu, under Instances choose an instance

Create a new instance and under configure page, you will see the no of instance enter 2 (We are creating two instances with the same configurations)

Launch your instance
Done You just launched your 2 instances.

Let’s create TargetGroups

Now on the left menu, under load balancing choose TargetGroups

Create a new target group

Under instances select your 2 instances and add them to pending instances and click on create target group.

Done you just created a target group

Let’s create a Load balancer

Now on the left menu, under load balancing choose a load balancer

Create a new Load Balancer

Under security groups select the group which you have given to your instances

Under Targetgroup select the Target Group that you have created just now.

At the bottom click on create a load balancer

Done You just created a Load Balancer

Now go to the instances page and click on your first instance.

Now click on connect and use EC2 instance connect, click on connect.


Now you will see a Linux console will popup

Now we need to load a sample website in your instance, for that we need root access in your console Type: This will move to the root directory

Now follow the code snippets:

yum update -y  # it will update the system
yum install httpd -y # it will install the Apache Hypertext transfer protocol server program
systemctl start httpd # It will start the server
cd /var/www/html/ # After install httpd you will see the html directory where we can add our html pages which will be displayed on the brower
nano index.html # it will create a index.html file in html folder

Add this HTML data to your index.html file

<html>
 <head>
  <title>
     Welcome to server 1
  </title>
 </head>
 <body>
    <h1>Welcome to AWS praticse web page</h1>
 </body>
</html>

Save the file and enter this command

systemctl enable httpd # server will be enable and it runs continuously

Now go to your instance and select your first instance. At the bottom you will see the public address, copy that address and paste it into a new tab you will see the sample web page with the title Welcome to server 1 and the main content as Welcome to AWS practice web page.

Now repeat the same process for the other instance In the index.html file update the title to Welcome to server 2

You can have the same title but, here are able to see which instance is running while loading a webpage Now on the left menu, under load balancing choose target groups and select your TG At the bottom, you will see that both instances are healthy.

Now go to the load balancer and select your load balancer. At the bottom, you will see the DNS name copy that URL and paste it into the new tab



Refresh your page you will notice that every time the instance is changing.

You can able see the webpage title is changing every time while refreshing.

This is how we can control web traffic by interchanging the instance for every refresh.

Note: You need to keep the same code in both instances.

If you have more users and if your CPU is more than 80% you need to use autoscaling.

I will write a new article on autoscaling what is the CPU moves to 80%…

I am a beginner at AWS. If you feel there is something wrong with the article you can comment on it. So, that I can improve myself.

** Note:
This is only for testing after some time you can delete your instance. If you keep it active for some days it may charge you so after practicing delete your Instance.

Thanks for reading the article…

adithyasrinivasan - Apr 2

Items.Title: 承诺 SEMÂNTICOS | COMMITS SEMÂNTICOS
Items.Description:
提交 ruim 示例❌

提交 bom 示例✅

 feat(banana): Frutas.js mudei a classe de cor da banana de amarelo para verde pois ela estava madura

壮举：（novo recurso para o usuário，não um novo recurso para script de construção）

  • 修复：（correção de bug para o usuário，não uma correção para um script de construção）
  • 文档：（mudanças na documentação）
  • 风格：（格式化，缺少分号等；没有生产代码更改）
  • 重构：（formatação、ponto e vírgula faltando 等；sem mudança de código de produção）
  • 测试: (adicionar testes ausentes, refatorar testes; sem mudança de código de produção)
  • 家务活：（atualização de tarefas grunhidas 等；sem mudança de código de produção）

    • Seguindo a documentação conventionalcommits você pode encontrar mais sobre o assunto. Seguindo a documentação contrastcommits você pode encontrar mais sobre o assunto。



    原文:

    A descrição de um commit bem estruturado consegue prevenir e corrigir possíveis bugs, e pra você que optou por usar commits semânticos eu trouxe um resumão pra sair logo do papel e botar logo em prática!

    Trago aqui exemplos claros da diferença de um bom commit e um ruim.

    exemplo de commit ruim❌

    exemplo de commit bom✅

    feat(banana): Frutas.js

mudei a classe de cor da banana de 
amarelo para verde pois ela estava madura

    O exemplo de commit bom mostra o quão importante é ter um commit descrito de forma clara e objetiva. No futuro, quando precisar alterar o método "banana" você pode estar verificando que esta alteração e entendendo o que ocorreu naquele commit.

    REGRAS DE COMMIT

    • feat: (novo recurso para o usuário, não um novo recurso para script de construção)
    • fix: (correção de bug para o usuário, não uma correção para um script de construção)
    • docs: (mudanças na documentação)
    • style: (formatting, missing semi colons, etc; no production code change)
    • refactor: (formatação, ponto e vírgula faltando, etc; sem mudança de código de produção)
    • test: (adicionar testes ausentes, refatorar testes; sem mudança de código de produção)
    • chore: (atualização de tarefas grunhidas etc; sem mudança de código de produção)

    Seguindo a documentação conventionalcommits você pode encontrar mais sobre o assunto.
